proverb: అంగటివీథిలో అబ్బా అంటే, యెవరికి పుట్టినావురా కొడకా అన్నట్టు.
translation: When addressed [by his son] in the bazar as “Father,” he replied, “Son! unto whom wert thou born?”Want of natural affection. Selﬁsh indifference.

proverb: అంగట్లో అన్నీ వున్నవి, అల్లుని నోట్లో శని వున్నది.
translation: There is every thing in the hazar, but Saturn is in the mouth of the son-in-law.Some unfortunate circumstance prevented a son-in-law from availing himself of the good fare of his father-in-law.Evil fate.
Offering the molasses in the bazar to the idol in the temple.Naivédyam is the ordinary oblation.Willing to be liberal at the expense of others. ​
Molasses in the roof of the mouth, and poison in the heart.Honey in his mouth, words of milk; Gall in his heart, fraud in his deeds. (Latin)
Poison in the palate, sweetness on the tip of the tongue.Full of courtesy, full of craft.
An Agraharam which should not be touched or felt.An Agraharam is a Brahman village or quarter.Said of any thing that should scrupulously be avoided.

proverb: అంటూ సంటూ ఆసాదివానిది, రట్టూ రవ్వా గంగానమ్మది.
translation: All the offerings go to the priest, the noise to Ganganamma.Ganganamma is a certain village goddess.

proverb: అంటూ సంటూ లేని కోడలి దాని మేనమామ కొడుకు చిక్కుడు చెట్టుకిందికి పోయి పక్కలు యెగరవేశినాడట.
translation: The wife’s maternal uncle’s son, who had nothing to do with ceremonial uncleanness, went and sobbed under the bean tree.Chikkudu Chettu is the Lablab Cultratus, often grown over a shed in the yard of a Hindu’s house.Claiming relationship.

proverb: అంతమాత్రమా కొడకా చెవులు పట్టుకొని తడవేవు అన్నట్టు.
translation: Am I so well off, my son, that you should feel my ears?Said by an old woman to a thief. ​

Annoyance at the beginning is better than annoyance at the end.A prompt denial causes less annoyance than compliance with a request and disappointment in the end.Better a friendly denial than an unwilling compliance. (German)
His beauty has overﬂowed and his politeness has gone.Said ironically of an ugly ruffian.

proverb: అందములో పుట్టిన గంధపుచెక్క, ఆముదములో పుట్టిన మడ్డి.
translation: A piece of sandal wood born of beauty, and the dregs of castor oil.
Like wishing for fruit which cannot be reached.

proverb: అందరికి నేను లోకువ, నాకు నంబి రామాయ లోకువ.
translation: I am below all, but Nambi Ramaya is below me.

proverb: అందరు వకయెత్తు, అగస్త్యుడు వకయెత్తు, అతని కమమండలం వక యెత్తు.
translation: The whole people [of the earth], the sage Agastya, and his jar are all of equal weight.The legend is that when all the great men of the earth had, on one occasion, assembled at Kaihtsa, their weight was so great that the earth began to topple over. On this, Agsstya was sent to weigh down the other side which he accordingly did, but his presence being afterwards required at Kailasa, he transferred his virtue to his jar of holy waters, left it to balance the earth, and obeyed the summons. ​

proverb: అందానికి రెండు బొందలు, ఆటకు రెండు తాళాలు.
translation: Two holes in beauty; two cymbals in a dance.Said of an ugly and bad dancer.
There is no substance in that, strain the rice water.Equivalent to the English "there’s nothing in him" or "there’s nothing in it."
Like giving a blind man 21 looking glass.A blind man will not thank you for a looking glass.

proverb: అంఅటి యేరు వచ్చినది అత్తగారూ అంటే, కొలబుర్ర నాచేతిలో వున్నది కోడలా అన్నదట.
translation: “A river of porridge has come, mother-in-law !” she cried. “But I have the measure, daughter-in-law,” replied the dame.
Do people that live on porridge keep men to hold up their mustaches ?Said of a poor man trying to appear grand.A proud mind and a poor purse are ill met.

proverb: అకట వికటపు రాజుకు అవిబేకి ప్రధాని, చాదస్తపు పరివారము.
translation: A foolish minister, and a clumsy retinue, to a perverse king. ​

proverb: అక్కన్న మాదన్నగారు అందలమెక్కితే, సాటికి వరప్ప చెరువు కట్ట యెక్కినాడట.
translation: When Akkanna and Madanna mounted their state palankin, Sarappa got on the bund of the tank to make him self equal.Envying and mocking the wealthy.Puppies imitating their mistresses. (Latin)
The moat is heaven to the cat that falls into it.It cannot possibly escape, and dies.An inextricable difficulty.
The brother of Agastya.Agastya was a great sage, who was not known to have any brother.Applied to a new-comer, attempting to make himself out a great personage.
When it is cheap, it comes to the bazar.
When it is cheap, all will buy.
Like a grass-hopper jumping into the ﬁre (ﬂame).To try, they say, to extinguish it.Applied to rash acts. ​

proverb: అగ్నిహోత్రమునకు పోయిన సోమిదేవమ్మ ఆరు నెలల గర్బముతో వచ్చినది.
translation: Somidévamma Went for ﬁre (for the sacriﬁce) and came back six months in pregnancy.Somidévamma is an honoriﬁc title given to the wife of a sacriﬁcing priest; she is supposed to be very pious.
Like ghee poured on ﬁre.To cast oil in the ﬁre is not the way to quench it.

proverb: అచ్చం తిరుమణిధారి అయితే పుల్ల పట్టడంలోనే తెలుసును.
translation: You can tell Whether he is really a Vaishnava or not by the way in which he holds the pencil.Tirumani is the clay used for the sectarian mark worn on the forehead by Vaishnavas, and Pulla is a bit of grass with which the mark is made.Halt not before a cripple.
If the ground is lucky, a foot of it is enough.Luck is all.

In the one case Kandi, in the other Pesara.Kandi is the Cajanus Indicus; Pesara is the Phaseolus Mungo.Said by a Brahman quack referring to the recovery or death of his patient. In either case he is feasted. ​

In the one case, Medical fees; in the other, the gift of a Vaitarani cow.A cow is presented at funerals to a Brahman to enable the soul of the deceased to get across the burning river Vaitarani which is said to separate heaven from earth.Said by a Brahman priest and doctor.
A Hopper-woman requiring six people to assist her."Hopper" is a kind of cake ( Tamil அப்பம்).Said of a person giving himself airs.
Like a nut in the nippers.Adakattu are nippers used for breaking Areca nuts.To be between the hammer and the anvil. (french)
I cannot ask, sweep the whole [ into my dish ].Feigned modesty.I don’t want it, I don’t want it, but put it into my hood. (Spanish.)
He who has to answer is inferior to him who questions.It is one thing to put questions, another to answer them.
Vain hopes are a source of grief.Hope and expectation are a fool's income. (Danish) ​
Like moon-light in the jungle.Wasted on a spot where it is not appreciated.
What does a wild Jackal care for the threats of a Police Ofﬁcer ?What does the moon care if the dogs bark at her? (German.)

Like salt from the sea being mixed with Emblic Myrobalan grown in the [ distant] jungle.Usirikakaya is the Emblica Offleinalis.Used with reference to an advantage gained by an unlocked for coincidence.

What! a caret in the commencement ?Said by a man to an ignorant scribe who, in writing the customary invocation "Sri Ramarajya" at the heading of a letter, omitted a syllable.Blundering at the very beginning of any business.He who begins ill finishes worse. (Italian.)Well begun is half done.
A post ﬁxed in the mire [swinging to and fro].Said of a time server.To sit on two stools. (Latin) ​
Like the cat on the cross wall.It can jump down either side.Applied to an unprincipled, double-dealing person.Jack o' both sides.

proverb: అడ్డెడు తవ్వెడు బియ్యము యిచ్చి అమ్మా నీ ప్రసాదము అన్నట్టు.
translation: Like giving [a cook] two and a half measures of rice and saying, “Madam, this is your gift.”The cook gets four-ﬁfths of the rice for herself, and yet she must be coaxed to cook the food. The allusion is to a traveller endeavouring to get some person to cook for him.

[Like a widow saying] “if he were alive, he would at least call the barber.”This is a reproach to the husband as being a useless fellow only ﬁt to be employed as a messenger, and is also a joke, as the widow only, and not the wife, requires the services of a barber, to shave her head.
The great secret is laid bare to all.Tom Noddy’s secret.

proverb: అత్త కొట్టిన కుండ అడుగోటి కుండ, కోడలు కొట్టిన కుండ కొత్త కుండ.
translation: The pot broken by the mother-in-law was a cracked pot, the pot broken by the daughter-in-law was a new pot.
The rancour of a mother-in-law.Among Hindus the husband’s mother, when living, rules the house. and to her the wife is subject.The husband's mother is the wife's devil. (Germain). ​
Like the daughter-in-law lamenting the death of her mother in-law.Feigned sorrow.Crocodile tears.
There is no one to blame the mother-in-law.

proverb: అత్తతో చెప్పి అషు అనిపించలేడుగాని, లంభించడానకు బ్రహ్మకొడుకు.
translation: He was not able to get the consent of my mother-in-law [ to give me my wife, ] but he is a very son of Brahma in reviling.
Giving her child her mother-in-law’s name, she threw it into the ﬁre.Cutting off one's nose to spite one's face.
There is no goodness in a mother-in-law nor sweetness in Margosa trees.Vêmu is the Azadirachts lndica.She is well married who has neither mother-in-law nor sister-in-law (Spanish)

proverb: అత్తలేని కోడలు వుత్తమురాలు, కోడలులేని అత్త గుణవంతురాలు.
translation: Where there is no mother-in-law, the daughter-in-law is perfect; where there is no daughter-in-law, the mother-in-law is good tempered.As long I was a daughter-in-law I never had a good mother-in-law, and as long as I was a mother-in-law I never had a good daughter-in-law. (Spanish) ​

Deceitfulness is learnt from mother in-law, adultry is learnt from a husband.Jealous watching leads to unfaithfulness.At the gate where suspicion enters, love goes out.If the wife sins, the husband is not innocent. (Italian.)
The pleasure felt in a mother-in-law’s house is like a blow on the elbow.Happy is the wife who is married to a motherless son.
The mother-in-law was herself once a daughter-in-law.The mother-in-law forgets that she was a daughter-in-law. (Spanish.)
Like the fig blossoming.This tree (Ficus Glomerata) bears no apparent blossoms.That is as likely as to see a hog ﬂy.
You should not encourage vain hopes in a helpless dependant, nor give place to a powerful man.
There are smouldering ashes there, and dust here; let us go out into the Pandili.Pandili is a verandah thatched with leaves. ​
When he says, “ here you are,” it means six months.One “ Take it" is better than two " You shall have its." (French)

If this were a brooch, might we not be made rich ?Patakam ( Sans. Padaka) is a valuable ornament attached to the Kanthasara (necklace). The allusion is to one made of false gold, and set with false gems.Disappointment.The treasure turned out charcoal. (Latin)

proverb: అదేమి రెడ్డీ వంగి వంగి నడుస్తావంటే, యెప్పటికాలు అట్టెట్టే అన్నాడట.
translation: “What is it that makes you limp, Reddi?” asked one. “My old leg is the same as ever,” he replied.Habit.

Like a Pesara seed on a looking glass.Used with reference to a remark aimed at a particular person, but conveyed in such general terms that he is unable to take notice of it.He said devil, but meant you. (Dutch)
Like a bag of money in a looking glass.Visionary prospects.
Will hired horses leap over ditches ? ​

[ Going to] an entertainment on hire is [ to get] the dirt off a leathern ghi bottle.Pérantam is an invitation given to married women on the occasion of any domestic festival. Family priests sometimes hire married women to attend on such occasions, the condition being that all presents received 30 to the priest. The food set before these substitutes is often had.Come uncalled sit unserved.Go neither to a wedding nor a christening without invitation.

proverb: అధమునికి ఆలయ్యేటంతకంటే, బలవంతునికి బానిసె అయ్యేది మేలు.
translation: Better be the hand-maid of a great man than the wife of a low fellow.Better fare hard with good men than feast with bad.
An intercalary Ashadha.The month Ashadha corresponds with June-July.Said by a man when excusing himself for not observing the rules of the house in which he was staying.
Excessive cupidity leads to excessive poverty.Avarice bursts the bag. (French)
O mother-in-law, should you accuse me and bring a reproach on yourself in return ?Those who live in glass houses should not throw stones.
Clever at his meals, but stupid at his books.Well fed, but ill taught. (French)He has two stomachs to eat, and one to work. ​

proverb: అన్నము పెట్టితే అరిగిపోతుంది, చీరయిస్తే చిరిగిపోతుంది, వాతపెట్టితే కలకాలము వుంటుంది.
translation: If I give you rice, you will eat it up; if I give you a cloth, it will wear out; but if I brand you, it will last for ever.Said by a stingy person to a beggar.

proverb: అన్నము లేకపోతే వరి అన్నము బట్ట లేకపోతే పట్టుబట్ట.
translation: If you have no common food, [give me] the best; if you have no common cloth, [give me] a silk one.Said by an impertinent beggar when refused assistance by s stingy person.
A kind reception is better than a feast. Welcome 1': the best cheer.In hospitality it is the spirit that is the chief thing. (Greek)

Alas! my son, drink the water of all the cocoanuts.The father had refused to give his son a single cocoanut while alive, but offered him the whole after he had died of thirst.To lock the stable door when the steed is stolen.
I lack nothing, but I am a widow.
In the “ city of injustice,” husband and wife even are accused of adultery.
You should do good even to him who has done you evil."If thine enemy be hungry, give him bread to eat; and if he be thirsty, give him water to drink." Proverbs xxv. 21. ​

I presented you with the robe in return for the compliments you then paid me, but did I intend you to wear it for ever ?A liberal gift regretted and withdrawn.Give a thing and take again, And you shall ride in hell's wain.
He is nothing to me but my sister’s husband; the millstone is only valued for the grain it grinds.Allu is the plural of Aruga ( Paspalum Scrobiculatmn )
Will a debt be repaid when you are hungry ?Can you ever get a debt repaid when you are in want?
If I could raise a loan, [I should be able to live on] dal and rice.An excuse made by a host for treating his guest with poor fare.
Debt is misfortune.Debt is the worst poverty.
Your creditor will wish you well, your debtor will wish you ill.He that doth lend doth lose his friend.Money lent, an enemy made. (Portuguese) ​
Rice water and salt without debt are good.Without debt. without care. (Italian)He is rich enough who owe: nothing. (French)
A cupful of rice water without debt is enough.Out of debt, out of danger.Happy is he who owes nothing. (Greek)
No meaning can be given to erroneous expressions.Applied to discrepancies in a statement.
A bad almanack makes all the hours of the day unpropitious.Applied to unreasonable objections.There are 60 Gadiyas in a day ; a Gadiya therefore equals 24 minutes.
If the boy dies, that cloth is mine.Want of feeling.

If possible, [ he seizes ] the tuft; if not, the feet.Applied to a base fellow who tries to ruin others, and, when he fails, cringes at their feet.Anoint a villain and he will prick you, prick a villain and he will anoint you. (French) ​

proverb: అబ్బురాన బిడ్డ పుట్టెను, గడ్డపార తేరా చెవులు కుట్టుతాను.
translation: The long desired child has been born, fetch a crow-bar and I will bore its ears.Ridiculing unusual and extraordinary care being taken of any thing not worthy of it.
Practice [makes] art easy.Practice makes perfect.Custom makes all things easy.

proverb: అభ్యాసము లేని రెడ్డి అందలమెక్కితే అటూ యిటూ అయినదట.
translation: When the unpractised Reddi got into a palankin, it swung from side to side.
The mother-in-law put her ﬁnger in every dish prepared [by the daughter-in-law ].No work and all the credit.One man knocks in the nail, and another hangs his hat on it. (German)
Will the new-moon bring out the brinjals ?Vanga (called by Europeans "brinjal") is the Solanam Melongena.
The only goat remaining unsold.A wretched fate. ​

అమ్మ గృహప్రవేశము, అయ్య శ్మశానప్రవేశము.The bride enters her husband’s house, and the bridegroom enters the tomb.Regarding Grihapravésa ("house-entering").Applied to a great calamity happening when most unlooked for. Also to a luckless woman.After a dream of a wedding comes a corpse.
Is this miserable wretched woman to wear a veil ?
The mother neither gives food herself [to her child,] nor allows it to beg.

proverb: అమ్మదగ్గిర కింద పడుకొన్నా వకటే, అబ్బదగ్గిర నేల పడుకొన్నా వకటే.
translation: It is all one whether [the child] sleep on the ground with its mother, or on the ﬂoor with its father.Equally bad.Where ball is the best, naught must be the choice.Whatever way you take there is a league of bad road. (Spanish)

proverb: అమ్మ పెట్టేవి నాలుగూ అప్పుడే పెట్టితే చెయ్యనా.
translation: If you had beaten me as my mother used to do, would I not have done [the work]?Said by a daughter-in-law to her mother-in-law.Applied to a. person with whom mild measures are of no avail, with whomThere is no argument like that of the stick. (Spanish)
When he went to sell, his bargains were [as ruinous as] a jungle; when he went to buy [his purchases were as bad as] ﬁrebrands.Losing on both sides.Buy and sell and live by the loss.

proverb: అయితంపూడి వుద్యోగము అయితే గియితే, ఆరావుల పాడిచేస్తే గీస్తే, పెరుగూ పాలూ మీవారికి పోస్తావా మావారికి పోస్తావా.
translation: “Were I to be employed in Aithampﬁdi, and possess six milch cows, would you supply milk and curds to your relations or to mine ?"The story is, that the wife being asked this foolish question by her husband answered "to my people," on which she was beaten.

“If We succeed, she shall be his wife; if not, she shall be a recluse.”Said by the parents of a girl, who had set their hearts upon getting her married to a certain man.Either Caesar or nobody. (latin)

If the Aruga fails, we shall have Kandi.These are both sown together. The Âruga crop sometimes perishes.To have two strings to one's bow.Good riding at two anchors men have told; For if one break, the other may hold.

proverb: అయినవారందరు ఆ దోవను పోగా, జంగాన్ని పట్టుకొని జాము యేడ్చినాడు.
translation: When all his friends went away, he caught hold of a Jangam and cried for three hours.Taking an unconcerned person into your counsels.Jamu is a watch of three hours, the day from sunrise to sunset being divided into four Jamus.

proverb: అయ్యకు రెండో పెండ్లి అని సంతోషమేకాని, అమ్మకు సవితిపోరు అని యెరగడు.
translation: [The son] is glad that his father has taken a second wife, but he little knows what quarrels there will be between her and his mother.

proverb: అయ్యకు విద్యా లేదు, అమ్మకు గర్వమూ లేదు.
translation: When the husband has no learning, the wife has no pride.

proverb: అయ్య దాసండ్లకు పెట్టితే, అమ్మ జంగాలకు పెట్టినది.
translation: When the master fed the Dasaris (devotees of Vishnu), the mistress fed the Jangams (devotees of Siva ).Applied to a spirit of contradiction.

proverb: అయ్యనీటు అడిగితీరదు, అమ్మ దమ్మయపాలెంలో దంచుకొని తాగుతున్నది.
translation: The foppish husband gives himself great airs, while his wife is living by pounding rice in Dammayapalem.
If the priest does not come, will the new moon wait for him ?Time and tide wait for no man.
When his lordship brands the buffalo, will she get over it ?Said of a clumsy fellow, who makes a mess of every thing.I meant to cross myself and put out one of my eyes. (Portuguese.)

His attempt to make [an image of] Ganésa ended in [the moulding of] a monkey.Mud images of Ganésa are made and worshipped on the 4th day of the light fortnight in the month Bhadrapada (August-September ).A ridiculous failure.
The Brahman is like a small pot, and his wife is like a basket.Spoken of things which do not match.
The man [weighs] a seer and a quarter, the lingam [round his neck ] two and a half.
Like a man with butter in the palm of his hand, calling out for ghi.
Do you require a glass to look at a berry in the palm of your hand ?The berry spoken of is the fruit of the Zizyphns Jujuba.As clear as crystal.As plain as a pike-staff.
If he puts a little salt in the palm of your hand, you should think of it for six months.The duty of gratitude.
He offers heaven in the palm of his hand.Deceiving by false hopes.
Lamentations in the jungle.Of no avail.
Like a Tamil mourning.Among the Tamilians, women are sometimes hired to mourn over a deceased person.Applied to an unwilling performance of any duty.
When sixty years have passed, dotage.
After sixty years had passed, he cried Amma. (mother).Second childhood.Old men are twice children. (Latin)
Sixty years have passed; am I to do without learning ?An ironical speech.An old dog will learn no tricks.

proverb: అరవై వరహాలు యిచ్చినా అత్తవంటి బానిసె దొరకదు.
translation: Although you pay sixty Pagodas, you cannot obtain as good a handmaid as your mother-in-law.Said jokingly of a mother-in-law not allowed to have the management of the house, but kept in subjection to her daughter-in-law.
A barking dog never bites.Great barkers are nae biters. (Scotch)A barking cur does not bite. (Italian)

proverb: అరిటాకు వచ్చి ముంటిమీద పడ్డా అరిటాకుదే కష్టము, ముల్లు వచ్చి అరిటాకుమీద పడ్డా అరిటాకుదే కష్టము.
translation: The plantain leaf alike suffers, whether it falls on the thorn, or the thorn falls on it.
Like placing peeled plantain in one’s hand.Applied to any thing made perfectly easy.

proverb: అరుంధతీ కనబడదు, అధ్వాన్నమూ కనపడదు, అరవై వరహాల అప్పు మాత్రము కనపడుతున్నది.
translation: I can’t see the star Arundhati, botheration take it! I can see nothing but a debt of sixty pagodas.Said by a man who had got over head and ears in debt for his marriage, when the priest pointed out the propitious star.

proverb: అర్తి దుఃఖం ఆరునెలలు, కడుపు దుఖం కలకాలం.
translation: The grief of the neck [lasts] six months; the grief of the womb, for ever.The " grief of the neck " signiﬁes widowhood, when the marriage cord is broken. The “grief of the womb" signiﬁes the loss of a child.

proverb: అర్థమూ ప్రాణమూ ఆచార్యాధీనము, తాళమూ దేహమూ నా ఆధీనము.
translation: The money [in the box] and the soul [Within the body] are made over to the spiritual guide, but the key and the body are retained by me.Applied to giving a person only the form of‘ authority, but no real power.
The man unaccustomed to burnt-offerings burnt off his mustaches in the attempt.

proverb: అలివిగాని ఆలిని కట్టుకొని మురిగిచచ్చెరా ముండా కొడుకు.
translation: The miserable fellow is ruined by his wretched marriage.The folly of being headstrong.Merry in haste and repent at leisure.

proverb: అల్పునకు ఐశ్వర్యం వస్తే, అర్ధరాత్రివేళ గొడుగు తెమ్మన్నాడట.
translation: When fortune smiled on a mean person, he ordered an umbrella to be brought at midnight.An umbrella is a sign of rank and authority.Set a beggar on horseback, he’ll ride to the devil.There is no pride like that of a beggar grown rich. (French)

proverb: అల్లి అడిగినది, యిల్లి యిచ్చినది, మల్లి మాయము చేశినది.
translation: Alli asked for it, Illi gave it, Malli made away with it.
If the son-in-law gets in a huff, he will [only] take away the daughter.Said by a fat-her-in-law or mother-in-law.

proverb: అల్లుడికి నెయ్యీలేదు, అల్లుడితోటికూడా వచ్చినవారికి నూనే లేదు.
translation: There is neither ghi for the son-in-l aw, nor oil for his friends.Said of inability in ‘a person to do that which his duty imperatively requires of him.

proverb: అల్లుడికి వండిన అన్నము కొడుకుకు పెట్టి కొట్టుకొన్నదట.
translation: She grudgingly gave to her son the food prepared for her son-in—law.The affection of a mother-in-law for her son-in-law.
Mallu is a great man among the weavers.Great among low people.Among the common people Scoggin is a doctor.

proverb: అవలక్షణము గలవానికి అక్షతలు యిస్తే, అవతలికి పోయి నోట్లో వేసుకొన్నాడట.
translation: When sacred grain was given to a luckless fellow, he went away and ate it up (instead of placing it on his head).The term Avalakshana is applied to a man who bears unlucky marks on his person, or whose manners are inauspicious.

proverb: అవసరగానికి ఆకులో పెట్టు, నాకు నేలను పెట్టు.
translation: Give the man who is in a hurry his food on a leaf, but give me mine on the ﬂoor.The food of Hindus is ordinarily served on a large leaf or several small leaves sewn together.Applied to a person wishing to have something done for himself in great haste, before others are attended to.
Enmity with a wise man is better than friendship with a fool.
Like playing games with your grand-mother.Lit. "Throwing scarlet water over her,” a sport among young people at marriages, &c.Disrespectful and unseemly conduct towards elderly people.

proverb: అవ్వ వడికిన నూలు తాత మొలతాటికి సరి.
translation: The thread spun by the grand-mother is only suﬁicient for the waist cord of the grand-father.Waste of that gained by another’s industry.
I want grand-mother and I want food.Said by a child who did not wish to leave its grand-mother, but who had to do so to get its dinner.You cannot drink and whistle at the same time. (Danish)
If you throw a stone into ﬁlth, it will ﬂy into your face.If you will stir up the mire, you must bear the smell”. (Danish)Meddle with dirt, and some of it will stick to you. (Dutch.)

The water drawn with the Picota is the drink offering of dis regarded ancestors.Tarpana is a drink offering to the manes of deceased ancestors (Pitris.).An irreverent'fellow, when pressed by his friends to perform the ceremony, offered the water he was drawing for other purposes.An unwilling action, done to satisfy others.
She is not only the wife of a priest, but is also in an interesting condition.A double excuse. Applied to idle persons.Sick of the idles.A large sheep thinks its wool heavy.

proverb: అ అంటే అపశబ్దము, నారాయణా అంటే బూతుమాట.
translation: If I say “ A” (yes), it’s Wrong; if I say “Narayana,”. it’s obsceneFaults are thick where love is thin. (Welsh.)
The appetite is as great as the sky, but the throat is as small as the eye of a needle.Applied to great ambition, but small abilities. ​

proverb: ఆకలి కాని భోజనము, ఆలి మీద లంజరికము.
translation: Eating when the appetite is satisﬁed; unfaithfulness to a wifeBoth bad.
The hungry Karanam looked into his old accounts.A Karanaam is a village accountant.

proverb: ఆకలి యెత్తుతుంది అత్తగారా అంటే, రోకలి మింగవే కోడలా అన్నదట.
translation: When the daughter-in-law said she was hungry, her mother-in-law told her to swallow the pestle.Rokali is a wooden pestle, ﬁve or six feet long, used for pounding rice.

proverb: ఆకలి రుచి యెరగదు, నిద్ర సుఖమెరగదు, వలపు శిగ్గు యెరగదు.
translation: Hunger knows not taste, sleep knows not comfort, lust knows not shame.Hunger is the best sauce.
A sky calendar.A fanciful tale.
A trader in the air.A swindler. An impostor. ​
It reaches not the leaf, it suits not the nut.Quite inadequate.
When the leaf went and fell into the well, seven men went in search and fell in after it.The gain is not worth the candle.(French)

proverb: ఆగడపలు వేశినవి అత్తగారా అంటే, కొలబుర్ర నాచేతిలో వున్నది కోడలా అన్నదట.
translation: “See! The signs of rain appear!” cried the daughter-in-law. “What of that?” said the mother-in-law “I have the measure.”

proverb: ఆగభోగాలు అక్క కుడిస్తే, అంబటి పరకలు బావ కుడిచినాడు.
translation: While my sister enjoyed every luxury, my brother-in-law had nothing to eat but the scum of the porridge.Said of a man who is poorly treated in his father-in-law’s house while his wife fares well.
Like taking an oath in a ﬁt of passion.

proverb: ఆచారం అదిరిపడ్దదీ, ధూపార్తి తుళ్లిపడ్డది, కొత్త కుండ తేరా నెత్తాళ్లు వండుకొందాము.
translation: Let our sanctity be scandalized, let the censer be shocked bring a new pot and let us cook sprats. ​

proverb: ఆచారం ఆచారం అన్నంభొట్లూ అంటే, పెద్ద చెరువు కుక్క ముట్టుకొన్నది అన్నట్టు
translation: Seeing a hypocrite] they cried out, “O Annambhotlu! purity ! purity!” He answered “Alas! a dog has touched the big tank.”

proverb: ఆచారానకు అంతమూ లేదు, అనాచారానకు ఆదీ లేదు.
translation: There is no limit to purity, nor any beginning to impurity.Man is sinful from his birth, and never attains to perfect purity.
If you think of your spiritual guide and put your hand in the ﬁre, will it not be burnt ?

proverb: ఆటా తీరె, పాటా తీరె, కోటలో రామరాజ్యము.
translation: The dancing is over, the song is ﬁnished, and every one is as free as in the kingdom of Rama.(Sec Nos. 278, 1644, 1987.)
Money left in the hands of a woman wont last ; a child left in the hands of a man wont live.
A woman’s word, a bundle of water.A woman's mind and winter wind change oft.

proverb: ఆడది బొంకితే గోడ పెట్టినట్టు, మొగవాడు బొంకితే తడిక కట్టినట్టు.
translation: If a woman lies, it’s like building a wall ; if a man lies, it’s like putting up a mat ( easily seen through. )
Not being able to dance, she abused the drum.A bad workman complains of his tools.An ill shearer never got a hook. (scotch)
Woman’s rule, a Tambali’s government.A Tambali is a Saiva priest of low origin, who wears the sacred thread, and endeavours to observe Brahminical customs.
The sacred water in which the man was going to bathe came to meet him.A thing sought for to come to hand without trouble.
Having promised, you should not fail; having spoken, you should not lie (retract).
Is he not the son of that father?Like father, like son.Such a father, such a son. (Portuguese)He is his father’s son.(Latin)
A hasty man is not wise.He that is hasty of spirit exalteth folly. Proverbs xiv. 29.A hasty man never wants woe.
That proﬁt and this loss are equal.

proverb: ఆత్మస్తుతీ, పరనిందా, అర్థాపేక్షా, లేని మహానుభావుడికి నాకూ డబ్బు, శుద్ధశుంఠకు వాడికీ డబ్బా
translation: Am I, an upright man, not a boaster, a fault ﬁnder, nor covetous, to have one pice, and is this great fool to have a pice also ?In this speech a man committed the very three faults from which he boasted he was free.

proverb: అత్మా రావే నూతపడుదాము అంటే, ఆదివారము రోజున అందరమూ పడుదాము అన్నదట.
translation: When he thought of putting an end to himself, his soul said within him “Let us wait until Sunday and all drown together.”The word “All" refers to the elements of which man is composed.Sunday is a propitious day.Judge well before you act.Consideration is the parent of wisdom.
Without [the hope of] gain, a Setti will not venture into the ﬂoodThe Settis (merchants) are very greedy and very cowardly.
Vows in times of trouble, carping [ at the gods ] in times of prosperity.The devil was sick, the devil a monk would be;The devil grew well, the devil a monk was he.(Latin)

That pulse cannot be cooked with this water.The pulse alluded to is the Csjanus Indicns commonly called Dal ( Hind.) It requires to be boiled in good sweet water to make it soft and ﬁt for eating.A phrase similar to “That game won't pay,” “That cock won’t ﬁght."

proverb: ఆపస్తంబులా ఆశ్వలాయనులా అంటే, ఆపస్తంబులమూ కాము, ఆశ్వలాయనులమూ కాము, అప్పారావుగారి హర్కారాలము అన్నారట.
translation: When asked whether they belonged to the Apastamba or Asvalayana sect, they replied “We are neither the one nor the other, we are messengers of Appa Rao.”Utter ignorance of religion.
They are seeds out of the same bowl.Coming from the same bad stock.
If the leagues are long, are the hearts far distant ?An Amada (4 Kos) is a distance varying from 8 to 10 miles.Distance cannot alter true friendship.
Only waste of castor oil, but not [saving] the child’s life.Useless endeavours.
He is like a ﬂock of cotton dipped in castor oil.To look like a drowned mouse.5
If the vital parts escape, there is no comfort like that which follows a wound.An invalid receives every attention from his friends.
He must die ﬁrst, orI must die before him.An ambiguous phrase used by a bad wife.
Will a man get by begging, what was wanting in the feast ?

proverb: ఆరాటపు కదురు యే రాటాన బెట్టినా బర బర.
translation: The restless spindle, to whatever spinning wheel it may be attached, rattles.

proverb: ఆరుగ కోశిన ముహూర్తాననే కంది కూడా కొయ్యండి.
translation: Reap the Aruga and Kandi together in the same propitious hour.Applied to hasty actions.

proverb: ఆరు నెలలనుంచి వాయించిన మద్దెల వోటిదా గట్టిదా అని అడిగినట్టు.
translation: Like asking if the drum is cracked or strong, after using it for six months.
Like a tree loaded with cakes falling below an Are tree.Are is the Bauhinia Racemosa; it bears fruit like cranberries.Great luck.
Would you comfort me, or remove my grief, or if necessary would you plunge [ into the water to save me ] ?Asking proofs of professed friendship.A friend in need is a friend indeed.
There are comforters but no real helpers.
Is feeding one’s wife a beneﬁt to the village ?

proverb: ఆలిని వల్లని వానికి యీలకూరలో వుప్పు చాలదు.
translation: For the husband who does not love his wife, there is not enough salt in the Ilakura.This herb (Salsola Indica) is occasionally used as a vegetable, and is naturally very salt.Where there is no love, all are faults.If you want a pretence to whip a dog, it is enough to say he ate up the frying pan.

proverb: ఆలి వంకవారు ఆత్మబంధువులు, తల్లి వంకవారు తగినవారు, తండ్రి వంకవారు దాయాదులు.
translation: Your wife's people are your own relations; your mother’s people are distant relations; your father’s people are enemies (because they are co-heirs)
She is not his mate but his fate.

proverb: ఆలు వల్లక అరవై యేండ్లు, మొగుడు వల్లక ముప్ఫయి యేండ్లు, బాలప్రాయము పది యేండ్లు.
translation: For ten years they were children, for thirty years the husband disliked the wife, and for sixty years the wife disliked the husband.Applied to an unproﬁtable and unhappy life.

proverb: ఆలూ మొగునిసందు పోట్లాట అద్దముమీద పెసరగీంజ వేశినంత సేపు.
translation: A quarrel between man and wife only lasts as long as a Pesara seed stays on a looking glass.

proverb: ఆలూ లేదు, చూలూ లేదు, కొడుకు పేరు సోమలింగం.
translation: There is neither wife nor conception, but the son’s name is to be Somalingam.We have no son and yet are giving him a name. (Spaniah.)Count not your chicken: before they are hatched.

proverb: ఆవగింజకు సందువుంటే, అరవై గారెలు అప్పుడే తిననా.
translation: Had there been room for a mustard seed, would I not have eaten sixty more cakes ?A glutton suffering from indigestion after a feast was recommended to eat some mustard, when he replied as above.
The woman who fed on mustard grew strong.Fattening on poor fare.
Yawning has elder and younger brothers, sneezing has not even a younger brother.Yawning is sympathetic, sneezing is not.A good gaper make two gapers. (French)
Making mustard seed into a ball.An impossible combination.

proverb: ఆవు చేనిలో మేస్తే, దూడ గట్టున మేసునా.
translation: If the cow grazes in the ﬁeld, will the calf graze on the bank ?Intimate friends, or near relatives, will follow in the same path.

proverb: ఆవు పాడి యెన్నాళ్లు ఐశ్వర్యం యెన్నాళ్లు, బర్రె పాడి యెన్నాళ్లు భాగ్యం యెన్నాళ్లు
translation: How long will you have the cow's milk, and how long will you have your fortune’! How long will your buii'aloe’s milk last, and how long will your riches endure ?Said to a man proud of his good fortune.Riches have wings.
Arjuna was the man who brought back the cows [of Virata].Used when speaking of a man to whom the credit of any successful undertaking is really due.

proverb: ఆవులూ ఆవులూ పోట్లాడితే, లేగల కాళ్లు విరుగుతవి.
translation: When the cows ﬁght, the sucking calves’ legs are broken.When the great fall out, the poor suffer.
There is no limit to desire.Much would have more.No one is content with his lot. (Portuguese)The more one has the more one wants. (spanish)

proverb: ఆశపడి ఆరు లంఖణాలుచేస్తే, ఆవేళా జొన్న మెతుకేనా.
translation: After fasting for six days, with the desire [of being fed with rice (i. e. dainty food given to an invalid)], am I only to have millet ?Failure of a foolish scheme.
Desire incites, the fear of shame prevents.Shame may restrain what the law does not prohibit.
There is no appetite for food unhoped for.
Avarice knows not shame; sleep knows not comfort.When sleep overpowers, comfort is not thought of.

proverb: ఆశా పాతక బాపడు గోచిపాతలో తూమెడు కట్టుకొన్నాడట.
translation: The avaricious Brahman tied up a tûmu [of grain] in his clout.

proverb: ఆసోది మాటలకు అర్థమూ లేదు, గుడ్డి కంటికి చూపూ లేదు.
translation: There is no sense in words of condolence nor sight in a blind eye.
In eating and in business you should not be modest.

A rag which had held assafoetida.Said of a man who is respected on account of his connection with the family of some famous person, no longer living ; or on account of his own greatness, now passed away.
Every house has an earthen ﬁre-place.Every man has his faults.

proverb: ఇంటింటికీ వక మట్టిపొయ్యి అయితే, మాయింట మరి వకటి.
translation: Every house has an earthen ﬁre-place, my house is still worse off.Acknowledging one’s failings.

The temple is stronger than the house.Said by a ruined man who had been obliged to take refuge in a temple, but pretended that he had gone there for the safety of his property.Make a virtue of necessity.
The goddess of misfortune at home, the goddess of fortune abroad.Bad to his own, but good to others.
When he kissed the lamp belonging to his own house, all his mustaches were burnt off.Undue familiarity. Extravagant demonstration.'A man may love his wool, and no ride on the riggin o’t. (Scotch)

proverb: ఇంటిదేవర యీగి చస్తే, పొలందేవర గంపజాతర అడిగినదట.
translation: When the household deity was dying of hunger, the goddess of the ﬁeld demanded a basket procession.After an abundant harvest, an earthen image of the goddess of the ﬁeld is placed in a basket, and carried in procession, with musical instruments and much rejoicing.To be asked to give to others when badly of!‘ one’s self.
Even Siva himself cannot ﬁnd out a thief amongst the household.

proverb: ఇంటినిండా కోళ్లు వున్నవిగని, కూశేటందుకు కోడి లేదు.
translation: The house is full of fowls, but there's not a cock to crow.Said of useless people.
His house name is Kasturi (musk), his house smells of bats.The "house name" is the gentilitious or family name; it precedes the proper name.A ﬁne name, but a paltry fellow.
Having thrown a stone on the top of the house, he stoops to catch it on his back.6

proverb: ఇంటివాడు గొడ్డుగేదె అంటే, పొరుగువాడు పాడిగేదె అన్నట్టు.
translation: When the owner said his she-buffalo was barren, the neighbour said it was milch.Applied to a spirit of contradiction.
Like waking the master, and giving the thief a stick.To hold with the hare, and hunt with the hounds.

proverb: ఇంటివారు వేలు చూపితే, బయిటివారు కాలు చూపుతారు.
translation: If the members of your family point their ﬁngers at you, the outsiders will point their legs.
He brought the house forward by going behind it.Said jokingly of a man who professed to have improved the family prospects.
His own property is like Ippapindi, his neighbour’s like ﬁne molasses.Ippapindi is the refuse of the nuts of the Ippa tree (Bassia Latifolia) and is remarkably bitter.Applied to a niggard.
What! milk pudding in the house and milk also in the ﬁeld ?Greediness.

proverb: ఇంట్లో పెండ్లి అయితే, వూళ్లో కుక్కలకు అడావుడీ.
translation: A marriage in the house is a ﬁne thing for the village dogs.Men crowd to enjoy that for which they don’t pay.
At home he is a spider, abroad he is a tiger.Demure at home, a ruffian abroad.He looks as if butler would not melt in his mouth.
In the house a buzzing of ﬂies, outside the hum of palankin bearers.Outward show, but poverty at home.

proverb: ఇంతమంది దొరలు చావకపోతే, నేను మాత్రము చస్తానా, నాకూ అక్కరలేదు అన్నాడట.
translation: If so many great people don’t die, shall I alone die’! I don't want it.Said by a man when unwillingly refusing to eat something because Velamas did the same.Never be ashamed to eat your meal.He that is ashamed lo eat is ashamed to live. (French.)

proverb: ఇక్కడ అక్కడ వుంటే యీడేరి పోతావు, నాయింటికి రావే నవిశిపోతువుగాని అన్నాడట.
translation: If you stay here and there you will thrive, come to my house and pine away.Said jokingly by a poor man to a young girl.
He who gives is a donor, he who does not give is a clout.The impertinent speech of a beggar.
The over liberal man is ruined ; when a man dies, his debts go with him.
She gave it, took it back again, and looked on her husband’s back for it.

proverb: ఇచ్చి వచ్చే నిష్ఠురముకన్నా, యివ్వక వచ్చే నిష్ఠురము నయము.
translation: The offence given by not lending is to be preferred to the annoyance caused after lending.By anger on account of some damage done to the article lent.

proverb: ఇచ్చుడుకాడూ గాడు, చచ్చుడుకాడూ గాడు, పూట పూటకూ కూటికి వచ్చుడుగాడు.
translation: He doesn’t pay, hang him, but he comes for every meal.Said by a cateress, of a customer who was more regular in his attendance than in his payments.
Even a dying man will rise up when he sees a liberal person.With the hope of receiving something from him.

proverb: ఇచ్చేవానికి పత్రమూ వద్దు, చచ్చేవానికి మందూ వద్దు.
translation: You need not take a bond from a man who is willing to pay, nor administer medicine to one who has made up his mind to die.

For sweet vegetable [ take your] “plain signiﬁcations,” and for acid [take your] “deep meanings.”The reply of the wife of s logician (who would pay no attention to household matters and had given her no means of providing food) when he grumbled at the bad fare.
Is this done wantonly or with an object ?

proverb: ఇదుగో పులి అంటే, అదుగో తోక అన్నట్టు.
translation: When one said “Here’s a tiger!” the other said “And there’s his tail !”Rivaling each other in exaggeration. Capping a long-bow man.

proverb: ఇనప గుగ్గిళ్లు గాని, మినప గుగ్గిళ్లు కావు.
translation: Grains of iron, not of black gram.The pulse spoken of is the Phaseolus Roxhurghii.Said of a puzzling passage or a jawbreaking verse.
What is there for ﬂies in the place where iron is melted ?A place where nothing is to be got.

proverb: ఇనుము, తీట, పేము, పట్టిన చెయ్యి వూరకుండదు.
translation: The hand which has a bit of iron, the itch, or a bit of cane in it, never remains quiet.
The ﬁre suffers the blows of the sledge-hammer for the sake of the hot iron.Being drawn into difficulties by one’s associates.

proverb: ఇనుము విరిగితే అతక వచ్చుగాని, మనసు విరిగితే అతక కూడదు.
translation: If iron be broken it may be united, but if friendship be broken it cannot be healed.Broken friendship may be soldered, but never made sound. (spanish.)
After living so long he died at the back of his house.A miserable end.
Must we search for the smell of the Ippa ﬂower ?The ﬂowers of the Ippa tree  have a very strong smell.

proverb: ఇరుపోటీల యిల్లు చెడును, వాతనొప్పుల వొళ్లు చెడును
translation: By rivalry a house is ruined, by rheumatic pains the body is worn out.
Is smearing the house always the sign of a feast ?
If the mistress of the house be blind, all the pots will be broken.Without supervision things will go to ruin.When the gude wife's awa', the keys are tint. (Scotch)

proverb: ఇల్లు కట్టి చూడు, పెండ్లి చేసి చూడు.
translation: Try building a house, try making a marriage.An expense greater than contemplated.Building and the marrying of children are great wasters.

proverb: ఇల్లు కాలినది జంగమయ్యా అంటే, నా జోలే కప్పరా నావద్దనే వున్నవి అన్నాడట.
translation: When the Jangam was told that the house had caught ﬁre, he replied “I have my bag and bowl with me”Selﬁsh indifference.
Like pulling the rafters out of a burning house.A clumsy expedient.
Will he who cannot rule his own house, rule in the council ?He who has no voice in the valley, will have none in the council. (spanish)
He got into the house and counted the rafters.Preparatory to laying a false claim to it, and adducing his intimate knowledge as a proof that the building was his own.Swindling.

proverb: ఇల్లు తిరిగి రమ్మంటే, యిలారం తిరిగి వచ్చినట్టు.
translation: When he was asked to walk round the house he walked round the shed.

proverb: ఇల్లు మింగే అత్తగారికి యుగము మింగే కోడలు.
translation: A daughter-in-law who swallows the age (Yuga) to a mother-in-law who swallows the house.
The house is small, and the wife like a monkey.A double difficulty.

proverb: ఇల్లు యేడ్చే అమావాస్య, యిరుగూ పొరుగూ యేడ్చే తద్దినం, వూరుయేడ్చే పెండ్లి లేదు.
translation: There is no new moon which the household regrets; nor annual ceremony, the neighbours; nor marriage, the whole village.At the new moon the Hindus abstain from eating rice in the evening, but take care to make up for it with other good things ; at the Taddina (ceremony on the anniversary of a deceased relative’s death) the neighbours are feasted ; and to a marriage the whole village is invited.
He pulled his house to pieces and built a Pandili [ with the materials ].Said of an arrant fool.

proverb: ఇల్లు వెళ్లగొట్టగా విడుపుల శృంగారము, మొగుడు వెళ్లగొట్టగా మొత్తల శృంగారము.
translation: Putting on ﬁne clothes when turned out of the house; adorning the threshold when driven out by the husband.Trying to please when too late.

proverb: ఇల్లు వెళ్లిపోరా నంబీ అంటే, నా మాన్యము యెక్కడ అని అడిగినాడట.
translation: When a Nambi was told to get out of the house, he asked for his globe-land.Nambi is a priest in a. Vishnu temple.Effrontery.
Getting on the roof [of a thatched house] and whirling a ﬁrebrand.
When the whole house was knocked down, the tiles did not fill a basket.Appearances are deceitful.

proverb: ఇల్లే తీర్థం, వాకిలే వారణాశి, కడుపే కైలాసం.
translation: My house is my holy place; my threshold is Benares; my belly is my heaven.Said by an irreligious man.“Whose God is their belly." Philippians iii. 18.

proverb: ఇవతల చెర, అవతల సొర, నడమ రామరాజ్యము.
translation: On this side is capture, on that side is sorrow, in the middle is the kingdom of Rama.7
One obstinate man who wouldn’t give and another who wouldn’t take a refusal.
Like taking possession of the whole house, when asked to come in for a while.Give a rogue an inch and he will take an ell. (Dutch)

Cheating with sand, cheating with cowdung.Two travellers met: one had a bundle of sand, the other a bundle of cowdung; one said his bundle contained raw rice, the other that his contained boiled rice. They agreed to exchange, and immediately ran off in different directions, each to ﬁnd himself outwitted by the other. (See story 54, in Telugu Selections).One trick is met by another. (Spanish)
I gave cakes and took cakes.Vayanam is a gift of cakes, &c., to a Brahman.Taking as much as you give.To one who has a pie in the oven you may give a bit of your cake. (French)

proverb: ఇస్తే చెడేది లేదు, చస్తే వచ్చేది లేదు.
translation: If a man gives, he will not be ruined; when a man dies, he carries nothing with him.Give and spend, and God will send.The charitable give out at the door, and God puts in at the window.“When he dieth he shall carry nothing away." Psalm xlix. l7.
.If you give, [I shall consider there is] a marriage [in your house] and if you don’t give, a funeral.The impertinent speech of a beggar.
Are these eyelids distant from this eye ?Applied to any thing which can easily be tested by actual observation.
No food for a ﬂy not offering for a snake.Said of a miscr’s house.Ye’ll brok your neck as soon as your fast i' his house. (Scotch)

proverb: ఈ చేత చేసి ఆ చేత అనుభవించినట్టు.
translation: Doing with this hand, and receiving the reward with that.Said of the certain result of either a good or bad deed.As you sow you shall reap.As you make your bed, so you must lie on it.

When a man has to swim it matters not what the depth is.When a man determines to face his diffiiculties nothing daunts him.Over shoes, over boots.It is all the same whether a man has both legs in the stocks or one. (German)
If you give, [I shall consider there is] a marriage [in your house] and if you don’t give, a funeral.The impertinent speech of a beggar.
.Are these eyelids distant from this eye ?Applied to any thing which can easily be tested by actual observation.
No food for a ﬂy not offering for a snake.Said of a miscr’s house.Ye’ll brok your neck as soon as your fast i' his house. (Scotch)

proverb: ఈ చేత చేసి ఆ చేత అనుభవించినట్టు.
translation: Doing with this hand, and receiving the reward with that.Said of the certain result of either a good or bad deed.As you sow you shall reap.As you make your bed, so you must lie on it.

When a man has to swim it matters not what the depth is.When a man determines to face his diffiiculties nothing daunts him.Over shoes, over boots.It is all the same whether a man has both legs in the stocks or one. (German) ​

proverb: ఈతకు మించిన లోతూ, గోచికి మించిన దారిద్ర్యమూ లేదు.
translation: There is no greater depth to the man who is obliged to swim, nor poverty to him who is reduced to a clout.Reduced to the last extremity.

A man who gives a date stone and takes a palmyra nut.Ita is the wild date (Phoenix Sylvestris ). Tadu is the Palmyra (Borassus FlabelliformisOffering a gift of slight value for the purpose of gaining a rich present.Throw in a spirit to catch a salmon.

proverb: ఈత చెట్టు కింద పాలు తాగినా, కల్లే అంటారు.
translation: If you even drink milk under a date tree they will say it’s toddy.People are judged by the company they keep.Tell me the company you keep, and I'll tell you what you are.From a river spring clear water flows. (Latin)
He is at the age when he can tread on and break a date thorn.Said of an unruly scamp.
When he went to swim, there was not enough water to drink.Disappointed hopes.
He watched [the ﬁeld] until the harvest, and then let it go to the jaokals.

proverb: ఈ వూరికి ఆ వూరెంతో, ఆ వూరికి యీ వూరంతే.
translation: The distance between that village and this village is the same as that between this village and that village.Six of one and half a dozen of the other.

proverb: ఈ వూళ్లో పెద్దలు యెవరంటే తాళ్లు, దాతలు యెవరంటే చాకళ్లు.
translation: When asked who were the great (men) in the village, he said “ The Pa1myras;” when asked who were the givers (of presents), he replied “The washermen.”A joke. The Palmyras are the tallest trees, and the washermen give people back their clothes.
A rap with ringed ﬁngers will cause no pain.A man who wears rings is generally well oil‘, and therefore pays hand some damages for injuries inﬂicted.
If [the people] stay, it is a village; if [the people] go, it is a ruin.
If you leave it alone, it will bear fruit; if you pull it up, it will wither away.
He watched [the ﬁeld] until the harvest, and then let it go to the jaokals.

proverb: ఈ వూరికి ఆ వూరెంతో, ఆ వూరికి యీ వూరంతే.
translation: The distance between that village and this village is the same as that between this village and that village.Six of one and half a dozen of the other.

proverb: ఈ వూళ్లో పెద్దలు యెవరంటే తాళ్లు, దాతలు యెవరంటే చాకళ్లు.
translation: When asked who were the great (men) in the village, he said “ The Pa1myras;” when asked who were the givers (of presents), he replied “The washermen.”A joke. The Palmyras are the tallest trees, and the washermen give people back their clothes.
A rap with ringed ﬁngers will cause no pain.A man who wears rings is generally well oil‘, and therefore pays hand some damages for injuries inﬂicted.
If [the people] stay, it is a village; if [the people] go, it is a ruin.
If you leave it alone, it will bear fruit; if you pull it up, it will wither away.

proverb: ఉండి చూడు వూరు అందం, నానాటికి చూడు నా అందం.
translation: Wait and see the beauty of the village, but examine my beauty day by day.Said by an ugly man in a dirty village.
What remains is solid, what has gone is empty husk.

[To perform the ceremony of] Dadi Biyyam to the priest’s wife when there are many other things to be done.Dadi Biyyam is a ceremony performed before the consummation of marriage.Going out of your way to do something for others, and neglecting much that you have to do of your own.

proverb: ఉండ్రాళ్లు పిండివంటా కాదు, వూద ధాన్యమూ కాదు.
translation: Ganésa’s rice-balls are not cakes, Uda is not grain.Uda is the Panicum Flavidum.

Will the lady who cannot get up into the sling get up into heaven ?Utti is a net work sling hung up in Hindu houses at a height of seven or eight feet, to keep earthen pots containing food in.He that can’t ride a gentle horse, must not attempt to back a mad call.

proverb: ఉడకకే వుడకకే వో వుల్లిగడ్ద, నీవెంత వుడికినా నీ కంపుపోదు.
translation: Don’t boil, don’t boil, 0 garlick! However much you boil, your smell will not go.Said to an envious person, who is said to burn inwardly.

proverb: ఉడకవే కూడా వుగాది దాకా అంటే, నాకేమి పనిలేదు యేరువాకదాకా అన్నాడట.
translation: [The host said] “Boil, boil away, O food, until new year’s day!” [the guest added] “My business will not take me away until the ploughing-season.”During the Pongal feast in January a stingy man tried by a broad hint to avoid giving a visitor food, but the latter was not to be done out of his dinner. The Telugu New year’s day falls in April, and cultivation commences in July.Eat and welcome; fast and heartily welcome.Let the guest go before the storm bursts. (German)

A squirrel performs a squirrel’s service.The squirrels are said to have assisted Rdma in building the bridge at Ramésvaram by dipping themselves into the Water and then rolling in and, which they brought for the mortar.Willingly doing what one can.
What has the squirrel to do with the management of the village ?Presumptuous interference. Impertinent meddling.
The Guana has two tongues.Said of a man that breaks his word." A sinner that hath a double tongue." Ecclesiasticus vi. l.
Going into a village with a guana in one’s arms.Eccentric conduct.
Never mind if I lose the guana, if I get my hand out it’s enough.Said by a man whose hand had been caught by a guana in a hole.Catching a Tartar.
An ear with a palmyra leaf in it is better than a bare ear.Half a loaf is better than no bread.
Like measuring the air.Idle dreams.
A wretch of a mother-in-law is better than a strange slut.A bad bush is better than the open field.Better a lean jade than an empty halter.

The boastings of Uttara Kumﬁra.Uttara, the son of king Virata, volunteered to attack the Kurus, but his courage failed him and he turned tail at the sight of the enemy (see Virataparva of the MnhAbhArata)Bragging. Bombast.

Take up your basket [of seed] when you see [the sun in] Uttara.Uttara is one of the twenty-seven lunar mansions. When the sun is in Uttara there is invariably rain, and seed is then sown.Make hay while the sun shines.Know your opportunity. (Latin.)

proverb: ఉత్తర వురిమి కురిశినా, పాము తరిమి కరిచినా తిరగదు.
translation: If there be rain with thunder when the sun is in Uttara, there will be no drought; if a snake pursues and bites you, the bite will be fatal.
Employment makes the man; he is a luckless fellow if he lose it.

proverb: ఉద్యోగం పురుషలక్షణం అన్నాడు, గొడ్డలితేరా నిట్రాడు నరుకుదాము.
translation: They say “endeavour makes the man,” so bring an axe, and let us out down the centre post.
A man starts with anger when the truth is told of him.It is truth that makes a man angry. (Italian)
If a man speaks the truth he will ﬁnd the village too hot for him.Truth produces hatred. (Latin)

proverb: ఉన్నవాడు వూరికి పెద్ద, చచ్చినవాడు కాఅటికి పెద్ద.
translation: He that is alive is the head man of the village, and he that is dead is the head man of the burial ground.A taunt used to one who tries to make out that he is very highly connected.
Immediately after doing good, I received an ill return.Base ingrntitude.

O teacher, uktam! uktam !Said by a school-boy when he saw a board about to fall on his master's head. He had been taught to render the Sanscrit word Uktam by Palaka baddadi (“it was said”). These words also signify “ the board has fallen," which the boy took to be the correct meaning of the Sanscrit word.

proverb: ఉపాధ్యాయులు యేమి చేస్తున్నాడంటే, అబద్ధాలు వ్రాసి దిద్దుకొంటున్నాడు అన్నాడట.
translation: When asked what the school master was doing, one replied “he is correcting the mistakes he has made.”A bad workman.
A witless fellow should not be allowed to remain in the village.
The man that has eaten salt drinks water.

proverb: ఉప్పువాడూ చెడె, పప్పువాడూ చెడె, తమలపాకులవాడు తమాం చెడె.
translation: The salt seller is ruined, the pulse vendor is ruined, and the betel seller is entirely ruined.

proverb: ఉభయపవిత్రాలూ తిని వుద్ధరిణెడు నీళ్లూ తాగి వూహూ అంటావా వుత్తమాశ్వమా.
translation: Dost thou neigh after eating two grass rings and a tea spoonful of water, O excellent steed ?Said by a poor Brahman priet to his miserable pony which he had only fed with two rings (made of sacred grass and worn on his ring ﬁngers), and had given him water out of the small spoon used in sipping water in ceremonies.Giving little pay and expecting much work to be done.
Ruined in both ways like a well-digger who turns Sanyasi.He is neither employed in his old trade nor supported in his new character.
The sheep which was the joint property of two persons, was deserted and died.The ass of many owners is eaten by wolves. (spanish.)Between two stools fall to the bottom.
Putting a child in a cradle and looking for it all over the village.He looks for his ass and sits on its back. (French.)

proverb: ఉరుకు వురుకుమనే నా సవతేకాని, తోడు వురికే నా సవతి యొకతెయున్నూ లేదు.
translation: All these wretched women say to me “run, run” [on to the pile], but not one comes with me.Said by a widow when pressed to become Sati.
Garlick is as good as ten mothers.

If there be a Balija man as small as a clove of garlick, he will ruin the whole village.Balija is a caste among Telugu and Canarese Sudras.One scabbed sheep will mar a whole ﬂock.One ill weed mars a whole pot of pottage.
Unless you had touched the garlick [your ﬁngers] would not have smelt.If you had not meddled with a bad business you would not have got a bad name.He who touches pitch defiles himself. (Italian)
When there are onions Malli is a ﬁne cook.Onions make up for bad cooking.
If you preserve your life, you can at least live by selling salt.Used as an argument not to risk life for the sake of gain.
How long does it take to salt an Ustekaya? How much is there of it to put on a dish?Ustekdya (Solanum Trilobatum) is a small fruit.Applied to something very insigniﬁcant.

.He is like an Uduga seed.The seed of the Uduga ( Alangium Decapetalum) after falling from the tree, is said to attach itself again to the trunk when the rain falls.To stick to a man like a leech in spite of all rebuffs.
Unless you had touched the garlick [your ﬁngers] would not have smelt.If you had not meddled with a bad business you would not have got a bad name.He who touches pitch defiles himself. (Italian)
When there are onions Malli is a ﬁne cook.Onions make up for bad cooking.
If you preserve your life, you can at least live by selling salt.Used as an argument not to risk life for the sake of gain.
How long does it take to salt an Ustekaya? How much is there of it to put on a dish?Ustekdya (Solanum Trilobatum) is a small fruit.Applied to something very insigniﬁcant.

He is like an Uduga seed.The seed of the Uduga ( Alangium Decapetalum) after falling from the tree, is said to attach itself again to the trunk when the rain falls.To stick to a man like a leech in spite of all rebuffs.
How long will the Uda food last? How long will your employment continue?
Can the belly be ﬁlled by holding in the breath?Inadequate measures. Idleness.Idleness must thank itself if it go barefoot.
The village is full of relations, but there is no place to hang up the sling (i. e. no shelter).Give out that you have many friends, and believe that you have but few. (French.)

proverb: ఊరంతా వడ్లు యెండబెట్టుకొంటే, నక్క తోక యెండబెట్టుకొన్నదట.
translation: When the whole village put their rice in the sun to dry, the jackal put his tail to dry.Foolish imitation.
The village is full of garlick, what do I want with thee O mother?
Lame in the village and an antelope in the jungle.A skulk.
All the village has one way, and the prig another.

proverb: ఊరికి పొయ్యేవానికి లేకపోయినా, బహిర్భూమికి పొయ్యేవానికి బత్యము కట్టుమన్నట్టు.
translation: Never mind giving provisions to the man setting out on a journey, but supply them to the man going to the rear of the village.
There is but one dancing girl in the village; before whom is she to dance?

proverb: ఊరికి వుపకారముగా ఆలికి కోక కొని పెట్టుతాను, యింటింటికీ డబ్బు యివ్వండి అన్నాడట.
translation: As a public benefaction, I will buy a cloth for my wife; give me a pice from each house.
If they are to be had for nothing, I have a relative here. Wishing to get two shares.Greediness.
If you can’t be quiet, hang yourself.

proverb: ఊరి పిడుగు వచ్చి వీరిశెట్టిని కొట్టుకొని పోయినది.
translation: The misfortune of the village has carried off Virisetti.The story is as follows: A burglar in digging through the wall of a house was killed by the wall falling upon him; his wife prosecuted the owner of the house for having badly built walls; the house owner blamed the builder; the builder blamed a woman who had distracted his attention while at his work, by passing by that way; the woman blamed a goldsmith to whom she was going to get certain jewels which he had not ﬁnished for her; the goldsmith blamed the banker for not letting him have gold in time; the banker was sentenced accordingly to be impaled, but on the point of being put to death he suggested that the size of the instruments would be much better suited to fat Virisetti (to whom he owed a grudge); fat Virisetti was executed accordingly. This happened at Anyayapura (the city of injustice.)The innocent sutfering for the guilty.

proverb: ఊరి మీద నూరు పడ్డా, కరణము మీద కాసు పడదు.
translation: Though a hundred [pagodas] be levied from the village, not a cash will be paid by the Karanam.A cash (కాసు) is l-GOth of an Anna.The instrument of oppression does not himself suffer.

The inhabitant of the village is afraid of the cemetery; the stranger is afraid of the water. The inhabitant of the village has a superstitious dread of the burial or burning ground, and avoids it; but the stranger does not know its situation and walks through it. On the other hand, the villager knows the depth of the tank, and does not fear going into it, but the stranger is afraid to venture.No safe wading in an unknown water.

proverb: ఊరివారి బిడ్డను నగరివారు కొట్టితే, నగరివారి బిడ్డను నారాయణుడు కొట్టును.
translation: If one of the royal family strike a villager’s child, Narayana will strike his child."[The LORD] will avenge the blood of his servants." Deuteronomy xxxii. 43.
The village says “go,” the cemetery says “come.”About to die.He is burnt to the socket.

proverb: ఊరు విడిచి పొరుగూరికి వెళ్లినా, పూనిన కర్మము మానదు.
translation: Although you leave the village and go to another, your evil destiny will still attend you.

The village is like the Ustekaya and the religious duties are like the Tatikaya.The Ustekéya is a small fruit, and the Tatikaya a large fruit.Applied to exactions imposed upon people for religious purposes greater than they can bear.

proverb: ఊరే చేరవద్దు రౌతా అంటే, గుర్రాన్ని యెక్కడ కట్టేది అన్నాడట.
translation: When a trooper was told not to come into the village, he asked where he was to put up his horse.9

proverb: ఊళ్లు యేలే కొమారుడికన్నా వుపాదానమెత్తే పెనిమిటి నయము.
translation: A husband that begs is better than a son that rules over villages.The one clings to his wife, the other despises his mother.
.Remains of a debt, of a sore, or of a ﬁre should not be left.
Enquire not into the origin of a Rishi, a river, or a woman.Rishi is a saint. (See Wilson‘s Sans. Diet. )
He will not even throw his leavings to the crows.Said of a great miser.
If you begin to count, you will ﬁnd the whole cot full of holes.Referring to a bed made of plaited rope.When a man wishes to ﬁnd fault, he loses no opportunities.When love fails we copy all faults.

proverb: ఊళ్లు యేలే కొమారుడికన్నా వుపాదానమెత్తే పెనిమిటి నయము.
translation: A husband that begs is better than a son that rules over villages.The one clings to his wife, the other despises his mother.
Remains of a debt, of a sore, or of a ﬁre should not be left.
Enquire not into the origin of a Rishi, a river, or a woman.Rishi is a saint. (See Wilson‘s Sans. Diet. )
He will not even throw his leavings to the crows.Said of a great miser.
If you begin to count, you will ﬁnd the whole cot full of holes.Referring to a bed made of plaited rope.When a man wishes to ﬁnd fault, he loses no opportunities.When love fails we copy all faults.

proverb: ఊళ్లు యేలే కొమారుడికన్నా వుపాదానమెత్తే పెనిమిటి నయము.
translation: A husband that begs is better than a son that rules over villages.The one clings to his wife, the other despises his mother.
Remains of a debt, of a sore, or of a ﬁre should not be left.
Enquire not into the origin of a Rishi, a river, or a woman.Rishi is a saint. (See Wilson‘s Sans. Diet. )
He will not even throw his leavings to the crows.Said of a great miser.
If you begin to count, you will ﬁnd the whole cot full of holes.Referring to a bed made of plaited rope.When a man wishes to ﬁnd fault, he loses no opportunities.When love fails we copy all faults.
Will the number decrease by drying?A reply given to a cheat who thus attempted to account for a defficiency in the quantity of vegetables.

proverb: ఎండబడితే వుండబడుతుంది, వుండబడితే వండబడుతుంది, వండబడితే తిండబడుతుంది, తిండబడితే పండబడుతుంది.
translation: If the sun gets hot, he will have to stay; if he stays, dinner will have to be cooked for him; if it is cooked, it will have to be eaten ; and if he eats it, he will have to lie down.The muttered grumblings of a stingy host.
How kind the soamp is, searching for the well-rope!Said scoffingly by the wife whose husband was looking for the well rope, not to draw water for her, but to chastise her with.

proverb: ఎంతప్రొద్దు వుండగా లేచినా, తుమ్మగుంట వద్దనే తెల్లవారినది.
translation: However early he rose the day always dawned while he was at the Tumma tank.Tumma is the Acacia Arabica. Tanks are often named from the trees planted around them. The earlier the man got up the more he dilly-dallied on the strength of it, so the sun always found him at the tank, in the way to his work.Early up, and never the nearer.
However good a man a shepherd may be, he will not be entirely free from foolishness.

proverb: ఎంతమంచి పంది అయినా, అమేధ్యము తినక మానదు.
translation: However good the pig may be, it will never cease to eat filth.A hay in armour is still but a hog.

proverb: ఎందుకు యేడుస్తావురా పిల్లవాడా అంటే, యెల్లుండి మావాండ్లు కొట్టుతారన్నాడట.
translation: A lad when asked “Why are you crying my boy?” answered “They say my people are going to beat me the day after tomorrow.”Anticipating evils.You cry out before you are hurt.

proverb: ఎక్కడ కట్టితే నేమి, మనమందలో యీనితే సరి.
translation: What matters it whose the bull was? If the cow calves in our herd, it‘s all right.Applied to an unprincipled action.

proverb: ఎక్కడయినా బావా అంవచ్చునుగాని, వంగతోట వద్ద బావా అనగూడదు.
translation: Call me brother-in-law any where but at the Brinjal garden.Unwillingness to acknowledge relationship when it would cost anything.

proverb: ఎక్కడ వున్నావే కంబళీ అంటే, వేశిన చోటనే వున్నాను వెంగళీ అన్నదట.
translation: “O blanket where are you?" said he “Where you left me, you madman,” it replied.A smart repartee.

proverb: ఎక్కడికి పోతావు విధమ్మా అంటే, వెంటనే వస్తాను పదమ్మా అన్నదట.
translation: “Where are you going to Madam Fate?” asked one “ I’ll follow you, go on” she replied.The evil results of mixing with bad company.

proverb: ఎక్కితే గుర్రపు రౌతు, దిగితే కాలి బంటు.
translation: If I ride I shall be a trooper, if I dismount I shall be a foot' soldier.A man who makes himself useful in any position.
The elephant is subject to the man who has mounted it.
Having mounted [the tree] and felt [the fruit], he came down again and threw stones at it.
He went to mock, and fell on his back.He went out shearing and he came home ‘horn.The biter bit.

If I say this, it will be killing a Brahman; if I say that, it will be killing a cow.Said by a reluctant witness to himself, when called to give evidence regarding a complaint made by a Brahman that another man's cow had grazed in his ﬁeld.Being placed in a dilemma. by having to give evidence in a quarrel between two friends.

proverb: ఎత్తుకొని తిన్నబోలె యెదురుగా వస్తే, చంకన వున్నబోలె సలాం చేశినదట.
translation: When he was met by the bowl that he used to collect alms in and eat out of, the bowl under his arm made salam to it.A begging impostor paying a visit on one occasion to a village where he thought he was not known, kept his alms-bowl under his arm. but when he met a man carrying an old bowl that had once belonged to him, the one under his arm is said to have betrayed its owner by claiming acquaintance with its predecessor.
Is a bullock which has fallen down from fatigue afraid of a tiger?Resignation to one’s fate through utter helplessness.

proverb: ఎదట అన్నది మాట, యెదాన పెట్టింది వాత.
translation: Reprove a man to his face; brand a man on his chest.Don't abuse a man behind his back.He who praises in praesentia, and abuse in absentia, have with him pestilentia. (German)
The man in front is the bridegroom.

proverb: ఎదటి పొయ్యి మండితే తన పొయ్యిలో నీళ్లు పోసుకొన్నట్టు.
translation: When the ﬁre was lighted in the opposite house, he threw water on his own.An envious man wazes lean with the fairness of his neighbour.Envy is its own torturer. (Danish)
The woman who was met wasted away.One woman met another out of whom a spirit had been driven, and fancied she was possessed.Unreasonable fears.
While the bullock was ploughing, the tick pulled and blew.Applied to a person who does little in assisting others but makes much of it.What a dust have I raised! quoth the ﬂy upon the coach.
Is the bullock's sore tender to the crow?Said of one devoid of sympathy for others.
“The bullock has calved” [said one] “Then tie it up in the shed" [replied the other].Applied to a foolish speech.

proverb: ఎద్దు యెండకు లాగ, దున్నపోతు నీడకు లాగ.
translation: The bullock pulled towards the sun, and the buffalo towards the shade.A bad match.Every couple is not a pair.
Ropes after bullocks.Taken to tie them up with.Providing one’s self with what is necessary.

proverb: ఎద్ద్దేమి యెరుగురా అటుకులచవి, గాడ్దేమి యెరుగురా గంధపొడి వాసన.
translation: What does a bullock know of the taste of parched grain? what does an ass know of the smell of perfume?To throw pearls before swine.Strew no roses before swine. (Dutch.)
As rain falls on a male buffalo.Apathetic indifference.
Fearing the buffalo would kick, he went behind the horse.

proverb: ఎన్నడూ యెరగని రెడ్డి గుర్రము యెక్కితే, ముందూ వెనక ఆయెను.
translation: The Reddi who had never mounted a horse before, sat with his face to the tail.
Abuse me as much as you like, I've got my handful of grain out of you.Said by a beggar who had received alms and abuse together.When you have given me roast meal, you beat me with the spit.

proverb: ఎన్నో రణాలు కోశినానుగాని, నారణమంత తీపు లేదన్నట్టు.
translation: I have out many boils, but there was never such pain as in my own.Said by a Surgeon.To cut into another man’s ear is like cutting into a felt hat. (Danish)He laughs at scars who never felt a wound. (German)
When a good deed [ is done,] then only [is it thought of].Eaten bread is soon forgotten.

proverb: ఎరగని వూళ్లో యెమ్మెలు చేస్తే, యేకులు నీ మొగుడు వడుకుతాడా.
translation: If you give yourself airs in the village where you are a stranger, will your husband spin the cotton?
Acquaintance is a handful of money.

proverb: ఎరువుల సొమ్ములు బరువుకు చేటు, తియ్యా పెట్టా తీపుల చేటు, వాటిలో వొకటి పోతే అప్పులచేటు.
translation: Borrowed ornaments cause inconvenience by their weight, pain by taking them off and putting them on, and debt if one of them is lost.He that goes a borrowing goes a sorrowing.
Like catching a ﬁsh with a worm.Fish follow the bait.10
Like a cat crouching for a mouse.
The cat a witness in the rat’s case.Interested evidence.A fox should not be of the jury at a goose trial.
Will the cat faint at the death of the rat?Indifference.
Like getting angry with a rat and setting ﬁre to one’s house.Burn not your house to fright away the mice.
Like pulling a bear’s hairs out with tweezers.An endless business.A cask that will never ﬁll. (Greek)

When it dawns, we shall know whose mother is alive.Said by a man to his wife, at whose instigation he had agreed that they should kill his mother. The mothers of the pair slept together and the wife's mother was murdered instead of the husband's, with the latter’s knowledge. The wife, having an inkling of the truth, endeavoured to persuade her husband to go and see which of the two they had spared, and he replied as above.
Elli Setti’s account is a single account.Receipts and disbursements, proﬁts and loss, all muddled up together.
Whose father’s property are you sobbing for?Said to a man who is disappointed at not getting what he has no right to expect.He has a good estate, but the right owner keeps it from him,
Whose child are you, that you cry and weep so bitterly?
Every man to his own way on the banks of the Yamuna (Jumna).There every man is free to do what he has a fancy for.
Every man must dip in his own waters.Every one must bear his own troubles.Each cross both its inscription.Every one bears his cross. (French)
Every man’s life is sweet to himself.Life is sweet.
Every man’s folly is pleasure to himself.Fools are pleased with their own blunders.

proverb: ఎవరు యేమి చేశినా, యింటికి ఆలు అవుతుందా, దొంతికి కడవ అవుతుందా.
translation: Will any service rendered by another be as a wife to the house or a pot for the pile?Said by a man whose wife has died or gone astray.

proverb: ఏకాదశా బ్రాహ్మణుడా అంటే, కాలే కొరివి యెగసన తోస్తావా అన్నాడట -ద్వాదశా బ్రాహ్మణుడా అంటే, ఆపదలు కాపురాలు చేస్తావా అన్నాడట.“Is it the fast O Brahman?” [asked one] “Will you stir up the ﬁre?” [replied the Brahman already irritated by fasting]. “Is it the festival?” [said the ﬁrst]. “Do troubles last for ever?” [answered the Brahman, delighted at the prospect of being fed.]
translation: Welcome and unwelcome intelligence.
Whichever leg slips there is danger to the child.
Every man’s folly is pleasure to himself.Fools are pleased with their own blunders.

proverb: ఎవరు యేమి చేశినా, యింటికి ఆలు అవుతుందా, దొంతికి కడవ అవుతుందా.
translation: Will any service rendered by another be as a wife to the house or a pot for the pile?Said by a man whose wife has died or gone astray.

proverb: ఏకాదశా బ్రాహ్మణుడా అంటే, కాలే కొరివి యెగసన తోస్తావా అన్నాడట -ద్వాదశా బ్రాహ్మణుడా అంటే, ఆపదలు కాపురాలు చేస్తావా అన్నాడట.“Is it the fast O Brahman?” [asked one] “Will you stir up the ﬁre?” [replied the Brahman already irritated by fasting]. “Is it the festival?” [said the ﬁrst]. “Do troubles last for ever?” [answered the Brahman, delighted at the prospect of being fed.]
translation: Welcome and unwelcome intelligence.
Whichever leg slips there is danger to the child.

proverb: ఏకుల వంటి మెతుకులు వేసుకొని యెనప పెరుగు పోసుకొని యమరాలిని గనుక తింటూ వున్నాను గాని, దగ్గిరికి రాకండి పిల్లల్లాలా జడుసుకొనేరు.
translation: I am a terrible woman eating grains of rice like ﬂocks of cotton mixed with buffalo curds, so don’t come near me children, you’ll take fright!Said by a greedy woman to keep her children off.
Will a basket be broken by being ﬁlled with ﬂocks of cotton?Ineffectual measures.
Like setting the sail according to the wind.Set your sail according to the wind. (French)
The pearls on the other side of the river are as large as palmyra fruits.Exaggeration in describing things not present.

proverb: ఏటికి యెప్పుడు పోయినావు యిసక యెప్పుడు తెచ్చినావు అంటే, ఆడవారు తలిస్తే అది యెంతసేపు అన్నదట-మొగవారు తలిస్తే యిది యెంతసేపు అని నాలుగు బాదినాడట.
translation: “When did you go to the river, when did you bring the sand?” [asked the husband] “If women like, how long will that take?" [saucily replied the wife] “If men like, how long will this take?” [said the husband] giving her four double-handers.The husband's question was caused by ﬁnding send in his food.
A tree on the river bank always totters.Said of a man in an insecure position.
The grazing on the island is not worth the swim across the river.
Although the jackal lives near the river, will it know where the ford is?Applied to ignorant persons in high position.
A ﬁeld on the bank of the river.A dangerous situation.

proverb: ఏట్లో వంకాయలు కాస్తవా అంటే కాస్తవి అన్నట్టు.
translation: Like saying “yes” when asked whether brinjals grow in the river.A time server.
Although you throw [things] into the river, take an account of them.Keep your expenditure within bounds.

proverb: ఏడిచేదాని మొగుడు వస్తే, నా మొగుడూ వస్తాడు.
translation: If the weeping widow’s husband returns, mine will come also.Stolid indifference. Want of feeling.
Like showing a plantain to a crying child.

proverb: ఏడుపులో యేడుపు యెడమ చెయ్యి బయట పెట్టుమన్నట్టు.
translation: Show your [withered] left hand and add to their grief.The story is that after a marriage the bridegroom was found to be a fool, which caused much regret to the family of the bride. The bride groom's friends then told him to show the withered state of his left hand which he had hitherto concealed.Making bad matters worse.

proverb: ఏడుస్తూ యేరువాక సాగితే, కాడి మోకులు దొంగలు యెత్తుకొని పోయినారట.
translation: While the man was sluggishly ploughing, thieves stole the rope collars.
When one says he’s going, the other says he’s dying.Spirit of contradiction.
The porcupine has sense in the back of its head.Said of a man without brains.

proverb: ఏనుగకు కాలు విరగడమూ, దోమకు రెక్క విరగడమూ సమము.
translation: The loss of a wing is the same to a mosquito as the loss of a leg to an elephant.Proportionate losses.

proverb: ఏనుగకు వక శీమ, గుర్రానికి వక వూరు, బర్రెకు వక బానిశె.
translation: To keep an elephant [a man requires] a country, to keep a horse a village, and to keep a she-buifalo a maid.
A wood apple is very small in an elephant's throat.A very easy matter.
Will the thirst of an elephant [be quenched] by the water which drops from the eaves?
Like dogs barking at an elephant.

proverb: ఏనుగను తెచ్చి యేకుల బుట్టలో వుంచి అది తననెత్తిన పెట్టి తన్ను యెత్తుకో అంటాడు.
translation: He tells me to put the elephant into the cotton basket, to place the basket on his head, and to lift him up.Ekulabutta is a very small hand basket.Being asked to do an utter impossibility.
The man who has mounted an elephant will not be afraid at the bark of a dog.He who is in favor with the great will not fear the enmity of the mean.
Even when the elephant lies down he is as high as the horse.A man much superior to another.
Like a wood-apple swallowed by the elephant.It is swallowed whole, and is said to be found afterwards empty though unbroken.Said of a person in an inﬂuential position who does not beneﬁt others.
Like asking a man on an elephant for some chunam.His hand cannot be reached.
To the man that eats elephants, corpses are as pastry.A thoroughly bad man won’t stick at triﬁes.
Whatever work is neglected, eating is not forgotten.11

proverb: ఏ పుట్టలో యే పాము వున్నదో తెలియదు.
translation: What snake it is, and what hole it lives in, we don’t know.Said of persons whose abilities are not yet known.

proverb: ఏమి అప్పాజీ అంటే, కాలం కొద్దీ రాయజీ అన్నాడట.
translation: “What, O Appaji! [is to be done?” said the king] “As occasion requires, O king!” replied [the minister].

proverb: ఏమి పోలిశెట్టీ అంటే, యెప్పటి మొత్తుకోళ్లే అన్నాడట.
translation: When Polisetti was asked how he was getting on, he replied “I am in trouble as usual.”
Not knowing what to do, he began to mimic others.

proverb: ఏమీ లేనమ్మకు యేడ్పుల శృంగారము, కలిగినమ్మకు కడుపుల శృంగారము.
translation: Weeping is the ornament of a poor woman, embonpoint is the ornament of a rich woman.

proverb: ఏమీ లేని ఆకులు యెగసి పడితే, అన్నీవున్న ఆకులు అణిగి వుండినవి.
translation: The leaf dishes which were empty rose up high; those that were full remained steady.( See Nos. 502, 1274.)The conceit of little knowledge, the humility of true learning.Shoal waters mak the maist din. (Scotch)

proverb: ఏ యెండకు ఆ గొడుగు పట్ట వలెను.
translation: The umbrella must suit the hour of the day.Suitable to the occasion.

proverb: ఏరుకొని తినే పక్షి ముక్కున ముల్లు విరిగినట్టు.
translation: Like a thorn piercing the beak of a bird that was picking up food.Losing the only means of support.
Like the ﬂood subsiding.Perfect stillness after a tumult.After a storm comes a calm.
As the river sleeps.Smooth waters run deep.There is no worse water than that which sleeps. (French.)

proverb: ఏరు మూరెడు తీస్తే, కయ్య బారెడు తీస్తుంది.
translation: When the river goes down a cubit, the water in the ﬁeld goes down a fathom.If you lose a little in the favor of the great, you sink four times as much in the estimation of the people.

proverb: ఏరు యెంత పారినా కుక్కకు కతుకు నీళ్లే.
