translation: Let the river ﬂow ever so abundantly, the dog only gets what it can lap up.A servant neglected by a master liberal to strangers.

proverb: ఏరు యేడామడ వుండగానే, చీర విప్పి చంక పెట్టుకొని పోయినట్టు.
translation: She took off her cloth and put it under her arm, While the river was still seventy milesoﬂ'.Do not strip before bed time. (French)
Day and night are one to the [Supreme] Ruler."The darkness and the light are both alike to Thee." Psalm cxxxix. 12.

proverb: ఏలుకోవడానకు వూళ్లు లేవుగాని, యెత్తుకొని తినడానకు వూళ్లు లేవా.
translation: There are no villages to rule over, but are there not villages to beg in?Said by a beggar refused alms.

proverb: ఏలేవాని యెద్దు పోతేనేమి, కాచేవాని కన్ను పోతేనేమి.
translation: If the master’s ox be lost what is it [to the servant?]. If the watchman’s eye be lost what is it [to the master?].
There is no limit to riches.
Either one way or the other.(Corrupted from the Hindustani.)

proverb: ఏరు యేడామడ వుండగానే, చీర విప్పి చంక పెట్టుకొని పోయినట్టు.
translation: She took off her cloth and put it under her arm, While the river was still seventy milesoﬂ'.Do not strip before bed time. (French)
Day and night are one to the [Supreme] Ruler."The darkness and the light are both alike to Thee." Psalm cxxxix. 12.

proverb: ఏలుకోవడానకు వూళ్లు లేవుగాని, యెత్తుకొని తినడానకు వూళ్లు లేవా.
translation: There are no villages to rule over, but are there not villages to beg in?Said by a beggar refused alms.

proverb: ఏలేవాని యెద్దు పోతేనేమి, కాచేవాని కన్ను పోతేనేమి.
translation: If the master’s ox be lost what is it [to the servant?]. If the watchman’s eye be lost what is it [to the master?].
There is no limit to riches.
Either one way or the other.(Corrupted from the Hindustani.)
Like buying and bringing a disease with which the body is not troubled.Willfully bringing trouble on one’s self.
Will the woman who cannot bear one [trouble] bear two?

proverb: ఒక కంచాన తిని వక మంచాన పడుకొనేవారు.
translation: They eat of one dish and sleep on one bed.Extreme intimacy.They are hand and glove.

proverb: ఒక కన్ను కన్నూ కాదు, వక కొడుకు కొడుకూ కాడు.
translation: One eye is no eye, one son is no son.
Will the morning dawn after dreaming one dream?After one trouble, think not that you have passed through all.
One blow and two pieces.To give a direct answer and settle a matter one way or the other.
A hundred one by one, or a hundred at once?Said in doubt of a person's liberality, as the “hundred” promised would probably turn out to mean only one.

With one day’s acting, all his mustaches were burnt off.By standing too near the torches which are used to light up the ﬁgures of the performers, and on which powdered resin is thrown.Said of a clumsy novice.
One day holding (fasting); one day breaking fast.A precarious subsistence.Change of fortune is the lot of life.To-day a king, to-morrow nothing. (French)
One day a feast, one day medicine.One day favor, one day disfavor.To-day in ﬁnary, to-morrow in ﬁlth. (German)

proverb: ఒకరి కలిమికి యేడిస్తే వక కన్ను పోయినది, తనలేమికి యేడిస్తే మరి వక కన్నూ పోయినది.
translation: By weeping at the good fortune of another he lost one eye, and by weeping at his own ill fortune he lost the other. ​
A man is no Doctor until he has killed one or two [patients.]Said to a bungling tyro.If the doctor cures, the sun sees it; if he kills, the earth hides it.
There are a thousand ways to a village.Every man in his way.There are more ways to the wood than one.

If you clap with one hand will there be any sound?Nothing can be done successfully by a single person.One man is no man. (Latin)Two heads are better than one.Hand washes hand, and ﬁnger ﬁnger. (Greek)
If you remove stone by stone, even a mountain will be levelled.You must pluck out the hairs of a horse's tail one by one. (Latin)Drop by drop the lake is drained.
The brazier has spoilt the dish which before I ate out of slantingly (on account of the hole in it).
The lazy woman complained that her toe-rings pinched her.Idle folks lack no excuses.

proverb: ఒళ్లు వాచిన రెడ్డీ వడ్లు యేమిధర అని అడిగితే, అవి లేకనే నా వొళ్ళంతా వాచినది అన్నాడట.
translation: “ Well, swollen-bodied Reddi! how do you sell your paddy?”“ The want of it alone has made my whole body swell,” he replied._ The Hindus believe that longing for any desired object causes the legs and face to swell.
Is there any inspiration which makes a man unaware of his own actions? Is there any lie not known to him that utters it?
Like one who does not know the alphabet attempting multiplication.

proverb: ఓడలు బండ్లా వస్తున్నవి, బండ్లు వోడలా వస్తున్నవి.
translation: Ships come on carts; carts come on ships.When ships are broken up, the pieces are carried on carts.Good and ill fortune follow each other.
The lazy woman complained that her toe-rings pinched her.Idle folks lack no excuses.

proverb: ఒళ్లు వాచిన రెడ్డీ వడ్లు యేమిధర అని అడిగితే, అవి లేకనే నా వొళ్ళంతా వాచినది అన్నాడట.
translation: “ Well, swollen-bodied Reddi! how do you sell your paddy?”“ The want of it alone has made my whole body swell,” he replied._ The Hindus believe that longing for any desired object causes the legs and face to swell.
Is there any inspiration which makes a man unaware of his own actions? Is there any lie not known to him that utters it?
Like one who does not know the alphabet attempting multiplication.

proverb: ఓడలు బండ్లా వస్తున్నవి, బండ్లు వోడలా వస్తున్నవి.
translation: Ships come on carts; carts come on ships.When ships are broken up, the pieces are carried on carts.Good and ill fortune follow each other.
One said “ Loss, loss !” the other replied “A hole in the dish.”A pun on the word Odu.
There is nothing which an invalid will not ask for; and there is nothing which an envious man will not say.
The lazy man looks upon those as his parents who say “Don’t [work ].”An idler is delighted at every interruption.

proverb: ఓబీ ఓబీ నీవు వడ్లు దంచు, నేను పక్కలు యెగర వేస్తాను.
translation: Obi, Obi, you pound the rice, and I’ll shake my sides.(i.e. imitate the action of pounding).Applied to an idle skulk, who pretends to be always busy.Lazy folks take the most pains.
With patience, Orugallu will become a city.Rome was not built in a day.

proverb: ఓర్చలేనమ్మ వడిని నిప్పులు కట్టుకొంటే, వడీ దడీ కూడా కాలినది.
translation: When the envious woman put ﬁre in her waist, her clothes were burnt and the mat screen also.Causing one's own ruin through envy.12

proverb: ఓర్చలేని రెడ్డి వుండీ చెరిచెను, చచ్చీ చెరిచెను.
translation: The envious Reddi ruined the village while living, and was a curse to it when dead.A Reddi who had oppressed the people of his village all his life, requested them, when dying, to burn his body in a certain spot. This they willingly acceded to, and took the Reddi’s corpse there for that purpose, when they were attacked by the inhabitants of the neighbouring village, within the limit of which the Reddi had desired to be burned, and this became an everlasting cause of contention. (See Tatachari’s Tales Page 5.)

proverb: ఓలి తక్కువ అని గుడ్డిదాన్ని పెండ్లాడితే, దొంతి కుండలన్నీ పగల కొట్టినదట.
translation: When a man married a blind woman, on account of the smallness of the jointure, she broke all the pots in the pile.

proverb: ఓలి తక్కువ అని గుడ్డిదాన్ని పెండ్లాడితే నెలకు మూడు ఆవాలు.
translation: When he married a blind woman, because of the small jointure, [she broke] three kilns of pots a month.False economy.

Aha! so you were not able to ﬁnd me!Said by a jackass of a bridegroom who had hidden himself on the top of the shed at the time of the marriage, and looked on while the bride was married to another man.

proverb: ఔషధానకు అవపథ్యానకు చెల్లు, రోగము పై పెచ్చు.
translation: [The beneﬁt derived from] the medicine is to be deducted from [the harm done by] carelessness in diet, and the balance remaining is an increase of the disease.Diet cureస్ more than the lancet.The best physicians are Dr. Diet, Dr. Quiet, and Dr. Merryman.

proverb: కంకణముల చెయ్యి ఆడితే, కడియముల చెయ్యి ఆడును.If the hand with Kankanamulu moves, the hand with Kadiyamulu moves also.Konkanamulu are bracelets worn by women. Kadiyamulu are those worn by men.Where woman leads man follows.

translation: Selling the dish and buying toe-rings.He has given the hen for the egg. (German)
She made the family as large as Kanchi into clods.Said of a woman who by going astray brings disgrace upon the whole family.Kanchi is the celebrated town of Conjeveram.

proverb: ఔషధానకు అవపథ్యానకు చెల్లు, రోగము పై పెచ్చు.
translation: [The beneﬁt derived from] the medicine is to be deducted from [the harm done by] carelessness in diet, and the balance remaining is an increase of the disease.Diet cureస్ more than the lancet.The best physicians are Dr. Diet, Dr. Quiet, and Dr. Merryman.

proverb: కంకణముల చెయ్యి ఆడితే, కడియముల చెయ్యి ఆడును.If the hand with Kankanamulu moves, the hand with Kadiyamulu moves also.Konkanamulu are bracelets worn by women. Kadiyamulu are those worn by men.Where woman leads man follows.

translation: Selling the dish and buying toe-rings.He has given the hen for the egg. (German)
She made the family as large as Kanchi into clods.Said of a woman who by going astray brings disgrace upon the whole family.Kanchi is the celebrated town of Conjeveram.

Does gold ever ring like hell metal ?A wise man is sparing of his words, but a fool likes the sound of his own voice.A deluge of words and a drop of sense.“It is not the hen which cacklcs most that lays most eggs." (Dutch.)
The life has reached the throat, (i. e. all but departed ).Being placed in a most diﬁicult position.
If out of sight, is it out of hearing also?
The God who destroyed the eyes, gave understanding.When God deprives a man of sight, He increases his intellectual powers.
Will you worship the sun after losing your eyes?If their sight begins to fail, Hindus worship the sun for their recovery.Persisting in that which has become of no avail.
[Apply] collyrium to your eyes while you have them.Make use of your opportunities before they pass from you.

proverb: కందకు లేదు, చేమకు లేదు, తోటకూరకు వచ్చెనా దురద.
translation: No acridity in Kanda or Chéma, how can there be any in Totakura?Kanda is the Arum Campanulatum; Chéma is the Colocasia Antiquorum; Totakura is applied to the Amaranthus Oleraceus, and other herbs.When a man’s relatives and friends won’t assist him, why should help be expected from a mere acquaintance ?
If the Kanda possess no acridity, why should it be looked for in the Bachchali?Bachchali is Basella. Cordifolia (the Indian spinach).

proverb: కక్కిన కుక్కవద్దికీ కన్న కుక్కవద్దికీ కానివాణ్ని అయినా పంపవద్దు.
translation: Don’t send even your enemy near a dog which has vomited, or a bitch which has pupped.
Those who criticize the design of a built house are a thousand.He that buildeth upon the highway hath many advisers. (Dutch)Every fool can find faults that a great many wise men can't remedy.

A man that has built a house has one house, a man that has built no house has a thousand houses.A man without a house can change his residence as often as he pleases.He who has no house of his own is every where at home. (spanish)

proverb: కట్టుకొన్న ఆపె పెట్టుకొన్న ఆపె వుండగా, యెదురుపడ్డ ఆపె యెండిపోయినదట.
translation: When the woman who had worn the cloth, and the woman who had the cloth in her possession met another woman, she began to pine away.The cloth spoken of is the కాటేరికోక worn by pregnant women to propitiate the goddess కాటేరి (Katéri). If a pregnant woman who has neglected the worship of this goddess, sees one of these garments, she takes fright.
The ﬁre place takes the crookedness out of the stick.A bad man’s evil qualities only disappear in the funeral pile.The wolf loses his teeth but not his inclination. (Spanish)

proverb: కడగా పొయ్యే శనైశ్చరుడా మా యింటిదాకా వచ్చి పొమ్మన్నట్టు.
translation: O Saturn! who art passing by, pay my house a visit.Saturn is the god of misfortune.He that courts injury will obtain it. (Danish)

Although the pumpkin be as big as a large pot, yet it is subject to the kitchen knife.Gummadi kaya is the Cucurbita Maxima.However great a family a wife may come from, she must still be subject to her husband.

proverb: కడి అంటే నోరు తెరిచి, కళ్లెమంటే నోరు మూశినట్టు.
translation: Like [a horse] which opens its mouth for a morsel, and shuts it for the bridle.
A lump of food [given in charity] will save you from trouble.He who lends to the poor, gets his interest from God. (German)

proverb: కడుపు కూటికి యేడిస్తే, కొప్పు పూలకు యేడ్చినది.
translation: When the belly cried for food, the hair cried for ﬂowers.

proverb: కడుపుతో వున్నమ్మ కనక మానునా, వండినమ్మ తినక మానునా.
translation: Will the woman with child refrain from bringing forth? will the woman who cooked not help herself?One is as certain as the other.He is a poor cook that cannot lick his own ﬁngers.
If you eat a bellyful of cakes, [you will get] a bodyful of fever.
A bargain [with a man] with his belly full.A person in easy circumstances will drive a hard bargain.
Cakes are bitter to a man with his belly full.A rich man will not care for triﬂing gains.
If you let out your secret, it will get all over the village.Three know it, all know it. (Italian.)The secret of two is God's secret; the secret of three is all the world's. (French)

proverb: కడుపులో బిడ్డ కడుపులోనే వుండగా, కొడుకు పేరు సోమలింగం.
translation: While the child is still in the womb, the son is named Somalingam.Boil not the pap before the child is born.
Will [affection] that does not exist in the heart be caused by embracing [the body]?
After conception there’s nothing for it but to bring forth.A work undertaken must be completed.
If he laps, it wont answer.If a person goes to get a wife for another, and eats in the house before the matter is arranged, it is sure to fall through.

proverb: కత్తి తీశి కంపలో వేశి యేకు తీశి పొడుచుకుంటానన్నట్టు.
translation: Like a man throwing away the knife and threatening to kill himself with a flock of cotton.Saying and doing are two things.
Though the stab of the sword may fail, the stab of the pen will not fail.
There is no softness in a sword, nor goodness in a mother-in law.13

proverb: కత్తివాణా కలంవాణా.Is the sword sharp or the pen?The pen in the hand of the strong is more powerful than the sword.A goose-guilt is more dangerous than a lion’s claw.
కథకు కాళ్లు లేవు ముంతకు చెవులు లేవు.
translation: No legs to the tale, no cars to the pot.Said of a story that one cannot make head or tail of.A story without a head. (Greek)

proverb: కననిది బిడ్డ కాదు, కట్టనిది చీర కాదు.
translation: That which has not been brought forth is not a child; that which is not worn is not a garment.Nothing belonging to others can make up for the want of one’s own.
Seeing, blind; hearing, deaf."Eyes have they, but they see not: they have ears, but they hear not," Psalm cxv. 5, 6.
When told to go and look, he goes and burns.Alluding to the story of Hanuman setting fire to Lanka, when sent to look for Sitâ.
Some things must be hidden even from the mother that bore you.
Should you not be careful of that which may stick into your eye?Keep an eye on your enemies.
Will you put so much collyrium on your eye, as to destroy it?Little sticks kindle a ﬁre, but great ones put it out.
Although the eye does not see, the belly ﬁnds.A man’s want leads him to seek and ﬁnd a livelihood.A hungry man sees far.A hungry man discovers more than a hundred lawyers. (Spanish)

proverb: కన్ను యెర్రనయినా మిన్ను యెర్రనయినా కారక మానదు.
translation: When the eye is red, or the sky is red, (at sunset) there will surely be a dropping.

proverb: కన్నేల పోయెనోయి కనకలింగమా అంటే, చేసుకుకొన్న కర్మమోయి శంభులింగమా అన్నాడట.
translation: “O Kanakalingam! how did you lose your eyes?” asked one;“The result of my deeds, O Sambhulingam!” replied the other.An evasive answer.
A frog never bites, a Brahman never ﬁghts.Said of a cowardly person.

proverb: కమ్మ నీచు కడిగినా పోదు, కాకిచిప్ప పెట్టి గోకినా పోదు.
translation: The smell of Kammavaru cannot be got rid of by Washing, or scraping with a shell.Kainmavaru are a class of Telugu S'udras, whose occupation is farming.They are proverbially dirty people.
Muttering like a man plucking Kari Vépa leaves.Kari Vépa is the Bergera Koenigii and is used in making curries. There is an idea that the leaves lose their ﬂavour if plucked silently.
The valour of Karna.A blusterer.

proverb: కర్మములుగల మగణ్ని కంబట్లోకట్టి బుజముమీద వేసుకొంటే జారి వీధిలో పడ్దట్టు.
translation: Like the guilty husband who slipped out of the blanket and fell into the street when his wife was carrying him on her shoulder (to save him from punishment).
Even a sheep will bite a man without a stick.Every man should be prepared to defend himself.
Will a black bitch become a Kapila cow?The Kapila is a tawny cow worshipped by the Hindus.
The everlasting thief is one day caught.The old fox is caught at last.The old ape is taken at last. (Latin)
There is no dryness in the aloe, nor newness in a daughter-in-law.A daughter-in-law is never a stranger.
What matters it whether one drinks milk in a dream out of bell metal or gold?Building castles in the air.
Enjoyment in a dream.
A girl who has come from a rich man’s house cannot even look at a Kâkarakâya.Kâkarakâya is the Memordica Charantia.Puffed up with pride.
If you can afford it, [your dress] should cover your feet; if not, it should cover your knees.Living according to one’s means.Cut your coat according Io your cloth.

proverb: కలిగినయ్య గాదె తీసేటప్పటికి పేదవానికి ప్రాణము పోయినది.
translation: By the time the rich man opened his corn bin, the poor man had died.

proverb: కలిగినయ్య కలిగినయ్యకే పెట్టును, లేనయ్య కలిగినయ్యకే పెట్టును.
translation: The rich man will feed the rich man and the poor man will feed the rich man.Who has, is - (Italian.)
Although they give him food mixed with rice washings, he looks towards the net.The guest disbelieved the poor state of the house.
Wealth is the paramour of all castes.A rich man is sought after by all.Rich people are every where at home. (German)
Having and not having are the pots in a Kavadi.Equally balanced.Fortune and misfortune are two buckets in a well. (German)Every day hath its night, every weal its woe.
The few days remaining in the Iron age must pass away.The Kali Yuga is said to contain 432,000 years of which the 4968th year commenced in A. D. 1866.An ironical phrase applied to procrastination.
A Ravana of the Iron age.Applied to a man as bad in this, the Iron age, as Ravana was in the Golden.

Like the Gachcha bush growing round the Kalpa tree.The Gachcha is a thorny bush, (Guilandina Bonducella); the Kalpa is the celestial tree of desire.Applied to a benevolent man surrounded by evil persons, who do not suffer others to approach him.
False gold is very rough.
False gold is very bright.All is not gold that glitters.
When marriage comes, or vomiting, it cannot be stopped.

proverb: కష్ట సుఖాలు రెండూ కావటి కుండల వంటివి.
translation: Sorrow and joy are like two pots on a Kavadi.Kavadi is the yoke made of a split bamboo, used by lndian coolies. To the ends are slung loads of equal weight.Joy and sorrow are to-day and to-morrow.
When the crow cawed, she started.Feigned sensitiveness.
The crow is plucky, the cuckoo is cowardly.

proverb: కాకిని తెచ్చి పంజరములో పెట్టితే చిలుకవలె పలుకునా.
translation: If you put a crow in a cage will it talk like a parrot?Wash a dog, comb a dog, still a dog is but a dog. (French)
The crow’s chick is dear to the crow.The crow thinks her own bird the fairest.
The crow is black when it is born, and black when it is grown.What’s bred in the bone will never be out of the flesh.The wolf changes his hair but not his nature. (Latin)
Like a Donda fruit in a crow’s beak.Donda is a bright red fruit (Coccinea Indica)A beautiful thing possessed by a person who is unworthy of it.
What is it to the crows if the Nemmi trees, which they don’t like, grow or blossom ?Nemmi chettu is the Dalbergia Oojeinensis.
To kill crows and throw them to the kites.He robs Peter to pay Paul.

Predestined events will be accomplished by Gandharvas.In allusion to the death of Kichaka whom Draupadi declared to have been killed by her husbands the Gandharvas. (See Virata Parva of the Mahabharata.) Gandharvas are celestial musicians.The judgments of God are fulfilled by unseen instruments.The feet of the avenging deities are shod with wool. (Latin)

proverb: కాటికి కాళ్లు చాచుకొని తిండికి చెయ్యి చాచేవాడు.
translation: He stretches out his legs towards the cemetery, and stretches out his hand for food.Greedy to the last.
Don’t trust a Karanam even when he is being taken to the cemetery.
The crows ate up the fruit which grew in the forest.Property common to all.14
If you say Kadu, it is a Tamilian’s ear.

proverb: కాదు కాదు అంటే నాది నాది అన్నాడట.
translation: When one said “ Kadu, Kadu” the other said “Nadi, Nadi.”Kadu in Telugu means " it is not," "no" in Tamil it signiﬁes "the ear." A Telugu man on one occasion accidentally stuck the barb of his spear into a Tamilian's ear, on which the latter cried out " Kadu ! Kadu ! (My ear! my ear!). The Telugu man thinking he meant to say " Not yours, not yours" pulled at the spear all the harder, saying "Nadi! Nadi!" (It's mine! it's mine !Said to a man who is obstinate in argument.

proverb: కానకుండా కట్టెడిచ్చేను కాని వినకుండా వీరణాలు వాయించేవా.
translation: I will give you a bundle of grain without any one else seeing me, but will you play on the double drum without any one else hearing it’?A silly request made by a female, who was not allowed to indulge her taste for music.
Speaking of people who are not known, as having distributed rice-milk by baskets full.Exaggerating the bounty of absent persons.
In your evil hour your own stick will become a snake.i.e. your own friends will turn against you.
One sixty-fourth part of folly will bring ten millions of sorrows.A little neglect may breed great mischief
An unfriendly person is equal to one that is not.
In an unlucky time his Kandi wouldn’t boil soft.
The famine came in the very year that the cultivator came to the village.An unfortunate coincidence.
There is no effect without a cause.Every why has a wherefore.There is a cause for all things. Italian)

proverb: కార్తెముందర వురిమినా, కార్యము ముందర వదిరినా, చెడుతుంది.
translation: If it thunders before the Karts, if you are hasty in a business, it will be spoilt.Karte is "the particular constellation of the 27 in which the sun happens to be.” The Karte here spoken of is that in which rain should fall. If it thunders before the time, the rain will be insufficient.
Is the business of importance or are the means ?

proverb: కాలము నాటి కందిగింజ, పెద్దల నాటి పెసరగింజ.
translation: In the ages past he was Kandi and in the time of his fore-fathers he was Pesara.Said of a prosy old fellow who spins long yarns.
Kalanémi’s prayers.Kalanémi was a pretended sage. (See the Ramayana.-—Yuddha Kanda.)Applied to hypocrisy.
Burning and rubbing on the ground.Annoying and ill-treating another.
Time must be spent in one way.Change of circumstances should not change the man.
Time is afflicted, and the country is wandering [in trouble].Said jokingly by a person when asked whether he was in trouble.Désamu here means literally the country and not the inhabitants of it.

proverb: కాలికి వేస్తే మెడకు, మెడకు వేస్తే కాలికి.
translation: He removes the rope from the leg to the neck, and from the neck to the leg.Attempting to entrap a person by unfair questions.
If he points with the foot, it must be done with the head.Said of the obsequiousness necessary to a dependant.Cringing is a gainful accomplishment.
Burnt earth will not adhere to unburnt.People in different positions in life cannot be very intimate.
A half-burnt log.Said of a mean spirited creature who resents no affront.
He hops about like a cat with a burnt paw.

proverb: కాలు జారితే తీసుకోవచ్చునుగాని, నోరు జారితే తీసుకో కూడదు.
translation: If your foot slips you may recover your balance, but if your mouth slips you cannot recall your words.A slip of the foot may be soon recovered; but that of the tongue perhaps never.Better a slip of the foot than of the tongue. (French)A word and a stone once let go cannot be recalled. (Spanish)

proverb: కాలు జారి నేలనుపడి భూమి అచ్చి వచ్చినది కాదన్నట్టు.
translation: He slipped and fell, and then said the ground was unlucky.Attributing events to a wrong cause.

proverb: కాలు పట్టుకొని లాగితే చూరు పట్టుకొని వేలాడేవాడు.
translation: When you pull him out by the leg, he holds on by the eaves.A man of no delicacy of feeling, not to be got rid of easily. A. toady.
Scalding rice water to a burning (hungry) stomach.Anything is acceptable to one who is in want.Beggars must not be choosers.Hungry dogs will eat dirty puddings.A hungry ass eats any straw. (Italian)
Will a snake coiled round your leg not bite you?Said of a helpless dependant who must be supported.

proverb: కావడి యెన్ని వంకలు పోతేనేమి యిల్లు చేరితే సరి.
translation: What if the Kavadi bends ever so much! If it reaches the house, it is enough.All's well that ands well.

proverb: కాశికి పోగానే కర్రి కుక్క గంగి గోవు అవునా.
translation: Will a black dog become a holy cow by merely going to Benares ?Formal observances cannot make a sinful man holy.He that goes a beast to Rome, a beast returns. (Italian.)Send a fool to the market, and a fool he'll return.
Like going to Benares, and bringing back dog’s hair.Great labour and small results.

proverb: కాశికి పోవడము వకటి, కావడి తేవడము వకటి.
translation: Going to Benares is one thing; bringing back a Kavadi(of Ganges water) is another.Two great things to be done. Applied to keeping one great object in view.
Like going to Benares and bringing back an ass’s egg.An absurd exploit.
He was without a cash, but raised his banner for a crore.A cash (Kasu) equals about one fortieth of a penny.Penniless, but bragging of having a plum.
He has not the means of [getting] one cash, but thinks nothing of [spending] a hundred [pagodas]. A pagoda = 3 1/2 Rupees.Wanton extravagance.He is able to buy an abbey.
A ﬁne of a fanam for [the trespass of] a cow worth a cash.A fanam = 80 cash. Expense disproportionate to the value of an article.
When asked “ what?" he cannot say “whom?”He cannot say bo to a goose.
Count the disadvantages ﬁrst, then the advantages.Measure twice, cut but once.Don’t buy a pig in a poke.
Like pouring spindles into a corn measure.A noiseless operation.

proverb: కుంచెడు గింజలకు కూలికి పోతే, తూమెడు గింజలు దూడ తిన్నట్టు.
translation: While she was out Working for a kuncham of grain, the calf [at home] ate a tumu.A tumu is a measure equal to 4 kunchams.One step forwards and two backwards.
What’s the cripple’s delight is harm to the house.Such a man is said always to be in mischief.
A slip of the leg is the excuse of a lame donkey.
Until the lame bullock comes, they Won’t lift the beam.

proverb: కుండలో కూడు కూడుగానే వుండ వలె, పిల్లలు మొద్దులలాగున వుండ వలె.
translation: The food must remain undiminished in the pot, and the children must be fat and strong.To wish for two things opposed to each other.You can't eat your cake and have it too.15
A driver of horses among the pots.A man that sticks at home. A mollycoddle.
Like a lotus springing up in a chaﬁng dish.Utterly incredible.
The dog ate ﬁlth.The nature of the animal.
For the bite of a dog, a slap with a slipper is the cure.The proper punishment for a slanderer.
All the teeth that a dog gets are crooked.Said of a man who spoils everything he meddles with.

proverb: కుక్క తీశిన కొయ్యా నక్క తీశిన కొయ్యా.
translation: A stick taken by a dog, a stick taken by a jackal.No one agreeing with his neighbour.All at sixes and sevens.
All that a dog brings is ﬁlth.'Tis the nature of the beast.
Could you swim over the Godavari, by catching hold of a dog’s tail?Reliance upon mean persons for great things.Trust not to a broken staff.

proverb: కుక్కను అందలములో కూర్చుండ పెట్టితే, అమేధ్యము చూచి దిగ వురికినది.
translation: When they seated the dog in a palanquin, it saw ﬁlth and jumped down and ran to it.Mean persons although exalted will not give up their low habits.Crooked by nature is never made straight by education."Set a frog on a golden stool, and of it hops again into the pool." (German.)
Do you require a painted stick to strike a dog with?
If you beat the dog, it fouls the whole house.

proverb: కుక్కను చంపిన పాపము గుడి కట్టినా పోదు.
translation: The sin of killing a dog cannot be expiated even by building a temple.

proverb: కుక్కను పెంచితే గండాయె కూటి కుండలకెల్లా చేటాయె.
translation: If you rear up a dog, when it becomes big it will empty all your dishes.Put a snake into your bosom and when it is warm it will sting you.

proverb: కుక్కను ముద్దు పెట్టుకొంటే, మూతెల్లా తెగ నాకుతున్నది.
translation: If you kiss a dog, it licks your whole face.The result of encouraging low people.
If you poke a stick into a dog’s mouth, it will snap.A man may make his own dog bite him.

proverb: కుక్క వేషము వేస్తే, మొరగకుండా వల్ల కాదు.
translation: If you assume the disguise of a dog, you must bark.In for a penny, in for a pound.

proverb: కుక్క సంతకు పోయి తునకోల దెబ్బలు తిన్నట్టు.
translation: When the dog went to the fair he was beaten with the scale-beam.A vain fellow will be ignominiously treated.

proverb: కుట్టిన తేలు గుణవంతురాలు, కూశినమ్మ కుక్క ముండ.
translation: The scorpion which stung is a good tempered creature, the [stung] woman who bawled out is a wretched slut.A mischief maker often escapes blame.
Like enquiring the ﬂavour of the vegetables when on the point of eating them.Want of patience.

proverb: కుడుములు వండలేని ఆడదీ, కూనరాగము తియ్యలేని మొగవాడూ లేరు.
translation: There is no woman who cannot cook kudumus, nor man who cannot hum a tune.Kudumulu is a common kind of cake.Applied to things which are very common.
A man who sets ﬁre to the whole heap and begins to eat parched grain.The work of a fool.
A diamond in a dunghill.One worthy amongst many worthless.A diamond is valuable though it lie on a dunghill.
Like throwing a Gachcha nut into a potter’s kiln.If the nut of this tree be thrown into a kiln, it will burst, and break the pots.A great injury done by a slight action.
Will a brass pot be found in a potter’s kiln ?A worthy person is not to be found amongst the worthless.
The manner of the mole-cricket.Never resting from doing mischief.
Earth does not adhere to the mole-cricket.Engaging in a business but keeping clear of all responsibility.

proverb: కుమ్మరికి వక యేడు, గుదియకు వక పెట్టు.
translation: One year to the potter, one blow to the cudgel.The cudgel destroys in one blow what has cost the potter a year’s labour.The sudden loss of that gained by much labour.

proverb: కుమ్ము చెప్పుకొనేటందుకు గూడూరు పోతే, యేడు వూళ్ల కుమ్ము యెదురుగా వచ్చినది.
translation: When he went to Gudur to tell of his misfortune, the misfortune of seven villages met him.Whither goest thou, Misfortune? To where there is more. (Spanish.)

When one enquired what the ugly man was doing, [he was told that] he was counting all the good looking people.An ugly person ﬁnds fault with the looks of others.The kiln calls the oven burnt house.The shovel makes game of the poker. (French.)The pan says to the pot "keep off or you’ll smutch me.” (Italian)
A monkey [-like wife] of good family is better [than a pretty wife of low origin].
[Form] your connexions when you know the caste, [choose] your residence when you know the place.
Though [a husband] be low in rank, he should not be younger than his wife. (Or, she should not be without one?)

proverb: కూచమ్మ కూడ బెట్టితే, మాచమ్మ మాయము చేశినది.
translation: Machamma made away with what Kuchamma gathered.
Though a man be poor, will he lose caste?

proverb: కూడూ గుడ్డా అడగకపోతే, బిడ్డను సాకినట్టు సాకుతాను అన్నాడట.
translation: If you don't ask me for food and raiment I will care for you as my own child.Good words and no deeds are rushes and reeds.Fair words butter no parsnips.
As for food I have it, but I came here from fear of the caste people.Said by an idle beggar, as an excuse for asking alms.
A karanam to talk.A great talker but not good at his work.
Great cries, but not a grain in the heap.A great noise of threshing, but no grain to thresh."Great cry and little wool” as the fellow said when he sheared the pig.

proverb: కూతుండ్ల కన్నమ్మకు కురిచీపీటలు, కొడుకుల కన్నమ్మకు గోడ పంచలు.
translation: The lady who has daughters is given a chair, but the lady who has sons has to lean against the wall.
When the mother gave her daughter a kuncham of grain, the daughter set before her mother a dishful of food.Kindness returned.One never loscth by doing good turns.Kindness produces kindness. (Latin.)

proverb: కూతురికి మంగళవారం శుక్రవారం, కోడలికి దియ్యలు దియ్యలు.
translation: [I only anoint] my daughter on Tuesdays and Fridays, but [I anoint] my daughter-in-law every Diyyalu.A catch. Diyyalu or Dipavali is a feast held on the 14th day of the dark fortnight in the month Asvayuja (October-November) on which occasion anointing the body with oil is indispensable. The "every Dipavali” of the mother-in—law therefore only amounted to once a year, but she took better care of her own daughter.

proverb: కూన అని పెంచితే, గండు అయి కరవ వచ్చినది.
translation: He petted it as a kitten, but when it grew into a big cat it tried to bite him.Evil men will injure their protectors.Foster a raven and it will peck out your eyes. (Spanish)
Is an antelope equal to a tiger’s cub?

proverb: కూనలమ్మ సంగీతము వింటే పుణ్యము లేదు, వినకుంటే పాపము లేదు.
translation: If you listen to the song [in praise] of Kunalamma, there is no merit; and if you don’t listen to it, there is no sin.Kunalamma is the goddess said to preside over children.
One must sit ﬁrst, and then lie down.Said to a. man inclined to do a thing hastily.First creep, then go.

proverb: కూర్చుండి లేవలేడు గాని, యెగసి తాటికాయలు తన్నుతానన్నాడట
translation: He could not stand up, yet he talked of jumping up and kicking down a palmyra fruit.Vain boasting.
He could not rise from his seat and yet he proposed to crawl to the holy place.Offering to do that which is difficult, when unable to do that which is easy.16
He came for hire, and asked for a share [in the business].A cool request.

proverb: కూశే గాడిదె వచ్చి మేశే గాడిదెను చెరిపినది.
translation: The braying donkey came and spoiled the grazing donkey’s business.When the one that was outside brayed, the other that was inside answered and was consequently discovered and driven out of the ﬁeld.
Good done to an ungrateful person.
Why a mountain of ﬁre to [burn down] a mountain of cotton?A little ﬁre burn: up a great deal of corn.

proverb: కొండంత దేవుణ్ని కొండంత పత్రితో పూజ చేస్తారా.
translation: Do they offer to a god as great as a mountain, leaves and flowers as much as a mountain?A worshipper is accepted according to his faith, not according to greatness of his offering.

proverb: కొండంత రెడ్డే పోగా పిడికెడు బొచ్చుకు యేడ్చినట్టు.
translation: When the big Reddi died they wept only for the handful of his hair.Indifferent to a great loss, but lamenting over some triﬂe that went with it.Fear not the loss of the bell more than the loss of the steeple.

proverb: కొండకు వొక వెంట్రుక ముడి వేస్తే, వస్తే కొండే వస్తున్నది, పోతే వెంట్రుకే పోతున్నది.
translation: If you tie a hair to a mountain, the mountain will come or the hair only go.The possibility of great gain with the risk of little loss.
Like putting a mountain under one’s head and searching for stones.
Digging up a mountain to catch a rat.
Like a ram butting a mountain.Great conceit.
When he applied medicine to the uvula, it took out the whole tongue.The remedy is worse than the disease.
Like dogs barking at a mountain.

As the hill-barber shaves.The pilgrims who visit the sacred hill of Tirupati have to be shaved before entering the temple. The barbers in their greediness to secure customers are said to keep numbers round them half shaven, leaving off one to commence another.Dabbling in many things, but completing none.
Will a man that swallows a mountain care for a Gopuram?Gopuram is the tower over the gate of a Hindu temple.

proverb: కొండ మీద దెబగుబలేమంటే, కోమట్ల రహస్యాలు అన్నట్టు.
translation: When asked what the row on the hill was, they said “ The Komatis’ secrets.”The secret of Anchuelos. (spanish)
How many are the wounds of a man who has fallen from the top of a hill?A man inured to diﬂiculties.
Like a sickle carried in the waist of a man climbing up a hill.An additional danger and difﬁculty.

proverb: కొండ వలె వచ్చి మంచు వలె తేలినట్టు
translation: Coming like a mountain, and melting away like dew.Said of a difficulty which appears great when distant but is easily overcome when grappled with.
Like a well-rope at Kondavidu.Where water is very scarce and the wells very deep.Said of a long-winded story.

proverb: కొంప తీస్తివా రామన్నా ఆంటే, అందుకు సందేహమా అన్నట్టు.
translation: “Have you ruined me Ramanna?” [said a man to his parrot] “Is there any doubt of that?” it replied.A man was duped into buying a parrot by the seller pretending it had power to discover hidden treasure. He pointed to a place where money had been previously buried and enquired of the parrot, which answered "Is there any doubt of that?" the only sentence it had been taught to repeat. The purchaser having bought the parrot for a large sum, in vain endeavoured to cause it to discover treasure. At last in his disappointment he cried "Have you ruined me Ramanna?" when he received the same answer " Is there any doubt of that?"Applied to a person who knowingly gives bad advice to others, and then laughs at their misfortunes.

“Why do you cry before being beaten?” he asked, “you are going to beat me in future” replied [the boy].Never yowl till you’re hit.Let your trouble tarry till its own day comes.Sufficient for the day is the evil thereof.

proverb: కొడుకు బాగుండ వలె, కోడలు ముండ మొయ్య వలె.
translation: The son must ﬂourish, and the daughter-in-law must become a Widow.
Like a gad-ﬂy ﬂying into a new pot.The pot contains nothing, and the mouth being narrow, the ﬂy has some diﬂlculty in getting out again.

proverb: కొత్త నీరు వచ్చి పాత నీరు కొట్టుకొని పోయినది.
translation: The new water came and washed away the old water.A little gain once was the cause of all being lost eventually.
Like ﬁsh going against the stream.Undertaking a diﬂicult task.It is hard to swim against the stream. (Dutch)
A new beggar knows not the time [to ask for alms]
Fresh [news is heard with] pleasure, stale [news with] disgust.
Will the bad bargain be improved by receiving something over?
The man that ate, was better off than the man that bought the things [and did not use them].
He that ate is he that boughtIf you want any thing, you must pay for it.Nothing is had for nothing. (French.)

proverb: కొబ్బరి చెట్టు యెందుకు యెక్కుతావురా అంటే, దూడ గడ్డికోసరము యెక్కుతానన్నట్టు.
translation: Like a man saying, when asked why he was getting up the cocoanut tree, that he wanted grass for his calf.An absurd reason.
Like scratching one’s head with a firebrand.Applied to the use of bad agents.

Nakkakorra to KorraNakkakorra is the Panicum Helvolum, an inferior grain produced in the hill countries. Korra is the Setaria Italica, a "farinaceous grain of the millet kind" (Shakespear’s Hind. Diety. under lllj).Thwarting the evil designs of a bad man by means of a worse.Devils must be driven out with devils. (German.)
The man who measures [the grain] is blind, and the man who has had it measured is blind also (i.e., the buyer and the seller.)Defects on both sides.

Soon after the destruction of Kollu by a ﬂood, Kona also was swept away.Kollu and Kona are the names of two villages on either side of a river. The calamity which happened to one was a source of rejoicing to the other, which soon however suffered the same fate.Tauntingly used by a person towards those who took delight in his misfortune but have now been overtaken by a calamity themselves.

proverb: కొల్లేట్లో పండే పంటేగాని, చచ్చే దున్నపోతులకు లెక్క లేదు
translation: [They look at] the crops which grow in Kolléru, but there is no account of the buffaloes which die there.Counting proﬁt, but not loss.
There is no taste in the greens unless some are received over.Said by Hindu women, who are never satisﬁed unless they obtain something over and above their money’s worth.
Ten million arts are only for [getting] food.All professions are only so many means of livelihood.
The mother-in-law gave good counsel to her daughter-in-law and went astray herself.

proverb: కోడలు కొడుకును కంటానంటే, వద్దనే అత్తగారు ఉన్నదా.
translation: When a daughter-in-law says that she is about to bring forth a son, is there any mother-in-law who says “Don’t”? i.e. wish the child to be a daughter.Will any one refuse to receive a beneﬁt ?
The daughter-in-law enters the house (as a bride) and the mother-in law enters the Ganges (dies).Death at the tae door an’ heirship at the tither. (Scotch.)

proverb: కోడికి కులాసం లేదు, కోమటికి విశ్వాసం ఉంది.
translation: A fowl has no happiness and a Komati has feeling.The Komatis are a class of shopkeepers.
Do you want a stone roller to break an egg with?
Sacriﬁcing a pig to save a chicken.
When the fowl ﬂew, it only mounted a branch.17
Like showing a looking glass to a monkey.Persistently offering that which is disliked.
Like a monkey with a cocoanut.Cannot use it, but won't give it up.Like the dog in the manger, he will neither do nor let do.
Like putting a gold-laced cap on a monkey’s head.
Like a snake in a monkey’s paw.Jacko is afraid of it, but won't let it go.
A garland of ﬂowers in a monkey’s paw.(800 Nos. 574, 712.)A good thing thrown away on a stupid person.
A monkey’s ﬁst.Not letting go any thing in its grasp.Applied to obstinacy.
A sore is a she-demon to a monkeyMaking a great fuss about nothing.
Like the monkey and the Guruvinda.The seed of the Guruvinda (Abrus Precatorius) is a small pretty black and red seed.
[Placing] a guard over a forest.Attempting impossibilities.
A Komati’s truth.
A Komati is a coward; if you hit him, he runs away.
Like the burning of a Komati’s house.A heavy loss.
The faith of a Komati.Faithlessness.

A Komati’s evidence.A story is told of a Komati who, when asked to identify a horse about which a Musalman and Hindu were quarrelling, said the forepart of it looked like the Musalman's horse and the hind part like the Hindu’s.
A blow without a rod, a bond without a rope.A sudden and unaccountable calamity.
Like the wings of fowls, and the ﬂower-stalks of palmyra trees.Domestic fowls make no use of their wings, and the ﬂower-stalk (spadix) of the palmyra  bears no fruit.Useless possessions.

proverb: కోళ్ల బేరానకు వెళ్లి కోటలో కబుర్లు అడిగినట్టు.
translation: Going to sell fowls and asking the news in the fort.A meddling rascal.
When the cuckoo ﬂew it only mounted a branch.
He makes the great, mean; and the mean, great.The power of God.
A blow without a rod, a bond without a rope.A sudden and unaccountable calamity.
Like the wings of fowls, and the ﬂower-stalks of palmyra trees.Domestic fowls make no use of their wings, and the ﬂower-stalk (spadix) of the palmyra  bears no fruit.Useless possessions.

proverb: కోళ్ల బేరానకు వెళ్లి కోటలో కబుర్లు అడిగినట్టు.
translation: Going to sell fowls and asking the news in the fort.A meddling rascal.
When the cuckoo ﬂew it only mounted a branch.
He makes the great, mean; and the mean, great.The power of God.
Like packsaddle, like quilt.Like pot, like cover. (Dutch.)
Like blowing a horn into perfumed powder.Gandhapodi is thrown over one another by the relations of the bride and bridegroom at Hindu marriages.
It is not the basket, but the hook.Galapu Sidi is the hook used in the swinging festival (Charak Puja) and Gampa Sidi is a basket sometimes used instead of the hook.Applied to a very difficult task.

proverb: గచ్చ పొద మీద యిసుక వేసి కయ్యానికి పిలుస్తున్నది.
translation: Throwing sand on a thorn bush and asking it to ﬁght.Done by a Xantippe who could get no one to quarrel with.
Like a ﬁght between an elephant and a tortoise.
He is a clever man no doubt, but his belly is hollow.An ironical phrase.The word rendered clever also means “solid.” The expression his belly is hollow is equivalent to “empty-headed."
Like leaving the grain, and ﬁghting for the husk.
Like ﬁghting with the ferry-man after getting over the river.The river past, the saint forgotten. (Spanish)The peril past, the saint mocked. (Italian)
My beard will be ﬁt for shaving.Said by a goat to a lion which he threatened to devour to fulﬁl a vow.

proverb: గడ్డ తిన్నా కంపే, పాయ తిన్నా కంపే.
translation: If you eat the root [of garlick] there is a smell, if you eat a clove of it there is a smellWhether you do a little evil or much, it is alike bad. ​

proverb: గడ్డపలుగులు గాలికి కొట్టుకొనిపోతూ వుంటే, పుల్లాకు నా పని యేమి అన్నదట.
translation: When the crowbars were blown about by the wind, the leaf platter said “What is to become of me?”Pullaku is a cast away leaf-dish which has been once used.A mean person thinking of his own loss when his superiors are suffering.
Swallowing crowbars and taking ginger draughts.An insufficient remedy.

proverb: గడ్డము కాలి వొకడు యేడుస్తూ వుంటే, చుట్టకు నిప్పు యిమ్మని వొకడు వెంబడించినట్టు.
translation: When one man was crying out that his board was on ﬁre another followed him asking him for a light for his cigar.If my beard is burnt, others try to light their pipes at it. (Turkish.)¤
Rice water is a rich drink to a poor lady.Panakam is a rich beverage made of expensive materials.
A helpless woman will get a foolish husband. ​
Like the bat which thinks it holds up the sky and keeps it from falling.Said of a man who thinks everything depends on himself.

proverb: గరుడాయ లెస్సా అంటే, శేషాయ లెస్సా అన్నట్టు.
translation: “How are you Garuda?” said the snake Sésha, “How are you Sésha?” replied the kite.Sésha took advantage of his position on the wrist of Siva to address Garuda, the sacred kite of Vishnu, in an impertinently familiar manner, to which Garuda was compelled to submit.A mean person taking advantage of his inﬂuence with a great man, to treat his betters with insolence.
The oil-crusher’s greediness cannot be contained even in a sack.But little oil is extracted from a large quantity of seed.
Like using a rice-pounder for [carrying] a bunch of glass bracelets.When put down, the weight of the stick would break the bracelets.A foolish action. ​
By trading in glass bracelets a bare subsistence only can be obtained.Because so many break.Applied to any business which is attended with heavy losses.
Like saying that a donkey has eaten a basketful of husk.Nothing wonderful.
A donkey’s row.A noisy brawl.Nothing passes between asses but kicks. (Italian)
The mildness of a young donkey.Seemingly good but really useless.
Ashes are the remedy for a deep ulcer.Severe measures must be used with the incorrigible.Desperate ills require desperate remedies. (French)

proverb: గాదెకింది యెలుక గాదెకింద బ్రతుక వలెనుగాని, మరియెక్కడ బ్రతుకుతుంది.
translation: The rat below the corn bin must live below the corn bin, where else can it live ?Said of an idle parasite. ​

proverb: గాదెనిండా బియ్యము వుంటే, కరువునా కాలానా నా భార్యనూ పిల్లలనూ నేనే రక్షిస్తానన్నాడట.
translation: He said that he himself would support his wife and family in dearth and in plenty, so long as the bin was full of corn.

proverb: గానుగ రోట్లో చెయ్యి పెట్టి పెరుమాళ్ల కృప అన్నట్టు.
translation: Putting your hand into an oil press, and saying “The favour of Perumal (Vishnu ) be upon me”Tempting Providence.Put your ﬁnger in the fire, and say ‘test your ill fortune.

proverb: గానుగాపే గానుగాపే నీవు కూర్చున్న పసేగదాఅ నూనె పొయ్యడం.
translation: O oil-woman! oil-woman! you pour out the oil as ﬁnely as you sit.Said to a cheat.
As though possessed of a devil.

proverb: గారాబము గార్లెకు యేడిస్తే, వీపు దెబ్బ్బలకు యేడుస్తున్నది.
translation: You are teazing me and crying for cakes and your back is crying for a whipping.Said by a father.
Born of the wind, and reared up by the dust.Said by parents to an ungrateful son, or by a benefactor to an ungrateful dependant. ​
Offering to God the ﬂour which had been blown away by the wind.Let that which is lost be for God.What the abbot of Bamba cannot eat, he gives away for the good of his soul. (spanish.)

proverb: గాలిలో దీపము బెట్టి దేవుడా నీ మహిమ చూపుమన్నట్టు.
translation: Having put the lamp in the wind, he prays “O God! show thy power.”
When a storm comes, the story ceases.A sudden stop to anything.
Three bushels of grain for twelve bushels of birds.

If you hide the grinding stone will the daughter’s marriage be stopped??A round grinding stone or pestle is used in marriage ceremonies.A triﬂing hindrance put in a man's way will not divert him from his purpose.
Bullets ﬂoating, Bendu sinking.Bendu is the Eschynornene Indica from which pith hats, models, &c. are made.The order of nature reversed. ​
The bite of the temple-snake, the goring of the sacred cow.An injury is none the less, though it be inﬂicted by a. relation or friend.
To him who swallows the temple, the lingam in it is a sugar-plum.
If the temple bell be lost what does it matter to the priest ling?A hireling cares not for the property of his master.
Like a temple coming and falling on you.An unexpected calamity.
A squint eye is better than a blind eye.Of two evils choose the least.

proverb: గుడ్డి కన్ను మూశినా వకటే తెరిచినా వకటే.
translation: It matters not whether the blind eye is open or shut.A useless man's absence is as good as his presence.
He is cleaning the teeth of a blind horse.Unproﬁtnble employment.He is teaching a pig to play on a ﬂute. ​
Like the blind jackal falling into the snare.

proverb: గుడ్డి మామగారా నమస్కారమయ్యా అంటే, రంధికి మూలమా రంకుల కోడలా అన్నాడట.
translation: When she said “I salute you, O blind father-in-law !” he replied “ Is this the commencement of strife? O adulterous daughter-in-law !”Let him that has a glass skull not take to stone throwing. (Italian)
Like a blind bullock going into a ﬁeld of millet.Not able to get much out of it.

Can a blind man discern the colour of pure gold?An uneducated man cannot judge of the attainments of the learned.A blind man is no judge of colours. (Italian)A pebble and a diamond are alike to a blind man.
The egg made faces at the chicken.Mocking and mimicking a superior.

proverb: గుణము మానవే గూటాల పోలీ అంటే, నా మనువయినా మానుతానుగాని నా గుణము మానను అన్నదట.
translation: When Gutala Poli was told to amend her ways, she said she would rather break off the match.Obstinnte behaviour. Stubborn conduct. ​

proverb: గుమ్మడికాయ పొయ్యే దారి యెరగడు, ఆవగింజ పొయ్యే దారి అట్టే పట్టి చూస్తాడు.
translation: He does not know the way the pumpkin goes, but troubles himself to ﬁnd the way the mustard seed goes.

proverb: గుమ్మడి కాయల దొంగ అంటే, తన బుజాలు తానే పట్టి చూసుకొన్నాడట.
translation: When the thief who stole the pumpkin was spoken of, he felt his shoulders.Thinking some mark might have been left.A guilty conscience needs no accuser.He that has a mucklc nose thinks ilka ane is speaking o't. (Scotch)
The pumpkins have become rotten, the mustard plants have spread.Said when the base and wicked rise in power and the great and good disappear.

proverb: గురువింద గింజ తన యెరుపేగాని ముడ్డి కింది నలుపు యెరగదు.
translation: The Guruvinda seed knows its redness, but not the blackness of its lower part.A man values himself highly, but does not know his own defects.No one sees his own faults. (German.)
To put Panganamams on your Guru.To outwit him.Panganamam is a very large Namam, the sectarial mark worn on the forehead by Vaishnavas. ​
A disciple greater than his Guru.Said of an insolent fellow.
May a man ﬁght with his Guru?
You should not transgress the commandment of your Guru, nor swallow a crowbar.The ﬁrst is as bad for you as the second.
Although the horse be blind, he won’t eat less corn.Supporting a useless person.A bad horse eats as much as a good one. (Dutch.)

proverb: గుర్రము చచ్చినదిగాక గుంట తవ్వ వొక రూక.
translation: In addition to the horse’s death, a fanam [must be paid] for digging a pit [to bury it in].Loss upon loss.After one loss come many. (French)
How can a dog which has eaten a horse live?

proverb: గుర్రము వలె కుక్కను పెంచి రెడ్డి తానే మొరిగినాడట.
translation: The Reddi fed his dog like a horse, and barked himself.To pay a. servant extravagantly and do his work yourself. ​
Must one teach a horse to eat boiled gram ?Teach your grandmother to suck eggs.

proverb: గుర్రానికి తోక వుంటే తానే విసురుకొంటుందిగాని, సావిట్లో వుండే గుర్రాలకన్నిటికీ విసురుతున్నదా.
translation: If the horse has a tail, it drives away its own ﬂies; does it drive away the ﬂies from all the horses in the stable ?Said when a selﬁsh person is exalted.An ill man in oﬂice is a mischief to the public.

proverb: గుళ్లో దేవునికి నైవేద్యము లేకుంటే, పూజారి పుళిహోరకు యేడ్చినాడట
translation: While the idol in the temple was in want of Naivédyam, the priest cried for Pulihora.Pulihora is a more expensive oﬂ‘ering prepared with acid.
The owl took to its nest, the grandmother took to her bed.
A humpbacked man with convulsions.(Sec Nos. 1755, 1830.)
The humpback alone knows how he can lie comfortably.Every man knows but where his own shoe pinches. (Scotch)

proverb: గృహప్రవేశముకు వెళ్లుతూ గుడ్ల గూబను తోడుతీసుకొని పోయినట్టు.
translation: Like taking an owl with you to the ceremony of entering a a new house.Grihapravesa is a ceremony only to be performed at an auspicious hour and in the absence of every bad omen. The owl is a peculiarly bad omen.Keeping company with your ill-wishers.
Will a cut-throat forget his knife ?
Like buying leather from a shoemaker.Purchasing at unreasonable prices. The shoemaker buys his leather to make up, not to retail to others.

proverb: గొడ్ల కాచేవాణ్ని కొట్టనివాడున్ను, గొర్రెల కాచేవాణ్ని తిట్టనివాడున్ను లేడు.
translation: There is no person who has not struck a herdsman, or abused a shepherdDespised people.
What does the barren woman know of the pains of child birth ?
What does the barren woman know of the pleasure of having children ?He who has no children knows not what is love. (Italian)19

proverb: గొడ్డువాడు గొడ్డుకు యేడిస్తే, గొడారివాడు తోలుకు యేడ్చినాడు.
translation: When the owner cried for the cow [he had lost,] the shoe maker cried for the hide.It is an ill wind that blows nobody good.
After shaving, all that is left is the top-lock.To sponge on another until he has nothing left.After shaving there is nothing to shear. (Italian)

If the sheep gets fat, it is for the beneﬁt of the shepherd.If the servant does in a huff more work than he is told, it is for the benefit of his master. The term translated gets fat also signiﬁes “becomes proud.”
The sheep only trusts him who cuts its throat.The simple and unwary only trust their deceivers.

proverb: గొర్రెను తినేవాడు పోతే, బర్రెను తినేవాడు వచ్చినట్టు.
translation: When the man who ate sheep went, a man who ate buffalos came.Parting with one rascal and getting a greater scoundrel in his place.
Like a wolf rushing in among sheep.Easy prey.

proverb: గొల్లవాడి కొమ్ము యెచ్చనూ యెచ్చదు, తగ్గనూ తగ్గదు
translation: The shepherd’s Kavadi neither rises nor sinks.Because he does not swing his pots on each end.The fortunes of a sheep-farmer are subject to sudden reverses.Applied to a precarious mode of living.
The morning dawns before the shepherds’ marriage is begun.They have so many disputes that nothing can be settled.
Like mixing unripe tamarinds with Gogu greens.Gogu or Gongura is the Hibiscus Cannabinus.A combination of evil persons.
Too big for a clout, too small for a body-cloth.Neither one thing nor the other.Too little and too much spoils everything. (Danish)Not too little, not too much. (German)
Mr. Clout-Ragamuffin is the robbers’ master.He that has nothing is frighted at nothing.There is no stripping a naked man. (German)

proverb: గోట మీటితే పొయ్యే పనికి గొడ్డలి యెందుకు.
translation: Why an axe for, that which can be done with a fillip of the nail?Unnecessary labour.

proverb: గోడకు పెట్టిన సున్నమూ, లంజకు పెటిన సొమ్మూ.
translation: Lime put on a wall, money given to a harlot.No return.
A slap in the face for knocking one’s head against the wall.Misfortunes seldom come alone.

proverb: గోడ మీది సున్నము విడయములోకి పనికి వచ్చునా.
translation: Will the chunam on the wall be ﬁt for betel leaf ?A little ﬁne chunam (lime) is commonly eaten with betel.Said of a man who does not serve his friends.
If there is a wall you can draw on it.
There are more small sacks than big ones.
When it is as small as a ﬁnger nail, he makes a mountain of it.To make a mountain of a mole-hill.
Like a jackal at a tombA tantalizing position, as he cannot get into it.Like a cat round hot milk.
A blow with a pestle on a whitlow.
Like killing cows and giving away sandals [made of the hide.]Steal the goose, and give the giblets in alms.
A barren buffalo is the emblem of Maha Lakshmi, in a village where there are no cows.Maha Lakshmi is the goddess of fortune.The one-eyed is a king in the land of the blind.
[Shaving] a bald head, to [propitiate] the village goddess.No other will submit to the ignominy.
Work Without pay.Work not paid for is ill done.Work done expects money. (Portuguese)

Like attempting to teach Ghantakarna the Ashtakshari.Ghantakarna ( Bell-eared) is the name of an attendant on Siva.Ashtakshari is a sacred formula used in the worship of Vishnu, composed, as the word denotes, of eight syllables (ఓం నమోనారాయణాయ.)

proverb: చంకపిల్ల జారిపడేటట్టు మాట్లాడుతాడు.He speaks so as [to cause one] to let the child drop from the arms and fall.A deceiver.A honey tongue, a heart of gall.Bees that have honey in their mouths have stings in their tails.

translation: Looking for bones in a Woman’s breast.A minute investigation.

proverb: చక్కగా కూకోరా చాకలినాయడా అంటే, విన్నావటోయి యీడిగనాయడా మంగలినాయడి సరసము అన్నాడట.
translation: “Sit properly Mr. Washerman” said one, “Mr. Toddy-drawer, do you hear Mr. Barber's joke ?” said the other.Chaffing between Satanis, who are Vaishnava converts from all castes.

Like attempting to teach Ghantakarna the Ashtakshari.Ghantakarna ( Bell-eared) is the name of an attendant on Siva.Ashtakshari is a sacred formula used in the worship of Vishnu, composed, as the word denotes, of eight syllables (ఓం నమోనారాయణాయ.)

proverb: చంకపిల్ల జారిపడేటట్టు మాట్లాడుతాడు.He speaks so as [to cause one] to let the child drop from the arms and fall.A deceiver.A honey tongue, a heart of gall.Bees that have honey in their mouths have stings in their tails.

translation: Looking for bones in a Woman’s breast.A minute investigation.

proverb: చక్కగా కూకోరా చాకలినాయడా అంటే, విన్నావటోయి యీడిగనాయడా మంగలినాయడి సరసము అన్నాడట.
translation: “Sit properly Mr. Washerman” said one, “Mr. Toddy-drawer, do you hear Mr. Barber's joke ?” said the other.Chaffing between Satanis, who are Vaishnava converts from all castes.
For beauty, a camel; for singing, an ass.

proverb: చక్కిలాలు తింటావా, చల్ది తింటావా అంటే, చక్కిలాలూ తింటాను, చల్దీ తింటాను, అయ్యతోటి వేడీ తింటాను అన్నాడట.
translation: When [the guest] was asked whether he would take biscuits, or cold breakfast, he replied that he would take both, and hot breakfast too with the good man of the house.Applied to an ill mannered glutton.The first cut, and all the loaf besides.
Poison covered with sugar.A ﬂattering speech is honied poison. (Latin)Sugared words generally prove bitter(Spanish)
The milk given by the streaked goat which is dead ﬁlled the lost pot with a piece out.
A dead man's eyes are very broad.Lavishing praises on the dead which were not bestowed on the living.

Any quantity of presents must suﬂice at a dead man’s marriage.When a marriage takes place, presents of cloths are sent to the bride and bridegroom by their relations. Return gifts of about equal value are made shortly after the marriage. Should the bridegroom, however, die during theceremonies, no presents are returned, and no complaints are therefore made regarding the value of those received.
If you live until I die, I’ll get you married.A jocular saying.
The sea to a desperate man is as [shallow water only up to] his knees.Men in despair fear nothing.Despair gives courage to a coward.

proverb: చదవ నేర్తువా వ్రాయ నేర్తువా అంటే, చదవా వ్రాయా నేరనుగాని చించ నేర్తును అన్నాడట.
translation: When asked whether he could read and write, he said, “I can’t read or write, but I can tear.”
When set to read, the [little] sense he had left him.Making bad worse.John has been to school to learn to be afaol. (French.)

A washerman is better than an educated person.The washerman, though illiterate, invariably distinguishes the clothes belonging to different people by putting certain marks on them, but many of the educated cannot discern between good and evil.
He reads the Ramayana, and knocks down temples.Much religion, but no goodness.

proverb: చదువు మా యింట లేదు, సంధ్య మా వంశాన లేదు.
translation: There is no learning in my house, nor performance of Sandhya in my family.Said by an uneducated and irreligious Brahman.
The best part of the cold food is the pickle.
The wife who has eaten breakfast [at home] does not think of her husband’s hunger.Little knows the fat sow what the lean one means.The full belly does not believe in hunger. (Italian.)
O Brahman! if you have brought food, eat it.Said to a selﬁsh man who bores others by talking everlastingly of his own concerns.20

Even cold Water should be drunk slowly.There is a pun here on the word చల్లార్చుకొని which also means “having cooled.”Nothing should be done hastily.Good and quickly seldom meet.Haste trips up its own heels.
Like making holes in the pots in a water-shed.The water-shed in India corresponds to the drinking-fountain in Europe.A despicable trick.
Like throwing stones at the pots in a water-shed.
Coming to beg buttermilk and hiding the cup.Pride with poverty.Pride and poverty are ill met yet often together.
When cheap it comes to the bazar.
Cheapness causes extravagance.A good bargain is a pick purse.What is not needed is dear at a farthinng. (Latin)

proverb: చస్తానని చద్ది అన్నము తింటే, చల్లగా నిద్ర వచ్చినదట.
translation: Saying she would die ( kill herself) she made a great break-fast and had a ﬁne sleep.A trick to get a good meal, as she said it was to be her last.
When one says “cha” (and), he cannot answer “ tu” (but).
A washerwoman of a mother-in-law, a barber of a father-in-law.A man abusing his wife’s parents.
Like the barber’s giving a written divorcement to the washerwoman’s wife.b

Although the mat be torn, will there not be found in it as much as a school boy’s mat ?Cadaru is a very small square mat.Every thing can be brought to some account.Nothing so bad as not to be good for something.

proverb: చాలీ చాలనందుకు చాకింట గుడ్డలు శానా వున్నవి.
translation: If you have not quite clothes enough, there are plenty at the washennan’s.Alluding to the habit which obtains among the natives of India of hiring other people’s clothes from the washermen.

proverb: చావటి ముందరి కొంప కదపా పుల్లలకు సరి.
translation: A thatched but opposite a Court-house, is used up for bonds.Bonds were formerly always written on Palmyra leaves, such as are used for thatching.
He would neither die not give up his bed.
He will neither allow me to die nor to live.Perpetual worrying.
He won’t fast unless told that his life is in danger.
Does death come more than once ?
Escaping death, but losing an eye.
The tamarind may be dried, but it loses not its acidity.Retaining vigour of mind though weakened in body.
The growth of the tamarind and the burgrass.The tamarind grows slowly, the Chigirinta (Panicum Verticillatum) shoots up very rapidly and soon withers.Slow and sure.
Grass-like prosperity.
It is better to go and mock another, than to whine when you are caught.

proverb: చిచ్చాయ చిచ్చాయ అంటే, సందాయె సందాయె అన్నట్టు.
translation: One cried out “Fire! Fire!” and another “ Now’s the time! Now's the time!”The cry of Dvarapudi robbers on a certain occasion when they had set ﬁre to a village, which they had long been unable to plunder on account of the wariness of the inhabitants.

proverb: చిత్త చిత్తగించి స్వాతి దయచేశి విశాఖ విశిరి కొట్టకుంటే, వీసానికి పుట్టెడు పండుతాను అన్నట్టు.
translation: “ If Chitta is kind, if Svati shows favor, and if Visakha does not blow too much, I will produce a Putti [of corn] to a Visam [of land]” said [the ﬁeld].Chitta and Swati are the 14th and 15th lunar mansions, in which rain is expected. Visఅkha is the 16th lunar mansion in which high winds are common.—Putti is a corn measure of 500lbs. Visam is the fraction 1/16 —here the 16th part of a Gunta, a land measure equal to 1/40 of an acre.Calm weather in June sets corn in tune.A shower in July, when the corn begin: to ﬁll,Is worth a plough of oxen, and all belong: there till.Dry August and warm doth harvest no harm.
He has a mind to worship S'iva, but his thoughts are on his shoes (left outside the temple).

If China Péri’s marriage-cord be broken, Peda Péri's will be broken at the same time.The Tadu is the cord tied round a bride’s neck. To it is attached the gold Talibottu, answering to the wedding ring. When a woman becomes a widow the cord is broken.Said by one fellow wife to the other.Both in the same boat.

proverb: చిన్నక్క చిలక, పెద్దక్క గిలక, చూస్తే చుక్క, రేగితే కుక్క.
translation: The younger sister is a parrot, the elder sister is a rattle; when you look at them they are like stars; when angry they are like dogs.
Though the snake be small, you must hit him with a big stick.

proverb: చిన్నమూ కావలె, చిదరా కావలె, మేలిమీ కావలె, మెడ తిరగవలె.
translation: The ornament must be one Chinnam or less in weight, of pure gold, and must go round the neck.A Chinnam is a weight equal to the ninth part of a pagoda .Wishing to get a good thing and to pay little for it.

proverb: చిలక తన ముద్దేగాని యెదటి ముద్దు కోరదు.
translation: A parrot likes to be petted itself, but not to see others petted.Applied to a selfish person.
Like a Donda fruit in a parrot’s beak.
When the “tin” goes, household broils disappear.Chilumu' is properly rust on other metals than iron; it is a slang term for money.
The name of a hole is a hole.Said of persons contradicting each other, though really driving at the same end.
A brilliant gem in the darkness.A bright exception among a bad lot.
A silk tassel to a broom.The grand appearance of a worthless person.A leaden sword in an ivory scabbard.

proverb: చీర కట్టినమ్మ శృంగారము చూడు, గుడ్డ కట్టినమ్మ కులుకు చూడు.
translation: Look at the ﬁnery of the well dressed and the strut of the poorly dressed woman.
A white louse with the staggers.The Hindus’ cure for the staggers is branding the ﬂanks and legs with a ploughshare.Applied to measures quite out of proportion ;to the necessities of the case.
By the time he had ﬁnished equipping himself, the whole town was plundered.Procrastination on the part of a boaster to conceal his cowardice or ignorance.
When he paid a visit to his relations, they all caught hold of him like devils.To get something out of him.
The eaves are all in good order, and there is a joyful song in the court.Expressive of prosperity.
What he saw was a snake, that which hit him was a mango stone.After seeing the snake, he had trodden on a split mango stone which nipped his toe.Flabbergasted with fright.
Every thing that is seen [is subjected to the payment of] duty; and every thing that is rotten [is thrown away in the] mud.Excessive demands.
As he looked and looked the horse turned out a donkey.By close inspection the true state is known.

To look at, it is like a musk rat, but to dig into walls it is like a bandicoot.Insigniﬁcant in appearance, but having the power of doing much harm.Little enemies and little wounds are not to be despised. (German.)
No relation to visit or god to worship.Utterly destitute.
If you visit them they are relatives, if you invite them to visit you they are angry.21

What is Friday to visitors ?If a married woman goes away from a house on Friday, the Hindus any that the goddess of fortune quits the house with her. The above is an excuse made by a casual visitor, who wished to get away.Lame excuses.

proverb: చెంబూ కంచమూ పోతే, ముఖము మీద కొట్టినట్టు ముంతా మూకుడూ తెచ్చుకో లేనా.
translation: If I lose my brass pot and bell metal dish, can I not beat you by getting others made of earth ?A determined man will not be discouraged by diﬂiculties.

proverb: చెట్టు అయి వంగనిది మాను అయి వంగునా.
translation: If a tree won’t bend when it is young, will it bend when it is full grown ?If a man is not humble in his youth, will he grow meek in his old age ?Bend the tree while it is young. (Italian.)The old branch breaks bent. (Dutch.)"Bow down his neck while he is young.” Ecclesiasticus xxx. 12.
Cutting a tree and letting it fall on one’s self.Bringing trouble on one’s own head.The fool hunts for misfortune. (French.)

proverb: చెట్టు చెడే కాలానకు కుక్క మూతి పిందెలు పుట్టుతున్నవి.
translation: When the tree is about to perish, it brings forth blasted fruit.Applied to the ﬁrst signs of the coming ruin of a bad man.Coming events cast their shadows before them.
Will he who planted the tree not water it ?Will God not support the creatures of his hand?Said by a man who has lost his means of support.God never sends mouths but he sends meat.
Like asking whether the tree was ﬁrst or the seed.A problem that cannot be solved.Ask which was born first, the hen or the egg. (Italian)
Making a man mount a tree and then taking away the ladder.Treachery. Breach of faith.
How far can you push a man up a tree ?How long is a man to be assisted ?

proverb: చెడిపోయిన బ్రాహ్మణుడికి చచ్చిపోయిన ఆవును దానము చేశినట్టు.
translation: Like offering a dead cow to an outcast Brahman.Knock a man down, and kick him forfalling.Him that falls all the world run over. (German)All bite the bitten dog. (Portuguese)

proverb: చెడి స్నేహితుని యింటికి వెళ్లవచ్చునుగాని, చెల్లెలి యింటికి వెళ్లకూడదు.
translation: When you are mined, you may go to your friend’s house, but not to your sisters.Friends are better in adversity than relations who envied you in prosperity.Friends are the nearest relations.Relationship gives rise to envy. (Latin)

proverb: చెడేవాడు అబ్బడున్నాడు, మరీ పిడికెడు తేరా దానము చేస్తాను అన్నాడట.
translation: It is Abbadu who will be ruined, bring me another handful [of money,] I will give it in alms.Abbadu is a familiar term applied to a lad.Hens are ay free o' horse corn. (Scotch)
Will a ﬁeld of withered corn require three watcher’s sheds ?To drive off the birds.

What! Offering the house-rice to a spoilt field ?Pongali is a preparation of rice oﬂ'ered to the ﬁelds after the harvest. In this case the crop had failed and the offering had to be made from the old store. It was therefore a needless ceremony.

proverb: చెప్పినంత చేశేవాడు శివునికన్నా వేరే యెవరూ లేరు.
translation: Besides Siva, there is no other who is as good as his Word.
Advice given [ will be forgotten] ; and stale food tied up [in a bundle] will not keep.Advice whispered in the ear is worth a jeer.
A scorpion under a shoe.A ruffian under restraint.
Lies are better than backbiting.

proverb: చెప్పులు తొడుగుకొన్న వానికి లోకమంతా తోలుతో కప్పబడ్డట్టు తోస్తున్నది.
translation: To a man who Wears sandals the whole earth seems to be covered with leather.A man in comfortable circumstances does not realize the wants of others.

proverb: చెప్పే వాడికి శిగ్గు లేకపోతే, వినేవాడికయినా వివేకము వద్దా.
translation: When the slanderer has no shame should not the bearer at least use discrimination ?Should he not swallow it cum grano salis ?Though the speaker be a fool let the hearer be wise. (Spanish.)Hear the other side, and believe little. (Italian)Every man’s tale is gude till anither's be tould. (Scotch)

Pointing with the hand, and being called a luckless fellow.Indiscreetly accusing a person openly of something which you are not able to prove.A fool's tongue is long enough to cut his own throat.Hear, see, and say nothing, if you wish to live in peace. (Italian)
Ruin not others, lest thou be ruined thyself; run not, lest thou fall.He falls into the pit who leads another into it. (Spanish.)
If you ask the sugar-cane to give you molasses will it do so ?Strong measures must be used with stubborn folk.
Is hire to be paid for eating sugar-cane ?

Because the sugar-cane is sweet are you to chew it with the roots ?Said of a greedy, grasping person.Milk the cow, but don't pull off the udder. (Dutch.)Give him an inch and he’ll take an all.Give a clown your finger and he will take your whole hand.
Though the sugar-cane is crooked, will it lose its sweetness ?Good blood cannot lie. (French)

proverb: చెరువు మీద అలిగి కాళ్లు కడుక్కోక పోయినట్టు.
translation: Getting into a pet with the tank, he would not wash his feet in it.When a man grows angry, his reason rides out.

The Setti is here to test the genuineness.A fool travelling with a Setti [merchant] was plundered. On the robbers’ questioning the genuineness of the coin, the man referred to his rich friend who had up to that time escaped their observation.To injure a friend unwittingly, from stupidity.

proverb: చెవిటి పెద్దమ్మా చేంత్రాడు తేవే అంటే, చెవుల పోగులు నా జన్మానా యెరగనన్నదట.
translation: When the deaf old lady was asked to bring the well-rope, she replied “I have never seen earrings in all my life.”Applied to a stupid person not doing what he is told.

proverb: చెవిటి వాడికి శంఖము వూదితే, అది కొరకడానకు నీ తండ్రి తాతల తరము కాదన్నాడట.
translation: When a horn was sounded in the ears of a deaf man, he said “ to bite that is more than your fathers before you could do.”Applied to a stubborn person.

proverb: చెవుడు చెవుడూ అంటే, తవుడు తవుడూ అంటాడు.
translation: When they call out “Deaf man! Deaf man!” he answers “[I’ve got] Bran, Bran!”The jingle of the Telugu words is similar to that of the English equivalents.

proverb: చెవాకు పోయినమ్మకు యెంత వ్యసనమో, దొరికినమ్మకు అంత సంతోషము.
translation: The lady who found the ear-ornament was as glad as the lady who lost it was sorry.Ill-luck is good for something. (French)
Are you eating bitter or cutting down trees ?Said of anything easy, or profitable.
The stubble tells the produce of the ﬁeld.A slight acquaintance suffices to tell what a man is made of.
Can the waste land be taken out of the ﬁeld ?You. must take the fat with the lean.
I have given the ﬁeld and the sickle into your hands.To hand anything over entirely to another. To give another full powers.
Must you teach a young ﬁsh to swim ?Don’t teach fish to swim. (French)

proverb: చేలోపత్తి చేలో వుండగానే, పోలికి మూడు మూళ్లు నాకు ఆరు మూళ్లు అన్నట్టు.
translation: While the cotton crop was still in the ﬁeld, he said “ Three cubits [of the cloth] for Poli and six for me.”Poli is a female name and here represents a cousin.Sane enough to cry "chick" when it's out of the shell. (Scotch)22
Sin is removed by confession.Confession of a fault makes half amends.A sin confessed is half forgiven. (Italian)
O Mahadéva! what a man does, that does he in full receive.

proverb: చేసేది బీద కాపరము, వచ్చేవి రాజ రోగాలు.
translation: A man living in poor style, attacked by king’s diseases.A poor man having to meet great expenses quite beyond his meansThe cure of the disease alluded to costs much money.

proverb: చేసేవి నాయకాలు, అడిగేవి తిరిపాలు, పెట్టకుంటే కోపాలు.
translation: He gives himself the airs of a great man, but begs for alms, and is angry if he gets none.
His employment is the worship of Siva, but his words are lies.A hypocrite.Much praying, but no piety.Beads about the neck and the devil in the heart.

proverb: ఛిద్రమునకు చీరపేలు, దరిద్రమునకు తలపేలు.A louse in the clothes is a sign of coming household broils, a louse in the head is a sign of coming poverty.

translation: Sin is removed by confession.Confession of a fault makes half amends.A sin confessed is half forgiven. (Italian)
O Mahadéva! what a man does, that does he in full receive.

proverb: చేసేది బీద కాపరము, వచ్చేవి రాజ రోగాలు.
translation: A man living in poor style, attacked by king’s diseases.A poor man having to meet great expenses quite beyond his meansThe cure of the disease alluded to costs much money.

proverb: చేసేవి నాయకాలు, అడిగేవి తిరిపాలు, పెట్టకుంటే కోపాలు.
translation: He gives himself the airs of a great man, but begs for alms, and is angry if he gets none.
His employment is the worship of Siva, but his words are lies.A hypocrite.Much praying, but no piety.Beads about the neck and the devil in the heart.

proverb: ఛిద్రమునకు చీరపేలు, దరిద్రమునకు తలపేలు.A louse in the clothes is a sign of coming household broils, a louse in the head is a sign of coming poverty.

translation: If children are born to a Jangam they are only an annoyance to the village.Because they will add to the number of beggars. The Jangams are the Vira Saivas or followers of the Hindu reformer Basava.Beggars breed, and rich men feed.
What is the use of the sacred thread to a well known Brahman ?That which is well known need not be published.
A grasshopper eating the seed in the drilling machine.Premature ruin.
As long as fortune favours you, you have nothing to fear.
Wisdom according to your birth, religious observances according to your caste.
A staff a cubic long in a house a span wide;An impossibility.
The hireling has become the master of the house.Applied to a forward person, who does not know his place.
[Where] the servant [is] without pay the master [must be] without anger
“I’ll watch the sheep without wages,” said the wolf.A man offering to serve without pay for the sake of dishonest gains.

Dead while living, living while deadA bad man is, in the estimate of his fellow men, as little valued as if he were dead, but the good man's deeds live after he himself has left the world.“The memory of the just is blessed." Proverbs x. 7.
When two Jogis jostled against each other ashes fell [from their bodies].Nothing is to be got out of the quarrels of the poor.
The stain of plantain [can never be effaced], the grumbling of cousins never ceases.
The man that did not pay his fare, got into the boat ﬁrst.
A harlot is as a mother to a man without money.Something beyond the reach of a man’s means.

proverb: డొంకలో షరాఫు వున్నాడు, నాణెము చూపుకో వచ్చును.
translation: The banker is in the thicket, you may get him to test the coin.
Tangédu blossoms.Tangedu is the Cassia Auriculata.
A vicious horse requires a comb made of Palmyra wood.Full of splinters.

proverb: తండ్రి తవ్విన నుయ్యి అని అందులోపడి చావ వచ్చునా.
translation: Will you drown yourself in the well because your father dug it ?
The man that did not pay his fare, got into the boat ﬁrst.
A harlot is as a mother to a man without money.Something beyond the reach of a man’s means.

proverb: డొంకలో షరాఫు వున్నాడు, నాణెము చూపుకో వచ్చును.
translation: The banker is in the thicket, you may get him to test the coin.
Tangédu blossoms.Tangedu is the Cassia Auriculata.
A vicious horse requires a comb made of Palmyra wood.Full of splinters.

proverb: తండ్రి తవ్విన నుయ్యి అని అందులోపడి చావ వచ్చునా.
translation: Will you drown yourself in the well because your father dug it ?
The scrupulosity of a Saiva priest.The Tambalas are a class of Sudra priests who wear the mend thread, and endeavour to observe Brahminical customs.Applied to foolish scruples.

proverb: తంబళి తన లొటలొటేగాని, యెదటి లొటలొట యెరగడు.
translation: A Tambali prates, but does not listen to what others say.

proverb: తక్కువ నోములు నోచి యెక్కువ ఫలము రమ్మంటే వచ్చునా.
translation: If you expect much fruit from few offerings will it be obtained ?
That makebate Brahma has made a pretty match of it!Said by one of an unhappy pair.
A Wounded foot is always striking against something.

proverb: తగు దాసరికీ మెడ పూసలకూ, అమ్మకన్న కాన్పుకూ అయ్య యిచ్చిన మనుముకూ.
translation: Like Dasari, like beads; like the bringing forth of the mother, is the match made by the father.Said by a miserable daughter, who had been married to a bad husband.
As a dog enters into a house with an open door.Applied to property not watched.
He cuts a [man‘s] throat with a wet cloth.Smooth words, but hidden malice.They scratch you with one hand and strike you with the other. (Latin)
A man will not build a hut until he has been drenched, nor stoop until he has hit his head.
