When the cot was wet, it became tight.The cots used by the poorest classes are plaited with the fibres of the Cannabis Szitiva which shrink when wet.A bad man is puffed up when ﬂattered.
Like carrying 30 Tums (750 lbs.) when you’re wet.
Like going to Macavaram round about by Tanuku.To do things in a roundabout way.
Buying a Taddinam.Bringing difficulties upon one’s own head.
The smell of a man is pleasant to himself, but the smell of others is disgusting.People do not ﬁnd fault with their own actions.
A man’s having is the wealth of Indra, his not having is the poverty of the world.In prosperity a man thinks himself the wealthiest of all, and in advercity the poorest.
He brought fetters for his own legs.He brings a staff to brak his ain head. (Scotch)

proverb: తనకు అని తవ్వెడు తవుడు వుంటే, ఆకటి వేళకు ఆరగించవచ్చును.
translation: If you have even a Tavva (1 lb. ) of bran of your own, you can eat it when you are hungry.Store is no sore.
[He called] the woman whom he could not get [as a wife] a harlot.Sour grapes as the fox said when he could not reach them.What you can't have, abuse. (Italian.)

proverb: తనకు కాని రాజ్యము పండితేనేమి పదట కలిస్తేనేమి.
translation: What does it matter whether the kingdom he has not possession of prospers or decays ?

proverb: తనకు చెప్ప తడికెలచాటు వొకరికి చెప్ప వొప్పుల కుప్ప.
translation: When [the priest] applies [the law] to himself he screens himself [from its penalty]; but in laying down [the law] for others he is a beauty.Justice, but not in my own house. (Spanish)No one likea justice brought home to his own door. (Italian)

proverb: తనకు మాలిన ధర్మము, మొదలు చెడ్డ బేరము.
translation: To give that in charity which a man himself needs to receive from others is like trading after the loss of one’s principal.

proverb: తన కొంగున వున్న రూకా తన కడుపున పుట్టిన బిడ్డా పనికి వస్తవి.
translation: The money tied up in your skirt and the child you have begotten will be of use to you.

proverb: తన చెయ్యి కాలుతుందని సవితి బిడ్డ చేతితో కలియపెట్టినదట.
translation: To save her own hand from being burnt, she stirred up the food with the hand of her fellow wife’s child.Cat’s paw.To draw the snake out of the hole with another’s hand. (Spanish)23

proverb: తన తలుపు తీశి పొరుగింటికి పెట్టి రాత్రి అంతా కుక్కలు తోలుతూ కూర్చున్నట్టు.
translation: Having taken his own door and put it on his neighbour's house, he spent the whole night in driving away the dogs.Over generous.Charity begins at home.

proverb: తన తల్లి చావుకన్నా పిన తల్లి చావు మేలు.
translation: The death of an aunt is better than the death of a mother.
A man’s shadow remains with himself.The fruits of a man’s actions go with him whether good or evil.
Killing a sacred cow that gores you is not sin.

proverb: తన పిల్ల తవిటికి యేడిస్తే, లంజపిల్ల రావిరేకకు యేడ్చినది.
translation: When the legitimate child cried for bran ( the poorest food ) the illegitimate child asked for an ornament.An unreasonable request at an inopportune time.

proverb: తన పుట్టి పిచ్చగా వుంటే, పొరుగు పందుం పరా కొలిచినాడట.
translation: When his Putti was found to be of short measure, he tested the Pandum and Para of his neighbour.Putti, Pandum and Para are measures of capacity.When a man was found cheating with a false measure, he tried to prove that his neighbour's measures were too large.

proverb: తన బలిమి కన్నా స్థాన బలిమి మేలు.
translation: The power of the place is greater than the power of the man.Every man is powerful in his own house.Every one is a king in his own house. (Portuguese)
If not on my posteriors, crawl as far as Kasi (Benares).

proverb: తనవారు లోతుకు తీతురు, కానివారు కడకు తీతురు.
translation: His own people will pull him into the depth, his enemies will pull him to the bankEven an enemy is to be preferred to a relative in a time of danger.

proverb: తన వాశి తప్పితే, వొక వన్నె తరుగును.
translation: If virtue fails, honor decreases with it.
The mortar came in the way of the foot which kicked.A fortunate hindrance in the commission of an act of violence.

proverb: తప్పులు వెతికేవాడు తండ్రి, ఒప్పులు వెతికేవాడు వోర్వలేనివాడు.
translation: The man who seeks out your faults is a father, but he that seeks for what is good in you is an envious person.You may be more thankful to the fault ﬁnder than to the good natured.
There is not a blameless man in the world.There's none without a foul. (Scotch)Lifeless, faultless.

proverb: తప్పూ వొప్పూ దైవమెరుగును, పప్పూ కూడూ బాపడెరుగును.
translation: God knows right and wrong; the Brahman knows Dal and rice.
A brand under one’s head.A dangerous companion.
There is no sentence beyond the head.Do your worst, you can but cut my head off.

proverb: తల కోశి ముందర పెట్టితే, గారడీ విద్య అన్నట్టు.
translation: Though you out off your head and place it before him he would call it magic.Incredulity.
A snake under the pillow.
When the head has been wet, the shaving must be completed.When a man has commenced a business he must ﬁnish it himself.
The life in a man’s head went into his tail.Applied to strenuous exertions made to accomplish a difficult task.
Their heads are clean shaven, but are their thoughts :clean also ?

proverb: తలా తోకా లేని కథ, ముక్కూ ముఖము లేని పిల్ల.
translation: A story without head or tail, a child without nose or face.A story without a head. (Greek.)
A Talari’s hate ends with one’s head.Talari is a village watchman.
Cakes are triﬂes to a man that swallows doors.
Unless the child cries, even the mother will not give it suck.Nothing is got without asking. 'Lose nothing for asking.Asking costs little. (Italian)

proverb: తల్లి కడుపు చూచును, పెండ్లాము వీపు చూచును.
translation: The mother will look at his belly the wife at his back.The mother will look to see how her son fares, the wife to see what her husband has brought home for her.Come but come stooping.

proverb: తల్లికడుపులో చొరకముందు దయ్యాలదేవత, భూమిలో పుట్టిన తర్వాత యమదేవత.
translation: Before he entered his mother’s womb he was the prince of devils; after he was born into the world he became the god of death.Said of a exceedingly wicked and relentlessly cruel man.
Will he who is disliked by his mother, be liked by his nurse ?

proverb: తల్లికి కూడు పెట్టనివాడు తగుదు తగుదునని తగువు తీర్చ వచ్చినట్టు.
translation: Like a man who neglects his mother coming with a bold face to settle a dispute between others.Shamefacedness.
Throw scarlet water over your mother.

proverb: తల్లికి తగిన బిడ్డా, యింటికి తగిన పందిలి.
translation: A child worthy of its mother, a Pandili fit for the house.

proverb: తల్లిగండము పిల్లగండము వున్నదిగాని మంత్రసాని గండము వున్నదా.
translation: Evil may be predicted to the mother or to the child, but Will any harm come to the midwife ?
When the mother dies, the father is equal to an uncle.He is not so affectionate as before.

proverb: తల్లిచస్తే కడుపు పెద్ద, తలమాస్తే కొప్పు పెద్ద.
translation: When the mother dies, a man’s gluttony is seen; when the head is dirty, [a woman’s] top-knot is large [and untidy].As long as the mother lives her son's greediness is concealed.
Will the child (daughter) fail to follow in its mother’s track ?She hath a mark after. her mother.As the old cock crows, so crows the young."As is the mother so is her daughter.” Ezekiel xvi. 44.
The ears of the aunt are better than those of the mother.They are better supplied with ornaments.To neglect one having great claims, for another whose claims are inferior.

proverb: తల్లి చెవుల మద్దికాయలు దండగలకు, భార్య మెళ్లో పూసలు బోగాలవారికి.
translation: His mother’s ear-ornaments went to pay ﬁnes, and his wife’s necklace of beads to pay harlots.

proverb: తల్లిచెవులు తెంచిన వాడికి ముత్తవతల్లి చెవులు వెంట్రుక మాత్రము.
translation: The man who tears [the ornaments out of] his mother’s ears, will not care a rush for the ears of his great-grand mother.
Partiality belongs to a mother and to the earth.A mother is partial to some of her children, and the earth is unequal in its favors, bringing forth more for one than for another.
A mother is a divinity, a father a treasure.

proverb: తల్లిని చూచి పిల్లనూ పాడిని చూచి బర్రెనూ తీసుకోవలెను.
translation: Look at the mother before you take the daughter; see how much milk the buffalo gives before you buy her.
Will you boast of your mother’s family before your maternal uncle ?
Will not the nurse hate the child which is hated by its mother ?

proverb: తల్లి లేని పిల్ల వుల్లి లేని కూర.
translation: A motherless child is like a curry without onions.
Should a man strut who lives on bran ?
By digging and digging the truth is discovered.
He dug and threw [the earth] on his own head.
The cat which could not drink, upset the pot.
Not a morsel to eat, but Atakali for his head.Atakali is an expensive preparation used for cleansing the head.

proverb: తాగేది దమ్మిడీ గంజాయి, యిల్లంతా చెడు వుమ్ములు.
translation: He smokes half a farthing’s worth of Ganjayi, and fouls the whole house with spittle.Ganjayi (Hind. Ganjha) is the hemp plant (Cannabis Sativa) the leaves or young leaf-buds of which are bruised and smoked to produce intoxication. In its liquid form it is called Bhang.—A Dammidi is the twelfth of an ana ( anna ).Great injury caused by a little evil.
The monkey ruined himself, and all the forest after him.24
Will hares be frightened by the rustling of palmyra leaves ?
Like the sun setting in a palmyra grove.Left in great diffiiculty.
Like sliding on a palmyra beam, against the grain.The bark of the Palmyra is scaly and rugged.Injuring one's self by perverse conduct.
Like ordering the well to be ﬁlled up when the rope was found too short.
How far can you help the man who is climbing up a palmyra tree ?A clumsy person must be helped to the end.
Must you teach your grandfather how to cough ?

proverb: తాతాచర్య్ల ముద్ర భుజము తప్పినా వీపు తప్పదు.
translation: If Tatachari’s brand misses your shoulder, you’ll catch it on the back.Tatachari was a wandering priest who branded his converts. If a man slipped away his shoulder, the seal caught him in the back.Not being able to evade something disagreeable.

proverb: తాతా పెండ్లాడుతావా అంటే, నాకెవడిస్తాడురా అబ్బా అబ్బానడట.
translation: When the grandfather was asked Whether he would like to be married, he said, “Who will give me [a daughter,] my boy ?”

O grandfather! Sankranti [has arrived] catch it! catch it!Sankranti is the transit of the sun from Sagittarius to Capricornus, when the Hindus celebrate the Pongal feast-This is chaff; old people observe the feast very scrupulously.

proverb: తాదిన తవుడు లేదు, వారానికి వక పందిపిల్ల.
translation: He has not even bran to eat, but he wants a pig every week.

proverb: తానుండేది దాలిగుంట పట్టు, తలచేవి మేడా మాళిగెలు.
translation: He lives in a pit, but dreams of castles.His ideas are far above his position.

proverb: తానుచేశిన పాపం తనువుతో, తల్లిచేశిన పాపం ధరణితో.
translation: The sin which a man commits goes with his body, the sin which his mother commits goes with the earth.This is interpreted to mean that if a man commits the sin of adultery, his crime may be expiated by a punishment equal to loss of life, but if a woman is guilty of the same offence, it clings to her descendants as long as the world endures.
The hare he caught had only three legs.Said of a pertinaceous person.
The hen he has caught has four legs.Exaggeration.All his geese are swans.

proverb: తాను పెంచిన పొట్టేలు తన చేతనే చంపబడ్డట్టు.
translation: Like the ram reared by one’s self being killed by one’s own hand.
When there is no room for him to pass himself, he hangs a, big drum round his neck.Said of a man who tries to provide for another, when he has difficulty in managing for himself.
You should look what you can swallow, and what can swallow you.Look before you leap.
Is a cat to come as well as himself ?Said when a man who is already a burden upon others, brings some one else with him. Amongst the Hindus a cat is an evil omen.

proverb: తానూ వక మనిషేనా తవుడూ వక రొట్టేనా.
translation: He calls himself a man, and the bran he eats, bread.A vulgar person taking airs.
After sneezing he blessed himself saying “May you live a hundred years!”A joke.The blessing ought to come from another.
When he planned one thing, God planned another.Man proposes, God disposes.
A mortar is a [sure] check to a runaway cow.When tied to its neck.Applied to measures which to check the progress of crime.
What he has planted although it be Strychnine he must cherish.Mushini Mokka is the Strychnos Nux-Vomica.A patron must not forsake his dependent under any circumstances.
When about to go off herself, [she ordered] the white ox [to be fed with] rice washings.Applied to the absurdity of a person troubling himself with affairs in which he has no longer any interest.
After being refused butter-milk, when he went himself, he ordered his servant to write for curds.After being refused a triﬂing request, to demand authoritatively some thing much greater.
Like a drop of water on a lotus leaf.A very unsettled state.
Patience will save you and your neighbour.

proverb: తాళ్లకు తలను చండ్లు మేకలకు మెడను చండ్లు.
translation: The Palmyra trees have teats on their heads, the goats have teats (Wattles) on their necks.The Hindus call the ﬂower stalk (apadix ) of the Palmyra a teat.Useless appendages.

proverb: తాళ్లపాకవారి కవిత్వము కొంత, తన పైత్యము కొంత.
translation: Part [of the book] is the poetry of Tallapakavaru, the rest is his own rubbish.Said of a man who has spoilt the good work of another.
A man that kicks the heads of people who kick the tops of Palmyra trees.More wicked than the other.
He thinks the woman he loves to be Rambha and the water he bathes in, the Ganges.Rambha is the most beautiful of all the Apsarasas or courtezans of Svarga, the Hindu elysium.
If I eat I can’t move, if I don’t eat I can’t stir.

proverb: తింటేగాని రుచి తెలియదు, దిగితేగాని లోతు తెలియదు.
translation: Without eating you can’t tell the taste; without going down [into the water] you can’t know the depth.The proof of a pudding is in the eating.
He’s not worth his food, and is a burden on the earth.He is not worth his salt.

He is a Timma Razu (monkey-king) at eating; and a Pota Razu (buffalo-king ) at work.As greedy as a monkey and as lazy as a buffalo.Like the smith’s dog, that sleeps at the sound of the hammer, and wakes at the crashing of the teeth.He eats tilt ha sweats, and works till he freezes.
Have you come to eat or to visit the shrine ?

proverb: తిట్టి చచ్చినవాడూ లేడు, దీవించి బ్రతికినవాడూ లేడు.
translation: No man has ever died from cursing, or lived from blessing.No one dies of threats. (Dutch)

proverb: తిట్టితే గాలికి పోతవి, తింటే లోగా పోతవి.
translation: The abuse which is heaped on me goes to the winds, but the food which I eat goes within.A mean sycophant.
I am not abusing you, my good son of a slut.
The body accustomed to sumptuous living was distressed for want of food.Applied to a man whose malpractiees have been put a. stop to, but who is eagerly seeking an opportunity of renewing them.

proverb: తిన్న కుక్క తిని పోతే, కన్న కుక్కను పట్టి కాళ్లు విరగకొట్టినట్టు.
translation: When the dog which had eaten [the food] ran off, he caught hold of another dog and broke its leg.Making the innocent suffer for the guilty.

proverb: తిన్నయింటి వాసాలు యెంచుతావేమిరా అంటే, పొరిగింటికి పోతే వూర్కుంటారా అన్నాడట.
translation: When a man was asked why he was counting the rafters of the house in which he had been well treated, he replied, “Would they allow me to do it in the next house ?”Gross ingratitude.I taught you to swim, and now you'd drown me.

A man that counts the rafters of his benefactor’s house.A story is told of a man who counted the rafters, &c., of a house in order that he might take possession of it and found his claim to it on this knowledge. Hence the term is applied to a person basely attempting to swindle another, who had befriended him, out of his property.All’s lost that’s put into a riven dish.

proverb: తిన్నయిల్లు గుద్దలించేవేమిరా అంటే, తినని యిల్లు నేనేమి యెరుగుదును అన్నాడట.
translation: When asked, “Why are you digging [for treasure] in the house where you have eaten ?” he replied, “What do I know of a house where I have not eaten ?”Do good to a knave and pray God he requite thee not. (Danish)
He that ate prospered, and he that prospered became a Mahârâja.
To a set of monkey-like scamps tasteless broth and plain rice [should be served].

He makes Timmi into Brâhmi and Brâhmi into Timmi.'Timmi is a familiar name for a she-monkey; Brâhmi (in the Proverb incorrectly Brahmi) is a name of Saraswati, wife of Brahma.He calls evil good, and good evil.25

proverb: తియ్యగా తియ్యగా రాగము, మూలగగా మూలగగా రోగము.
translation: The more you practise the better you will sing; the more you groan the more will your illness increase.
Are there sweet diseases, and delicious medicines ?

proverb: తిరగ మరిగిన కాలూ తిట్ట మరిగిన నోరూ వూరకుండవు.
translation: The feet which are wont to wander, and the mouth which is accustomed to abuse, will never be quiet.
Will a Mussulman become a Dasari by going to Tirapati ?Tirapati (prop. Tirupati) is a sacred hill about 80 miles from Madras.

Like the Tirapati barber.Has it all his own way. Pilgrims visiting the place have to get their heads shaved, and as one man has the entire monopoly he keeps them waiting for hours, taking payment in advance and shaving a little bit of one man's head and then a little bit of another, to prevent them from going away.Applied to a person selfishly taking work out of others’ bands which he is unable to complete himself.

proverb: తిరిపెపు మజ్జిగకు వచ్చి పాడి బర్రెను బేరము చేశినట్టు.
translation: Like coming to beg buttermilk, and bargaining for tho milch buffalo.Pretended wealth.

proverb: తిలాః పాపహరా నిత్యం, తలా పిడికెడు పిడికెడు.
translation: Sesamum seed always takes away sin, [give them] a handful a head.Tila is the Sesamum Indicum.A jocose proverb, half Sanscrit, half Telugu.
Is the gourd too heavy for the creeper ?Applied to having a large family to support.
When the creeper was pulled the whole bush shook.Injury to the head of the house reaches to all the members.
The man with itch scratches himselfLet him that itches scratch himself. (french)
When the man received a blow on his back, he cried out that he had lost his teeth.Applied to inappropriate actions or words.

proverb: తుడుం తుడుం అంటే, దురాయి దురాయి అన్నట్టు.
translation: When the drum sounded “tudum, tudum” a man called out “durayi, durayi.”Tudum is the (onomatopoetic) name of a kind of drum (Tom-tom ). Durayi is a form of adjuration in the name of Government.
The beauty of his slobberings, not of his mantras
If your nose won’t stand sneezing how long will it last ?
A sneeze warns you as a younger brother.On the commencement of an undertaking, if any one sneezes it is considered a bad omen.
The butter-milk of a Mussulman mendicant is Toddy.Applied to pretended sanctity.
When a Mussulman raid is expected is one [afraid of] facing Venus ?Are omens observed in times of danger ?Necessity has no law. (Latin)

In a village where there are no Mussulmans, the cotton cleaner is Saiyid Miyan.He is made much of.—The Saiyids are the high-born descendants of Russia, grandson of Muhammad. Miyan is a title. The cotton cleaners are 'mongrel Mussulmans.' (Brown).

proverb: తులసి కోటలో వుమ్మేశినా వేమిరా అంటే, యజ్ఞవేదిక అనుకొన్నాను అన్నాడట.
translation: When a man was asked why he spat on the Tulasi-Kota, he replied that he thought it was an altar.The irreverent answer of a sacrilegious sceptic, an altar being still more sacred-Talusi is the holy basil (Ocimum Sacrum). Tulasi-kôta is the Telugu name for the altar-like bed in which it is grown, in the inner yard of a Hindu house.
Like a hemp plant growing in a Tulasi garden.A black sheep in the family.
A frisky bullock carries a good load.A spirited man works well.

proverb: తూమెడు వడ్లు తూర్పారపెట్టేటప్పటికి యేదుము వడ్లు యెలుకలు తిని పోయినవి.
translation: While he was winnowing one Tûm of rice, the rats devoured five Tûms.Tûm is the Indian bushel.
A straw as Mount Méru, Mount Méru as a straw.The slightest favour done to a good man is gratefully acknowledged, but a bad man forgets the greatest benefits.

proverb: తెగించి దానము చేస్తాను తేరా పిడికెడు ఆళ్లు అన్నట్టు.
translation: I have determined on being liberal, bring a handful of grain.
The man who is determined [to worship] uses his ladle as a lingam.Where there’s a will, there’s a way.The will is everything. (Italian.)The will is the soul of the work. (German.)

proverb: తెగించి వెనకకు పోయినంత తెలివి తక్కువ లేదు.
translation: There is no greater folly than turning back after having once ventured to run the risk.Turning back after putting one’s hand to the plough.Fortune lost, nothing lost; courage lost, much lost; honour lost, more lost; soul lost, all last. (Dutch)
Liberality leads to Indra’s heaven.

proverb: తెచ్చుకొంటే భోంచెయి జగన్నాయకా, లేకుంటే వూరకుండు జగన్నాయకా.
translation: If you get any thing eat it O Jagannayaka! if you don’t get any thing do without it O Jagannayaka!Jagannayaka is the idol at Jagannatha, in Orissa.
Like licking the ladle when you’re hungry.

proverb: తెడ్డు యేది అంటే, కొయ్య యేది అన్నట్టు.
translation: One said “ Where’s the ladle ?” the other said "Where’s the stick ?"Two lazy fellows.
Burning his hand when he had a ladle.To stir the rice with.
Like the oilmonger’s stone seat.Of permanent utility.
Little sense, great appetite.Eating one's senses away.
Although he knew the pit well, he fell into it.

Before you know it, it’s hard; when you know it, it’s easy.Every thing is easy when you know it.All things are diﬂicult before they are easy.All beginnings are hard, said the thief, and began by stealing an anvil. (Dutch)
Your sluggishness is equal to a full fathom.The word Bâra properly signiﬁes the length of the arms extended as when a man stretches himself.
What is the roughness of the ear to the man who gets grain for nothing ?
A horse for nothing, a Tangédu switch.Tangedu is the Cassia Auriculata.—“When you ride gratis you use your cudgel well.” (Brown's Tel. Dicty.)
Property got for nothing is Bira peel.Bira Kaya is the Luffa Foetida.
Born to a scorpion and becoming a black ant.An unworthy scion of a. noble stock.
When the scorpion was given power, it continued stinging until the morning.Applied to the abuse of authority by evil persons.
Who did evil to the scorpion ?Thoroughly bad by nature injuring others without provocation.
The sting among the scorpions, the hood among the snakes.The worst among the bad.
Like a man waking up the sleeping custom house oﬁicer to help him to put down his load.A “green trick.”

The bear is witness for the bloodsucker orThe hedge is witness for the bloodsucker.According to the ﬁrst interpretation, a band of Mahomedan freebooters, when seeking on one occasion to discover treasure which had been buried by the inhabitants of a plundered village, having perceived ﬁrst a chameleon nodding on certain spots and afterwards a bear sniffing in the same places, spent much labour in digging up the earth, but all in vain.—-The application is that natural habits are not to be taken notice of.According to others, the hedge where the chameleon lives should be questioned as to the latter’s veracity and character,—-the application being that the evidence of one man should be corroborated by that of another.Ask my chum if I am a thief. (Italian)
The Tonda grew old, and hecarne an Usaravilli.Tonds is a bloodsucker (chameleon) which when old is called ûsaravilli.Vice growing with age.

proverb: తొంభై తొమ్మండ్రుగురు పోగయి తోలు చిరగ పొడిచినారు.
translation: Ninety-nine persons joined together [attacked a man, but only] succeeded in scratching his skin.A cowardly mob.26
The woman who couldn’t pound, put water into the condiments.She was too lazy to pound the condiments and therefore purposely spoilt them.
If a slut be inspired by the Deity, can one escape saluting her ?Respect must be paid to those in power, however low their origin.
He sprang up like a serpent when its tail is trodden on.Applied to a sudden burst of anger in any one on his evil ways being exposed.

proverb: తోచీ తోచనమ్మ తోడికోడలి చెల్లెలి పెండ్లికి పోయినదట.
translation: A stupid woman going to the marriage of her husband’s brother’s wife’s sister.Doing useless things.

proverb: తోట కూరకు కడిగి పెట్టిన యెసరే చాలును.
translation: The water which remains in greens after they have been washed is sfficient to boil them in.

proverb: దండమయ్యా బాపనయ్యా అంటే, మీ తండ్రి నాటి పాత బాకీ యిచ్చి పొమ్మనాడట.When the Śûdra saluted the Brahman, the latter in return asked him to pay an old debt due by his father.Rudeness in return for civility.

translation: The woman who couldn’t pound, put water into the condiments.She was too lazy to pound the condiments and therefore purposely spoilt them.
If a slut be inspired by the Deity, can one escape saluting her ?Respect must be paid to those in power, however low their origin.
He sprang up like a serpent when its tail is trodden on.Applied to a sudden burst of anger in any one on his evil ways being exposed.

proverb: తోచీ తోచనమ్మ తోడికోడలి చెల్లెలి పెండ్లికి పోయినదట.
translation: A stupid woman going to the marriage of her husband’s brother’s wife’s sister.Doing useless things.

proverb: తోట కూరకు కడిగి పెట్టిన యెసరే చాలును.
translation: The water which remains in greens after they have been washed is sfficient to boil them in.

proverb: దండమయ్యా బాపనయ్యా అంటే, మీ తండ్రి నాటి పాత బాకీ యిచ్చి పొమ్మనాడట.When the Śûdra saluted the Brahman, the latter in return asked him to pay an old debt due by his father.Rudeness in return for civility.
దండుగకు రూపాయిలూ, తద్దినానకు కూరలూ పోగు కాకుండా పోవు.
translation: Rupees to meet the exorbitant demands of Government, and vegetables for the annual ceremony of a deceased relative are never wanting.Foolishly giving aid in matters which bring no personal proﬁt.
If you go to the wars, it will be one out of two.Either victory or defeat.
Will the nose that can’t stand a cough bear a sneeze ?

proverb: దగ్గిరికి పిలిచి దాసరీ నీ కన్ను లొట్ట అన్నట్టు.
translation: Beckoning to a religious mendicant and then telling him that one of his eyes is hollow.Leading a person to suppose you wish to confer a. beneﬁt on him, and then insulting him.
Grief for an adopted son.Slight grief.

proverb: దయగల మొగుడు దండుకు పోతూ, రోలు తీశి రొమ్మున వేల వేశి పోయినాడట.
translation: When the kind husband went to the wars, he hung the mortar round his wife’s neck.Rôlu is a large heavy mortar used for pounding rice in.Said ironically of a bad husband cruel to the last.

When your favor answers my call with a Darr-rr my head will ache with a ḉurr-rr.Darr is a whirring sound; ḉurr is applied to acute or burning pain.Said by a man to another in whose friendship he puts no faith.
In the eye of a devil, parched grain is evil.
When the devil attacks will the child live ?Said of a meddling fool.
Could a child live in front of devils?

proverb: దరిద్రుడు తల కడగబోతే, వడిగండ్ల వాన వెంబడే వచ్చినది.
translation: When the poor man was about to anoint his head, it began to hail.Difficulties always attend an unfortunate person.He who is born to misfortune stumbles as he goes, and though he fall on his back will fracture his nose. (German.)
A Poppy seed in the ocean.(Corrupted from the Hindustani ).A drop in the ocean.
For the ten gifts, [I give you] a bundle of greens.The Dasa Danalu ( ten gifts to Brahmans) are as follows :—
రౌప్యంలవణమిత్యేతేదశదానాఃప్రకీర్తితాః||A cow; land; sesamum seed; gold; ghee; cloth; grain; molasses; silver; and salt.Cheating the Brahmans.
The devils caught him in the place he went to hide in.One ill calls another. (Italian.)

proverb: దానము అయితే యివ్వలేను, దండగ అయితే పెట్టగలను.
translation: As for charity, I can’t give; as for ﬁnes, I can pay.Said by a quarrelsome ruffan.
Buying and bringing a suit which was going along the road.Buying the right to carry on a heavy law suit from a stranger.
The crime of a Dasari is excused with an apology.
For the song of a Dasari an aims is the payment.All that his singing is worth.Small payment for bad work.
The troubles of a Dasari are known only to Perumal.

proverb: దాసరివా జంగమవా అంటే, ముందరి వూరికొద్దీ అన్నాడట.
translation: When a mendicant was asked whether he was a Dasari or a Jangam (i. e. a follower of Vishnu or Siva) he replied “Oh that depends on the next village!”A time server.
A man with money is a king though he be the son of a slave girl.

I have saved [money] live separate with me, husband.Said by a wife to her husband to induce him to quit his father's house. After he had yielded to her importunity and left his father's house, he enquired the amount of her savings when she replied " I have saved myself for you.”
A government which affords no protection.
In a neglected house, devils take up their abode.
God is the help of the helpless.God is where He was. ​
If you fear, will the pain be less?
Daily danger of death, but duration of life a thousand years.Snakes are believed to live 1000 years if they do not meet with a violent death.Applied to a man always in danger of losing his situation.
Trusting to the “lucky day” the thief let the dawn overtake him.Taking advantage of liberty.
A lamp under a torch.One insigniﬁcant before the other.
Like a niche with the lamp taken out of it.The reality wanting.
Like saying “O naked man! cover him lying at your feet.”

proverb: దిసమొలవాడి దగ్గిరికి దిగంబరుడు వచ్చి బట్ట అడిగినట్టు.
translation: Like one naked man asking another naked man for a cloth.
It is always dark underneath a lamp.Roguery hide under the judgment seat.
Put all things in order while the lamp is yet burning.Make good use of your opportunities.
Like the ox being away when wanted for the plough.Absent when most wanted.
When the ﬁeld was sown without being ploughed it yielded without being reaped.i. e. it yielded nothing.If you neglect the use of the right means you must expect poor results.
When one said “The male buffalo has calved” the other replied “Then tie up the calf.”
When [wanted for] ploughing it goes among the calves; when grazing it goes with the cows.The best at eating, the worst at working.
Like pointing out antelope to ploughers.Diverting the attention of people from their work.

proverb: దుబ్బు కాగెడు, వెన్ను మూరెడు, దూసితే దోశెడు, వూదితే యేమీ లేదు.
translation: The stock of the millet is as large as you can embrace; the ear is a cubit in length; when you pluck the corn out it is a double handful; when you blow all goes.Great show but no reality.
Shun a wicked man.
Is it that the calf has sucked, or that [the milk] has fallen into the pail?In either way beneﬁcial.
Unless the calf sucks, the cow will not let milk into her udder.
Where there is no call‘, the milk is got with pain.
Like blaming the post when the calf or the she buffalo bellowed.27

proverb: దూరపు కొండలు నునుపు, దగ్గిరికి పోతే రాళ్లూ, గుండ్లూ.Mountains are smooth at a distance, but rugged when near.'Tis distance lends enchantment to the view,And robes the mountains in its azure hue. Campbell.

translation: A devil even takes fright at a blow.Strong measures will set things to right.
God will give, but will He put the food in your mouth ?God helps hose who help themselves.

proverb: దేవుడితోడు నా మీద దయ వుంచ వలశినది.
translation: I adjure you by God to show me favor.Attempting to obtain a person’s favor by force.
At the marriage ceremony of a god all are rulers.Where there is no head, every one does what he likes.
Although God grant a boon, yet the priest will not grant it
The thief does not care if the corn be not quite ripe.A man is not particular about that which does not belong to him.
He opens the door for the robber and then awakes his master.A double dealing rascal.
A thief is content with what he gets.
When one cried out “I have got the thief” the other said “Take care! he’ll bite.”A cowardly fellow.

proverb: దొంగ చెయ్యి దాచిపెట్టితే, అమావాస్య వచ్చినప్పుడు అల్లల్లాడినట్టు.
translation: The thief’s hand that was kept began to shake when the dark night of the new moon came.The time it had been accustomed to steal.
The devil follows close on the thief‘.To urge him on.
Like a robber stung by a scorpionA man does not cry out when he suﬂ'ers from his own folly.

proverb: దొంగను పుట్టించిన వాడు మతిభ్రష్టును పుట్టించక మానడు.
translation: He that created a knave will not fail to create a dupe.A fool only is cheated.

I060, was.)Said by a poor woman to a rich mam-At many Brahminical ceremonies gifts and food are distributed to the wives of the Vaidikas while a superior entertainment is provided for those of the Laukikas of theNiyyogi class.Forcing oneself upon another.

proverb: నీ ముష్టి లేకపోతే మానెగాని, నీ కుక్కను కట్టివెయ్యి.
translation: If there are no aims for me, never mind, but tie up your dog.Said by a beggar.

proverb: నీ యెడమ చెయ్యి తియ్యి, నా పుర్ర చెయ్యి పెట్టుతాను.
translation: Take away your left hand, I will use my ‘sinister hand.(SeeNmﬂﬁJA: broad as long.
Water runs towards water.Men haste to help those of their own caste, 8:0.
