proverb: నీలి నీళ్లకు పోతే, బావి లోతుకు పోయినది.
translation: When the jade went to the well, the water was too low.Said of a lazy person glad of any excuse to get off work. ​

proverb: నీళ్లలో నిమ్మలు బ్రతికినవి, అడివిలో తుమ్మలు బ్రతికినవి.
translation: Lime trees lived in the water, acacia trees lived in the jungle.Very common.
Tying up water in a bundle.He draw: water with a sieve.
If you won’t do it, your father will.i. e. a better person.

proverb: నీవు చచ్చిన రోజూ లేదు, నేను యేడ్చిన రోజూ లేదు.
translation: The day of your death has not come, nor the day of my mourning.Said by a wife who hated her husband.
If you were to die, would the whole earth be bones ?

Though you don’t give alms, point out a house where they do.am s's’oaaso a3, is) assesses wmémrgaia.If you pour for one thing, I drink for another.A woman poured out stale rice-water to make a man eat less solid food afterwards. The drink however has the property of increasing the appetite.Diamond cut diamond. ​

proverb: నీ సాకు చట్టుబండలు కానూ, నాలుగు బేకులు బేకు అన్నాడట.
translation: ay your Sakus become stones! Give me four Békus.Said by a greedy Telugu man at an entertainment given by a Cansrese gentleman. The servant handed round the dishes a second time with theusual enquiry " Siiku?" (enough P ) Some of the guests would reply"Bélni’ (wanted) and help themselves moderately. The Telugu man not understanding Canarese, cried out " None of your Sdkus, give me some of your Békus."
When they began to dig a well, a devil came out.Applied to a sudden and heavy misfortune.
A hundred diseases to the Sesamum.Nuvuu is the Sesamum Indicum. The Sesamum crops are peculiarly liable to attacks of sap worm, blight, &c.
Oil seed and oil are one, the oil-crusher is another.Joining to oppose a common enemy.
Instead of a hundred [cloths] a bit of thread; instead of a crore [ of cows] a cow’s tail.
Is the sea near to a frog in a well ?Applied to a clumsy fellow. ​

proverb: నూనె పోగొట్టుకొన్నవాడూ యేడ్చినాడు, బొండాపకాయల వాడూ పొర్లి పొర్లి యేడ్చినాడు.
translation: The man who lost the oil cried, and the oocoanut man cried bitterly.A bullock laden with pots of oil belonging to one man and unpeeled cocoanuts belonging to another rolled over. The pots broke, but the cocoanuts were none the worse. The ﬁrst man cried quietly, but thesecond was loud in his lamentations.Making a. fuss about nothing.
Can she drink [medicine] who could not bear to grind it’!

proverb: నూరు కల్లలాడి అయినా, వక యిల్లు నిలప వలెను.
translation: You should save another even by telling a hundred lies.

proverb: నూరు కాకులలో వక కోవెల యేమి చెయ్యగలదు.
translation: Among a hundred crows, what could a single cuckoo do’!What can one good man do among so many bad men ?
One act of adultery does away with a hundred meritorious deeds.

proverb: నూరుమంది గుడ్డివాండ్లు పోగయి వక పాడు నూతిలో పడ్డట్టు.
translation: Like a hundred blind men falling into a well.An ignorant assemblage. ​

proverb: నూరుమంది మొండిచేతులవాండ్లు పోగయి వక గొడ్డు గేదెను పాలు పితక లేక పోయినారు.
translation: A hundred ﬁngerless people together could not milk a barren buffalo.A thing in every way impossible.

proverb: నూరు మాటలు వక వ్రాతకు యీడు కావు.
translation: A hundred words are not equal to one writing.
A thread joined in weaving.No strength.
Like demanding duty for a bundle carried on the head.

proverb: నెమలి కంట నీరు కారితే వేటగానికి ముద్దా.
translation: If the peacock shed tears, will the hunter’s heart be moved’!( See Ne. 38'. )
He walks thirty miles in a month, as fast as a round plate can be thrown.

proverb: నెల తక్కువ అయినా రాజు యింట పుట్టుమన్నట్టు.
translation: Never mind though you be a seven months’ child, be born in a king’s house. ​
A thread to the new moon.When Hindus see the new moon, they unravel a thread from one of their garments and oﬁ‘er it, asking for a new cloth.
If you die to-day, to-morrow will be two.i. e. the second day, on which milk will be poured on his bones, according to custom, after his body has been burnt.
What does a Weaver want with a young monkey’!The monkey would do much mischief.Imprudent association with bad people.
A man that puts the pot of ghi on the ﬂoor, and the empty pot in the sling.The eye is blind If the mind is absent. (Italian-l‘
Mounting a ladder with a jar of ghi in one’s arms.
Like a tasteless cucumber.(Bee Nos. no, 940, m, m, 1250,1542, ms. )Néli birakéya is the ghi cucumber.Grand in name but not worth anything.' Ciecc c l'occhlo, se l'nnimo é distratto. ​

proverb: నేను పుట్టకపోతే నీకు పెండ్లామే లేకపోను అంటే, నీవు పుట్టక పోతే నీ తల్లినే పెండ్లాడి వుందును అన్నాడట.
translation: When a wife said “If I had not been born, you would not have had a wife,” the husband replied “ If you had not been born, I should have married your mother.”i. e. a better woman.
Rising off the ground and fencing in the air.If you loose your footing you will loose your power. (Brown’s Tel. Diet. )
The writing written on the forehead never failsHindus believe that their fate is mysteriously written by the Creator’: hand in the sutures of the skull.
No pollution is caused by the hand being put in the mouth.Engili literally means saliva.Said by a careless Brahman.
Would the pearls fall out of your mouth’lIf you were to speak. ​

[By] a slip of the mouth, a bundle of food on the back.In Ujjayini ( Ougein) there lived a merchant called Diptimanta. He went to Ja'ingala to trade. There he met another merchant named Vivekamanta who on one occasion said to him “ Tell me a story that is not known in the world, that has not come to your ears, and that l have not heard.” After being twitted often by Diptimanta on account of his inability to comply with this unreasonable request, Vivéknmanta undertook to per form the task, provided Diptimanta should not pronounce his story to be false, and in the event of his doing so, should pay him 100 pagodas. Diptimanta having agreed, Vivékamanta said “ On one occasion my great grandfather Vanikpati when going to trade in another country met your great grand-father Prithikpati and they entered into partnership. Shortly afterwards they reached a spot where grew :1 large and beautiful Banyan tree near which was a tank. Liking the place, they agreed that Vanikpati should remain there and sell the goods which Pljithikpati would send him from another country. This was carried on for some time and afterwards when they met and compared their accounts, it was found that Prithikpatiwas in Vanikpati's debt 100 pagodas. For long I have sought you," added Vivékamanta, “ and now I beg you will pay me the balance due." Diptimanta being unable to deny the truth of the story lest he should incur the forfeit, was compelled to pay the 100 pagodas, and beg his way to his own country, carrying what food was given to him in a bundle on his back. Some people who met him enquired what bundle he was carrying. to which he replied " By a slip of the mouth, have I been reduced to carry a bundle of food on my back."The evil consequences of an idle word.
Even a mustard seed cannot be hidden in his mouth.A gossipping fellow who repeats all he hears.

proverb: నోరు అంబాళపు పండు, చెయ్యి బలుసు ముల్లు.
translation: Your mouth is like a sweet plum and your hand is like a thorn-bush.(stﬂ Nos. ass, 17", ms.)Ambﬁlam is the Spondias Mangifera; Balusu is the Canthium Parviﬂorum.You speak sweetly with your mouth, but you give nothing with your hand.Fair words don't fill the pocket. (G€rmnn.)'’ SchGns Worte fﬂllen den Sack nicht. ​
When the mouth gets fat, it is made to salivate.A man suffers for his arrogance.
If your words are good, the world will be good to you.

proverb: నోరు మాట్లాడుతూ వుంటే, నొసలు యెక్కిరిస్తూ వుంటున్నది.
translation: Speaking with the mouth but mocking with the forehead.Insincerity.

proverb: నోరు మూస్తే మూగ, నోరు తెరిస్తే రాగ.
translation: If you shut up her mouth she remains like a dumb Woman, if you let her open her mouth she’s a vixen.
What does a just man care for the favor of the priest’!

proverb: పంచపాండవులు మంచం కోళ్లవలె ముగ్గురు అంటారేగాని, యిద్దరే అని వక వేలు చూపినాడట.“The ﬁve Pandavas they say are three like the legs of a bed, but there are only two,” said he, showing one ﬁnger.
translation: Said of a stupid accountant.

If he has a mouth he will live.The son of the minister of a king of Ayodhya (Oudh) was caught stealing the king's ﬂowers.—-As he was being taken to the palace, his father called out " It is of no consequence, if he has a mouth he will live" The son took the hint, ate up the ﬂowers. denied the offence, and was believed by the king, who punished his accusers. (See Telugu Selections, 36th story. )
When the mouth gets fat, it is made to salivate.A man suffers for his arrogance.
If your words are good, the world will be good to you.

proverb: నోరు మాట్లాడుతూ వుంటే, నొసలు యెక్కిరిస్తూ వుంటున్నది.
translation: Speaking with the mouth but mocking with the forehead.Insincerity.

proverb: నోరు మూస్తే మూగ, నోరు తెరిస్తే రాగ.
translation: If you shut up her mouth she remains like a dumb Woman, if you let her open her mouth she’s a vixen.
What does a just man care for the favor of the priest’!

proverb: పంచపాండవులు మంచం కోళ్లవలె ముగ్గురు అంటారేగాని, యిద్దరే అని వక వేలు చూపినాడట.“The ﬁve Pandavas they say are three like the legs of a bed, but there are only two,” said he, showing one ﬁnger.
translation: Said of a stupid accountant. ​
Five good things, ﬁve bad things.Differently interpreted to mean ﬁve good and ﬁve bad qualities ; the ﬁve senses applied to good and to bad uses, &c.
If the almanaoks are lost, do the stars go also ?Can the original source not be applied to, when that derived from it has been lost ?
Standing in the middle of ﬁve ﬁres.An act of penance.Said of a person being unable to extricate himself from a diﬂicult position in which he is surrounded by his enemies.
What has a beggar to do with the price of cotton ?

proverb: పండగ తొల్నాడు గుడ్డల కరువు, పండగనాడు అన్నము కరువు, పండగ మర్నాడు మజ్జిగ కరువు.
translation: The day before the festival, there is a scarcity of cloth ; on the festival day, a scarcity of food; and on the day after the festival, a scarcity of buttermilk.
The fruit slipped, and fell into the milk.(Bee Nan. no, use, uso, lass.)Happy go lucky. ​
what can a pig do with a rose water bottle ?Gindi is a. narrow mouthed vessel used for rose water.
He can make a common pig into Slva’s bull, and Srva’s bull into a common pig.(Ben Nu‘. I081, I215.)Ability in argumentation.

proverb: పందిలి పడి చచ్చిన వారూ యిల్లు పడి బ్రతికిన వారూ లేరు.
translation: No men die from the falling of a Pandili ; no men live after the falling of a house.

proverb: పందుం తిన్నా పరకడుపే, యేదుం తిన్నా యేకాదశే.
translation: Though he eat ten Tums of food he will say he is starving ; and though he eat ﬁve Tums, he will say it is a fast.
Why do you increase the sore in my side’!
A sword by the side [on a bed].

proverb: పగటి మాటలు పనికి చేటు, రాత్రి మాటలు నిద్రకు చేటు.
translation: Talking in the day time spoils one’s business; talking in the night time spoils one’s sleep. ​

proverb: పగలు చస్తే వాతికి లేదు, రాత్రి చస్తే దీపముకు లేదు.
translation: If he dies in the day, there is no rice to put in his mouth; if he dies in the night, there is no oil for a lamp.Rice is put into the mouth of the dead at the funeral ceremony.Said of a miserably poor person.

proverb: పగలెల్లా బారెడు నేశినాను, దివ్వెతేవే దిగ నేస్తాను.
translation: Like a weaver saying in the evening to his wife “woman during the day I have woven two yards, if you bring a lamp now I’ll undo it all again.”Said of an idle fellow.
If alms are given ten times in an enemy’s house, so much the better.
Put green grass [between two rivals], and it will blaze up.
Like an unripe wood apple sticking in one’s throat.Distress caused by continual worrying.

proverb: పట్టపగలు కన్నము వేస్తావేమిరా అంటే, నా కక్కురితి నీకు యేమి తెలుసును అన్నాడట.
translation: A burglar being asked why he was house-breaking in the day time, replied “ What do you know of my necessities?”Distress tempts to crime.31
In the full light of day, he makes the stars appear.Professing to do wonders. Applied to a great cheat.

proverb: పట్టినది చింత కొమ్మ గాని ములగ కొమ్మ కాదు.
translation: What I am clinging to is a Tamarind bough, not a Mulaga.The tamarind wood is pliable, but the Malays (Hyperanthera Moringa) is brittle.Said by a parasite of his staunch protector.
If there be a rainbow in the west, all the dry tanks will be filled.
One cold in the head is as bad as ten diseases.
A house full of young girls, and a ﬁre of little twigs.Difficult to manage.

proverb: పత్తిగింజలు తింటావా బసవన్నా అంటే ఆహా అన్నాడట, గంత కట్టనా బసవన్నా అంటే వూహూ అన్నాడట.
translation: When Basavanna was asked if he would eat cotton seed, he said “ Aha”; when Basavanua was asked whether he would have on the pack saddle he said “Uhu.”Basavanna is the name of a bull sacred to Siva. Bullocks are often familiarly called by this name. Âhâ is an exclamation signifying consent. Ûhû signiﬁes refusal.
With many people death is equal to marriage.Applied to an honorable death in a good cause.

A snake falling among a lot of people will not die.A bad man often escapes through the difference of opinion among others as to his guilt, as a snake does through the Hindu’s superstitious dread of killing it.
If you throw ten stones, will not one hit?

proverb: పని అంటే నా వొళ్లు భారగిస్తుంది, భోజనమంటే నా వొళ్లు పొంగి వస్తుంది.
translation: If you talk of work, my body becomes heavy; if you talk of dinner, my body swells [with delight].The trade of thick-headed Michael: eating, drinking, and idling. (Dutch)

proverb: పని చెయ్యనివాడు యింటికి దొంగ, పన్ను యివ్వనివాడు దివాణానకు దొంగ.
translation: A man who does not work robs the house, a man who pays no taxes robs the government.

proverb: పనిలేని పాపరాజు యేమి చేస్తున్నాడంటే, కుందేటి కొమ్ముకు రేఖలు తీరుస్తున్నాడు అన్నాడట.
translation: When they asked what idle Pâpa Râzu was about, they were told that he was carving lines on a hare’s horn.
The barber without work shaved the cat’s head.A man pretending to be fully employed.
The jade who had nothing to do milked the cat.

proverb: పన్నెండు ఆమడ మధ్య బ్రాహ్మణుడు లేకపోతే యజ్ఞాము చేయిస్తాను అన్నాడట.
translation: If there is no Brahman within a hundred miles, I’ll conduct the sacriﬁce.An ignorant professor gets on well in the absence of the learned.

proverb: పప్పన్నమంటే పది ఆమడ అయినా పరుగెత్త వలెను.
translation: When we hear of D61 and rice we should run for it, even though it be hundred miles off.Said of a greedy Brahman.

proverb: పప్పుతో పది కబళాలు తింటే, పులుసు యెందుకు బుగ్గిలోకా.
translation: If one gets a few mouthfuls of rice with Dal, why should one want vegetables dressed with acid? To throw among the ashes?Said by an epicure.
Is there a marriage without Dal, or a vegetable dressed with out salt?Said to a great friend whose presence at an entertainment is indispensable.
A man without money is chaste.

proverb: పయిబడ్డ మాటా, మడి పడ్డ నీళ్లూ పోతవా.
translation: Will an accusation [be forgotten], will water which falls in a banked ﬁeld escape ?Throw much dirt, and some will stick.An ill wound may be cured, but not an ill name.
The property of others is as sweet as molasses.Being liberal at others’ expence.To out large shiver of another man's loaf.

proverb: పరుగెత్తుతూ పాలు తాగేకన్నా, నిలుచుండి నీళ్లు తాగడము మేలు.
translation: It’s better to stand and drink water, than to run and drink milk.A comfortable berth with a moderate salary, is better than a bad situation with a larger income.
The man that runs away, is inferior to the man who follows him.
Give honor, get honor.

proverb: పలకని వాండ్లతో పది వూళ్లవాండ్లూ గెలవ లేరు.
translation: The people of ten villages cannot overcome men who won’t open their mouths.
Water lies in the low ground.Truth will out at last.

proverb: పసుపు కొమ్ము యివ్వని కోమటి పసారమంతా కొల్ల యిచ్చినాడు.
translation: As impossible as that a Kômati who would not give away a bit of turmeric should allow his whole store to be plundered.

proverb: పసుపూ బొట్టూ పెట్టి పెండ్లికి పిలిస్తే వెళ్లక పెంకు పట్టుకొని పులుసుకు వెళ్లినట్టు.
translation: When turmeric was rubbed on her feet and a mark put on her forehead and she was invited to the marriage feast she would not go, but afterwards she went with a broken pot to beg for broth.Suffering by pride.

proverb: పక్షి మీద గురి పెట్టి మృగమును వేశినట్టు.
translation: He aimed at the bird and shot the deer.He ﬁred at the pigeon and he killed the crow.

proverb: పాండవుల వారి సంపాద్యము దుర్యోధనుల వారి పిండా కూళ్లకు సరి.
translation: The wealth gained by the Pândavas was only suffiicient for the funerals of the Duryôdhanas ( Kauravas ).Ill use of well gotten gains.
The war horn of the Pâkalapâti family.Said of a loquacious person.

proverb: పాగావంటి బంధువుడూ లేడు, అంగరకావంటి హరాంఖోరూ లేడు.
translation: There is no relation like your turband, there is no scamp equal to your jacket.A turband can be tied so as to hide all holes, which is not the case with the jacket; the turband is therefore likened to a trustworthy friend, and the jacket to an unfaithful fellow.
The goddess Gangânamma who presides over the burial ground, thinks of nothing but eating [the corpses].Entirely given to the selfish pursuit of one object.
If you get work, will food be wanting?
If you are industrious, you will become rich.
Sing again what you have sung, O Dâsari with dirty teeth.Said to an ignorant imposter.Give the piper a penny, and two pence to leave off.
A jackal is the watchman of a ruined village.( See Nos. 830, 1413.)Applied to a person who gets on well with his work when there is no one to question him.
In a ruined village, the leg of a bed is Pota Razu.Pôta Râzu is a rustic deity.

proverb: పాడు వూళ్లో పొగిడేవారు లేరు, నాకు నేనే పొగుడుకొంటానన్నాడట.
translation: He said “ in this ruined village there is no one to praise me, so I praise myself.”Your trumpeter’s dead, and so you trumpet yourself.
Like the old widows assembling to break the marriage cord of a new widow.A gang of rascals conspiring to ruin a. new comer, who they fear will be in their way.
A bit of straw in sherbet.

proverb: పాపటకాయ కొరకనెంత యాక పెట్టనెంత.A bramble is neither large enough to bite nor to place on a dish.Pâpața is the Pavetta Indica.A quantity quite inadequate to one’s wants.
పాపి సొమ్ము పరులపాలు, ద్రోహి సొమ్ము దొంగలపాలు.
translation: The riches of a sinner go to strangers ; the riches of a traitor go to thieves.
The snake only knows where it’s feet are.The Hindus believe that a snake has feet, though they are invisible.A man knows his own business best.
Feeding a snake with milk.Showing kindness to an ungrateful person.
The snake is your relation, and it’s hood your enemy.Being on friendly terms with members of a family though on bad terms with the head of the house.
Friendship with a snake, fencing with a sword.32
He won’t allow the snake to die, and he won’t allow the stick to be broken.Applied to a trimmer.

proverb: పాములలో మెలగ వచ్చునుగాని, స్వాములలో మెలగ కూడదు.
translation: You may walk among snakes, but keep clear of Vaishnava priests.
Like the ghi falling into the milk pudding.When a pot of ghi slipped from a woman's hand, it fortunately fell into a pudding.He is like a cat, he always falls on his feet.
You must look for a thing in the place where you lost it.Seek redress from the person who has injured you.
A man who hears the noise of a creeping ant.As wary as a blind horse.He is so wary that he sleeps like a bare with his eyes open.
Begging for milk and hiding his cup.Poverty and pride.

proverb: పాలబుట్టితే మాత్రము మేలు గుణము కలుగునా.Does every thing that comes from milk possess good qualities ?Good qualities are not always possessed by people of high birth.

translation: Like sugar dropped into milk.(5420 Non. IN, I382, “5, I888.)
Are we to look at milk or at the pot’?The pot is only valued for the milk in it.The jewel i: not to be valued for the cabinet.
Although you feed a snake with milk, it will not refrain from biting you.
He sucked the milk, and struck the breast.(Bee N“. 1011,1019, 1019.)
Can rotten food ever be made sweet ?Past reformation.

proverb: పిండి బొమ్మను చేశి పీటమీద కూర్చుండ బెట్టితే, ఆడబిడ్డతనాన అదిరదిరి పడ్డదట.
translation: When they made an image of an Ada Bidda of ﬂour and put it on a seat, it shook itself about [in threatening postures ] and gave itself great airsA‘qla Biajgia (literally "afemale child") is the familiar name for a husband’s sister, who manages her brother's household in the absence of her mother. ​
The cake will be as much as the flour.Payment in proportion to work. Results according to labor.
Can there be a marriage without ﬂour and a procession ?Indispensable accompaniments.
A fruit ripened after being- plucked.Said of a precocious youth.
Attacking a sparrow with a Brahmastra.(For Brahmasrtra see Nos. 615, 820, 880. )He takes a spear to kill a ﬂy.
How long does a gypsy’s marriage take? how long does an entertainment last ?Applied to the grandeur of arrogant people.
A little bird with a great cry.A little man sometimes casts a long shadow. (French)A little dog, a cow without horns, and a short man are generally proud. (Danish) ​
Like the cat settling the dispute between two birds.By eating them both up ( See Telugu Panchatantra, page 94. )
He went to Pithapuram, and brought a little potful of water
[He has] one Mantra for the thunderbolt and for the rice.Hindus believe that repetition of the ten names of Arjuna
బీభత్సో విజయః కృష్ణః సవ్యసాచీ ధనంజయః ||drives away thunderbolts. Au ignorant Brahman is said to have repeated this S‘ldka when receiving a dish of raw rice.Applied to impostors.

proverb: పితికే బర్రెను యిచ్చి పొడిచే దున్నను తెచ్చినట్టు.
translation: He bartered a milch she buffalo for a goring he buffalo.

proverb: పినతండ్రి పెండ్లాము పినతల్లి కాదు, మేనమామ పెండ్లాము మేనత్త కాదు.
translation: Your paternal uncle’s Wife is not your maternal aunt; your maternal uncle’s wife is not your paternal aunt.
A cowardly soldier is stopped by a sneeze.A sneeze is considered a bad omen among the Hindus.Ready for any excuse. ​
Attending a feast to which you are not invited, a fast you are not asked to observeFidders' dogs an' flees come to a feast unca’d. (Scotch)

proverb: పిలిస్తే బిగిస్తే సరిగాని వస్తే వాడి అబ్బ తరమా.
translation: If I invite him and he is too proud to come, it will be all right; but if he does come, how shall I manage it?Said by a poor man who was obliged to show hospitality which he could ill afford.
What does a young crow know about the blow of a pellet ?Inexperience.
Children and the legs of a stool do not feel the cold.To a child all weather is cold.

proverb: పిల్లగలవాడు పిల్లకు యేడిస్తే, కాటివాడు కాసుకు యేడ్చినాడు.
translation: When the father cried for his child, the sexton cried for his money.Crows bewail the dead sheep and then eat them.
Pinching the child while swinging the cradle.Done by a lazy fellow who wanted to make it appear that the child did not like him. ​
Like a grandfather groping for the children in a house where there are none.

proverb: పిల్లి కండ్లు పోగోరును, కుక్క పిల్లలు కలగకోరును.
translation: The cat wishes to see your eyes ~out, and the dog wishes to see you have children.Both will then be well fed.

proverb: పిల్లి కండ్లు మూసుకొని పాలు తాగుతున్ను యెవరూ యెరుగరని యెంచుకొన్నట్టు.
translation: Like a cat shutting her eyes, and fancying that no one could see her drinking the milk.A man fancying that he is unseen when committing some crime.The forest has ears the field has eyes. (German.)
Sport to the cat, death to the rat.What is sport to the cat is death to the mouse. (German)What is play to the strong is death to the weak. (Dutch)

proverb: పిల్లికి రొయ్యల మొలతాడు కట్టితే, అషుంటా బోయి నోట్లో వేసుకొన్నదట.
translation: When they tied a waist cord of prawns round the eat’s loins it went a little way and then ate up the whole.Want of appreciation of honors conferred. ​
Going to a marriage with a cat under your arm.The cat is a bad omen.Transgressing the rules of society.

proverb: పిల్లిని చంపిన పాపము నీది, బెల్లము తిన్న పాపము నాది.
translation: You take the sin of killing the cat, I’ll take the sin of eating your molasses.Said by s stingy Komati to a Brahman priest to whom he had presented the cheapest possible oﬂ‘ering that would expiate the sin he had committed by killing a cat, viz., a little image of a cat made of molasses, which he afterwards was greedy enough to take back again and swallow.
Kill a cat, kill a Brahman.Doing harm to a cat is considered as sinful as injuring a Brahman.
A cat [is as sacred as] a Brahman, and the stool ( used for prayers, &c.) is as sacred as his wife.
Enmity between the posteriors and the seat.

proverb: పీతాంబరము యెరువిచ్చినమ్మ పీట వెంబడి పట్టుకొని తిరగవలశినది.
translation: The lady who lent a woman a silk cloth, must walk after her with a stool.If you lend any thing good to a careless person you must take measures to prevent his spoiling it, as the silk cloth would have been spoiled had the woman sat upon the ground. ​1465. పీనుగకు చేశిన జాతర.Carrying a corpse in procession.A vain ceremony.
Like the Punganuru state.Punganuru is in the North Arcot District.No government.

proverb: పుచ్చకాయ దొంగ అంటే, భుజము తడిమి చూచుకొన్నాడట.
translation: When the robber of the water melon was spoken of, he felt his shoulders.Guilty conscience.

proverb: పుచ్చిన మిరియాలయినా, జొన్నలకు సరి తూగక పోవు.
translation: Though the pepper be worm eaten, it will weigh as much as the millet.A brave spirit under adverse circumstances.
To be born is but for death.
Tying beads round an unborn child.Don't reckon your eggs before they are laid. (Italian.)
He gives a name to an unborn child.To celebrate the triumph before the victory. (Latin) ​

proverb: పుట్ట మీద తేలు కుట్టినా, నాగుమయ్య మహిమేనా.
translation: If one is stung by a scorpion on an ant bill has it the venom of the snake-god ?Puțța is a white ant hill, where snakes are found.Applied to a person supposing himself to have been injured through the malice of an enemy.
Would your Maker feed you with grass ?The Creator provides suitable food for all his creatures ; the benefactors of others need not therefore be proud.
Has Pulligadu been my husband ever since I was born ?Applied to ungrateful servants or dependants.
The faggots of the funeral pile alone remove the [evil] qualities with which a man is born.What belongs to nature lasts to the grave. (Italian)

proverb: పుట్టిన పిల్లలు బువ్వకు యేడిస్తే, అవ్వ మొగుడికి యేడ్చినది.
translation: When the children cried for food, the grandmother cried for a husband.Wishing for something quite unsuitable to one’s age. ​

After living for so many years the man performed a sacriﬁce.Good luck comes at last.Fortune knock: once at least at every man's gale.Fortune is like the market, where if you hide your time, the price will fall.

proverb: పుట్టిన వానికి తమ్ముడు, పుట్టే వానికి అన్న.
translation: The younger brother of him who is born, the elder brother of him who is about to be born.Said jokingly of a dwarf.
The natural colour will remain, but will the artiﬁcial colour stand ?False pretensions.

proverb: పుట్టెడాముదము పట్టించుకొని పొర్లాడినా అంటేదే అంటును గాని అంటనిది అంటదు.
translation: Although a man besmear himself with a Puțți of castor oil and roll [among the grain] only what sticks, will stick to him, what does not, won’t.A Puțți is 500 lbs.A man can only obtain that which his destiny has prepared for him.Every man hath his lot.No butter will stick to his bread. ​
A bushelful of good works, and a dishful of wormsA great show of piety, but the prospect of future punishment for evil deeds.
When a man was given a Putti of corn he complained of short measure.Look not a gift horse in the mouth. (Latin.)

proverb: పుణ్యానికి పెట్టే అమ్మా నీ మొగుడితో సమముగా పెట్టుమన్నట్టు.
translation: Like a beggar who said “ O charitable lady, give me food like that which you give to your husband.”An absurd request.

proverb: పుత్రుడనై వేధింతునా, శత్రుడనై వేధింతునా, పేరులేని దయ్యమునై వేధింతునా, పెనిమిటినై వేధింతునా.
translation: Shall I torment you as a son? shall I torment you as an enemy ? shall I torment you as a nameless devil? or shall I torment you as a husband ?
Like squeezing an empty civet bag.You cannot get blood out of a stone. ​

If Pubba bursts forth (in rain) or if obscenity breaks out, they won’t stop.Pubba is the eleventh lunar mansion.If the ﬁrst of July it be rainy weather,’Twill rain more or less for four weeks together.

The child was demon-struck before it was ten days old.A Hindu infant is not taken out of the house during the mother's Purudu or ten days of ceremonial uncleanness after its birth. During this time the child is thought to be perfectly safe from the evil spirits, to attacks from which it is afterwards liable.
When a man was dying'from diarrhcea, some one played pranks as if possessed by Pota Razu.
A ladleful of 1331 on Pulagam.-Pulagam is rice mixed with pulse (called in Hindustani Khichri. ) — To add more dal is considered barbarous.
A ruffian even has his bugbear.
The tiger licked him and left him.A marvellous escape. ​
The jackal branded himself with spots like a tiger.Vulgar display.Apeing one’s superiors.
Like a gadﬁy on a tiger’s side.A safe refuge.
A tiger-cub is a tiger-cub, a kid is a kid.Cat after kind.
Taking hold of a tiger’s mustaches and swinging one’s self.A rash enterprise.
As soon as a ﬂower is full blown, it’s fragrance is perceived.A man’s worth is known when he is grown up.

proverb: పూజ కొద్దీ పురుషుడు, పుణ్యము కొద్దీ పుత్రుడు.
translation: A husband according to the worship [of the wife], a son according to the good works [of the father].

proverb: పూటకూళ్లకు వచ్చిన వారికి పుట్ల ధర యెందుకు.
translation: What have people buying cooked food, to do with the price of Puttis [of grain] ? ​
The cateress has no conscience.Said of the women who provide meals to travellers, and give them wretched food.
Worm eaten before blossoming.Premature. Precocious.

proverb: పూరా ముణిగిన వానికి చలియేమి గాలి యేమి.
translation: When a man plunges into the water does he care for cold or Wind ?Overwhelmed with grief.
Like lifting up a string of ﬂowers.A very easy business.
Like stringing beads.All in harmony.
Although a man has necklace and earrings is he to sit on [ another man’s] shoulder?Should a rich man be puffed up with pride?
One man said “Let us go to the marriage,” the other replied “Let us leave the country.”A spirit of contradiction. ​
Thinking of the good fare at the marriage when suffering from the effects of it afterwards.

proverb: పెండ్లి మర్నాడు పెండ్లి కొడుకు ముఖాన పెద్దమ్మ వేలాడుతున్నది.
translation: The day after the marriage the goddess of misfortune appeared in the face of the bridegroom.The bridegroom looked a poor creature when stripped of all his ﬁnery.
Are the people coming to the marriage waiting in the back yard ?i.e. “ Why such haste?"
The buffalo you intended to buy has eaten up the vegetables I intended to plant.Taunting a man who is always talking but never acts.

proverb: పెట్టనమ్మ పెట్టనే పెట్టదుగాని, పెట్టేముండకు యేమి వచ్చినది రోగము.
translation: A woman who does not give [alms ] will never give ; what evil has happened to the jade who does give ?Said by a beggar of one from whom he generally received alms. ​
If you feed, [people] will eat; but if you abuse none will bear it.
If you feast me [I shall consider it] a marriage and if not, a funeral.Fair and foul words.

She that gives will lose her life, she that advised will lose [the advantage of] her birth.The story runs thus: In the city of Matipuram, dwelt a king named Nayaśâli. He had three wives named Sumédâh, Suvarchalâ, and Nitimati. Sumédâh was blessed with a son; her rival wives, being barren, were less loved by the king. This caused them to be jealous of Surmédâh, and Nitimati counselled Suvarchalâ to poison the son, saying that afterwards they would share the king's favor equally with their more fortunate rival. Suvarchalâ consulted in the matter with her intimate friend Vichitravati,the minister's daughter: Vichitravati after some deliberation replied " She that gives will lose her life, she that advised will lose her birth" (i.e. will be banished from the king's presence.) Suvarchalâ proﬁting by her friend's advice and perceiving the fatal consequences which would follow the commission of the intended crime, desisted from her wicked purpose.
The treasure is known to the man who buried it.

proverb: పెట్టిన వారికి పుట్టినదే సాక్షి, పెట్టని వారికి పుట్టనిదే నిజము.
translation: The aims received witness to those who gave; those who gave not, truly do not receive.If the charitable become poor, they will not ask for help in vain ; but the uncharitable will obtain nothing. ​
She that gave neither food nor drink, beat [me] and said "be off"Said by a beggar.
Entertainments [bring] relations, ﬂattery [brings] fortune.While the pot boils friendship blooms. (German)
Will the meat and drink last for ever?Can one expect to live always on the bounty of others ?
The elder brother-in-law is like a woman.Said of a stupid clumsy person.

proverb: పెదిమకు మించిన పల్లు, ప్రమితకు మించిన వత్తి.
translation: A tooth projecting beyond the lip, a wick too big for the oil dish.Said of an insolent fellow.He is on the high ropes
If it passes your lips it will go over the Penna river.A secret revealed spreads quickly abroad.Three may keep counsel if two be away.Two eyes, two ears, only one mouth. (German) ​
The eldest son’s marriage, and the giant’s dinner.A giant had to be supplied with a man daily by the villagers in turn.A happy event and a great misfortune coming together.

proverb: పెద్ద తల లేకపోతే, గొర్రె తల తెచ్చుకొమ్మన్నారు.
translation: If you can’t get a head for the family get a sheep's head.Better than nothing.When you can’t get bread, oat cakes are not amiss. (Spanish)

proverb: పెద్ద పులి తరుముక వచ్చినా, హజారము ముందరికి పోరాదు.
translation: Though you be pursued by a tiger, you should not pass in front of the royal court.Applied to a tyrannical Government.

proverb: పెద్ద పులి యెదటనయినా పడ వచ్చునుగాని, నగరి వారి యెదట పడరాదు.
translation: You may face a royal tiger but you should not face a government official.
Disputing with the aged [is as wrong as] keeping company with ghosts.
The sorrow [you have caused ] to the pious will come upon you again like a great serpent."His mischief shall return upon his own head." Psalm vii. 16. ​

When we have crossed the Penna we can visit Perumal.The meaning is “ Let us get over the river ﬁrst and talk of visiting Perumal (Vishnu) afterwards. —The Penna (Pennar) falls into the sea near Nellore. It is rapid and deep during the monsoon, though its bed is nearly dry for 5 or 6 months of the year.
Like allotting glebe land in the Penna.A doubtful beneﬁt.
The tree in the back yard won’t do for medicine.That which is near is not valued.Far fetch'd and dear bought is good for ladies.A cow from afar gives plenty of milk. (French)
To grow is but to be broken.Ruin follows speedy exaltation.Fortune and glass soon break, alas! (Dutch.)
Your curds take away the respect due to age.Because they cause eructation.
Like mixing rice in the husk with curds.
Is he a blood relation, or a dinner relation ?A table friend is changeable. (French) ​
Must the effects of an evil eye be removed from a dunghill ?A cringing speech. " Should such a worm as I be honored ?"
Like an onion set in cow-dung.A bad man in like company.
Poor men’s superciliousness.
When a poor man is angry, he only bursts his own lips.Anger without power is folly. (German.)Anger makes a rich man hated, and a poor man scorned.

proverb: పేను కుక్కినా కుక్కుతాడు, చెవ్వి కరిచినా కరుస్తాడు.
translation: [ The monkey ] may either kill the louse, or bite off the ear.
When power was given to a louse, it bit all over the head.Abuse of authority.

proverb: పేరంటానికి వచ్చి పెండ్లి కొడుకు వరస యేమి అన్నట్టు.
translation: When she came to the marriage feast she began to enquire how the bridegroom was related to her.i. e. she was enamoured with him.Very unseemly behaviour. ​
Your name is mother Ganga, but when I want a drink I ﬁnd you have no water.Where you think Here are flitches of bacon there are not even hooks to hang them an. (Spanish)

proverb: పేరు పల్లకీ మీద, కాలు నేల మీద.
translation: His name is in the palankin, and his legs on the groundi. e. be once rode, but now walks.
A great name but the village a heap [of ruins ].

The name is my husband’s, the suffering is my father-in law’s.Said by Sita to the Rishis’ wives when they condoled with her on her husband's sufferings. She knew that Rama's misfortune was not to be compared with that of her father-in-law Dasaratha who died of grief, cursed by the old hermit whose son he had accidentally killed.The name with one, the reality with another.

proverb: పేరు పెన్న మోశింది, వొళ్లు నేల మోశింది.
translation: His name is borne by the Penna, and his body by the ground.Said of a wretched fellow with a great name.Fame is a magnifying glass. ​
Like the ant that crawls below a bird ﬂying above.It has no chance.
All that boils over goes to the ﬁre-place.Said of a silly man puffed up with conceit. whose vanity ruins him.
Fighting for the ﬁre-dogs.Pokkati Rallu are three clods of earth, or round stones used as a temporary ﬁre place.Quarrelling for something of no value.

proverb: పొట్టకరుకులు తిన్న వారికి వూచ బియ్యాలు లేవు.
translation: There is no young corn for those who eat the cars.
Having eaten a bushel of food, [he begs] for cakes as it’s Sunday.Sunday is a last day among some sects.Great greediness.
Short and strong, tall and weak.A short man needs no stool to give a great lubber a box on the car. ​

The service of Potti Pota Razu.A worrying fellow, of whom it is said that when his followers were behind him. he abused them for treating him as their (Pariah) guide; when they went before him, he accused them of acting as if they were his superiors; and when they travelled at each side of his palankin, he reprimanded them for daring to place themselves on an equality with himself.
A short man has a Putti of wisdom.
The joint-husband was neglected and died.The ass that is common property is always the worst saddled. (French)
Like cutting the udder, and drinking the milk.Ruining one’s self by ambition.

proverb: పొయ్యి అరిస్తే బంధువులు, కుక్కలు కూస్తే కరువు.
translation: If the ﬁre place cries out (i. e. if the wood sings) relations [ are coming], if the dogs bark a famine [may be expected ].Let us have florins and we shall ﬁnd cousins. (Italian)
The cat in the ﬁreplace is still sleeping.No preparations for cooking, little prospect therefore of dinner. ​
When asked to blow the ﬁre, he broke the pot which was on it.To do much harm when asked to do a little favor.

Look in my neighbour’s house, and see how openhanded I am !When I distribute his alms.It is easy to be generous out of another man's purse. (Danish)Broad thongs are cut from other men's leather. (Latin)
Quarrelling in one’s neighbour’s house is pleasant to listen to.

proverb: పొరుగు పచ్చగా వుంటే, పొయిలో నీళ్లు పోసుకొన్నట్టు.
translation: When his neighbour prospered he poured Water on his own hearth.An envious act.To lose one eye that you may deprive another of two. (Spanish)
Like trying to get rice by pounding empty husk.
Like breaking a pot full of areca nuts on a stone slab.A noisy brawl. ​
Like the Boa Constrictor.Applied to indolent persons; or to those who, from religious motives, separate themselves from the world and strive to quench all natural emotions.

To the men of Potépalli, Dal is the money present.Said of very poor Brahmans ready to rush to feasts for the sake only of the food, although they know that they will not receive a present in money.Pôtêpalli literally means " Going-village."

proverb: పోయిన కంటికి మందు వేస్తే, వున్న కన్నూ వూడ్చుకొని పోయినది.
translation: When he applied medicine to his bad eye, the sight of the other eye was lost.
That which is gone is the Scabbard, that which remains is the sword." He who is dead is nothing to his successor." ( Brown.)

proverb: పోరాని చుట్టము వచ్చినాడు, తమలపాకులు బొడ్డు వంచి కోయుమన్నట్టు.
translation: “A near relation has come," said he “stoop and pluck the betel leaves.”The owner of the garden unwillingly complied with his relation’s demand to be supplied with betel leaf gratis, and instructed his gardener with the ambiguous phrase “ stoop and pinch," which the relation understood to mean that the order was to be obeyed with alacrity, the object really being that the worst leaves, which are always nearest the ground, should be given. The word translated " near" signiﬁes literally " one who goes not."Applied to that which is done because politeness requires it, but unwillingly. ​

proverb: పోరాని చోట్లకు పోతే, రారాని నిందలు రాక మానవు.
translation: If you go where you ought not, you will not escape scandal.
[In] a quarrelsome neighbourhood there is no thriving, pots rubbing together will not last.
Strife is loss, friendship is gain.Where there are friends there is wealth. (Latin)
Poligâdu’s hand fell on the hole.Where the money was kept.A lucky chance.

proverb: పోలీ పోలీ నీ భోగము యెన్నాళ్లే అంటే, మా అత్త మాలవాడ నుంచి వచ్చే వరకూ అన్నట్టు.
translation: One said to a wife “ O Poli, Poli, how long will you enjoy yourself?” “ Till my mother-in-law comes back from the Pariah quarter” she replied.When the cat’s away the mice play.

proverb: పోల్నాటిలో పోకకు పుట్టెడు అమ్మితే, ఆ పోకా దొరకక పొర్లి పొర్లీ యేడ్చినదట.
translation: When a Puțți of grain was sold in Pôlnâdu for an areca nut, she rolled herself on the ground and cried, for she could not even get that.Very unfortunate. ​

proverb: ప్రతిష్ఠకు పెద్దినాయుడు చస్తే, యీడవ లేక యింటివాండ్లు చచ్చినారు.
translation: When Peddinayudu died for fame, the members of his house hold suﬂ'ered greatly in dragging away his body.Peddinayudu here means a Nayudu, (a title borne by certain castes) great both in social position and bodily frame.
The journey was a sham, but eating (the provisions prepared) was a reality.

proverb: ప్రసూతి వైరాగ్యము, పురాణ వైరాగ్యము, శ్మశాన వైరాగ్యము.
translation: Three causes of transient repentance, viz., the pains of travail, the effects of preaching, and the sight of death.The chamber of sickness is the chapel of devotion.Vows made in storms are forgotten in calms.
Is life sweeter than molasses ?Said of a peculiarly timid nervous man.
Though you lose your life, you must preserve your honor.
If given with love, a handful is sufficient.
బంగారపు కత్తి.A golden knife.Said of something of intrinsic value but of no utility.
A golden sparrow.The Telugu term for the grossbeak ( Loxia Philippine ).Pretty to look at but of no use.

proverb: బంగారు పొల్లు వున్నదిగాని మనిషి పొల్లు లేదు.
translation: There is dross in gold, but there is no defect in man.
Stealing a cart.Open robbery.
There is no greenness in Bandédu, nor newness in a daughter-in-law.Bandedu is the Dodonaes Burmanniana, a light wood that ignites like touch-wood.

proverb: బగబగమను వాని పంచనుండ వచ్చునుగాని, నాలిముచ్చు నట్టింట నుండరాదు.
translation: You may live in the verandah of a hot-tempered man’s house but you should not dwell in the middle of the house of a man who conceals his resentment. ​
Like tying bald heads and knees in a knot.Said of a very cunning person.
Debts for food and clothing do not remain long unpaid.
His arrogance is two yards long and his cheroot a cubit long.

proverb: బడాయి బావగారా అంటే, యేమే గుడ్డికంటి మరదలా అన్నాడట.
translation: When a woman said “O conceited brother-in-law,” he answered “Well, one eyed sister-in-law.”

proverb: బడే సాయబు జ్యోశ్యులూ తొలి యేకాదశి యెన్నడు.
translation: Like asking Bare Sahib the astronomer on what day the feast of Toli Yékadas’i would fall.Asking a man something which he cannot possibly answer.

proverb: బర్రె కొమ్ము అంటే, యిర్రి కొమ్ము అంటాడు.
translation: When one says it is a she-buifalo's horn, the other says it is a doe’s.Foolish argumentation.
When the buffalo dies, it comes out how much milk she gave.

proverb: బర్రె దూడ వద్దా పాత అప్పుల వాడి వద్దా వుండరాదు.
translation: You should not stay near a young buffalo, or an old creditor. ​
Forced bathing in Magha.The mornings of the month Mâgha ( January-February) are cold.A disagreeable duty.
Property is the strong man’s not the poor Brahman's.Might overcomes right.

proverb: బలిజె పుట్టుక పుట్టవలె, బతాయి బుడ్డి కొట్టవలె.
translation: You must be born a Balije to toss off Batavia arrackBalije is a caste.

proverb: బాపనవాని కొలువూ తెల్ల గుర్రపు కొలువూ కొలువ కూడదు.
translation: You should not serve a Brahman or a white horse.There is no end to your labor.

proverb: బారకాడివలె పడ్డావు, నీవెవరవురా మా యింటి దేవుడికి మొక్కను.
translation: You have fallen like a log, who are you to worship our household god ?Bârakâdi is a long yoke used for training hullocks.Said to a stranger who, under pretence of worshiping the god, came for food.

proverb: బావ మరిది బ్రతకకోరును, దాయాది చావ కోరును.
translation: Your wife's brother will wish you to live, your paternal relatives will wish you to die.When a Hindu dies his widow is supported by her relatives, and his portion of the family property goes to his relations on the father's side. ​

proverb: బావా నీ భార్య ముండమోశినదోయి అంటే, మొర్రో అని యేడ్చినాడట.
translation: When his brother-in-law said to him “O brother-in-law! your wife has become a widow,” he cried bitterly.Said of a blockhead.

proverb: బావిలోతు చూడ వచ్చునుగాని, మనస్సులోతు చూడ కూడదు.
translation: You can see the depth of a well, but not ‘the depth of the mind.No one can see into another further than his teeth. (Danish)
A man that breaks his word and a vagrant are alike.
Saturn prevented the [eating of] food got by begging.

proverb: బిచ్చపు వాణ్ని చూస్తే బీద వానికి కోపము.
translation: The sight of a beggar rouses a poor man’s anger.Said impertinently by a beggar when he gets nothing.

proverb: బిచ్చానకు పోయినా బిగువు తప్పలేదు, దుప్పటి పోయినా వల్లెవాటు తప్పలేదు.
translation: Although he goes to beg his pride is not diminished, although his cloth has gone the scarf is not removed from his neck.There’s nothing agrees worse, than a proud mind and a beggar’s purse. ​

proverb: బిడ్డ చక్కిలము వలె యెండిపోయినాడంటే, చక్కిలాలు యిమ్మని యేడ్చినాడట.
translation: When one said “the child has shrunk up like a Qakkilam” the child cried out “ give me Qakkilams.”Çakkilamu is "a biscuit made of twisted rings of paste without sugar" ( Brown.)
As the child grows, the pot grows.i.e. It eats more.A growing youth has a wolf in his belly.

proverb: బిడ్డ లేని ముద్దు, వాన లేని వరద.
translation: Fondling without a child, a ﬂood without rain.
A poor man is even lower than a beggar.
Like a pig in the mire.
A man like an eel.Sloth. Apathy.

proverb: బుర్రకు వక గుణము, జిహ్వకు వక రుచి.
translation: Every head has its idiosyncrasy, and every tongue its taste.All tastes are tastes. (Italian)There is no disputing of tastes, appetites, and fancies. ​
Like a dog lying in the warm ashes.Keeping quiet for a while, but soon returning to mischief.
Like pouring out an offering on ashes.A useless or unproﬁtable work.
Like sprinkling rose water on ashes.Wanton waste.

proverb: బెదిరించి బెదిరించి బెల్లపు కుండకు తూటు పొడిచినదట.
translation: All her threats ended in making a hole in the molasses pot.
Offering the Molasses Ganêśa a bit nipped off his own person.Said of a stingy man.

proverb: బొంకరా బొంకరా పోలిగా అంటే, టంగుటూరి మిరియాలు తాటి కాయలంతేశి అన్నాడట.
translation: “Go on with your lies Poliga,” said one, “The Țanguțûru pepper is as big as Palmyra fruit,” he replied.He may lie boldly who comes from afar. (French) ​
Can he who has sewn himselfa quilt, not cover himself with it ?
A beautiful parrot (Ramachiluka ) among coals.A good thing found in a bad place.
If you burn hair will it become charcoal ?Vain economy.
When he has chunam on his thumb, he is upset with conceit.Having "chunam on the thumb" means ability to afford to eat a little betel nut. Chunam is rubbed on the leaf with the thumb.
There is no love in harlots or whiteness in coals.
Like decorating a bald man with a jasmine garland.Boddu Malle is the Jasminum Sambac.Something being thrown away upon a person.
Like beating a shaven head with a cocoanutAs used to be done to a Sannyâsi ( religious mendicant) by his disciples when he was about to die.
Training a hornless bullock to gore.Leading the innocent to vice. ​
The dead infant is always a ﬁne child.Thought more of when dead than when living.For folks fare well, and fair children die.

proverb: బ్రతికి వుంటే, బలుసుకూర తిని వుండ వచ్చును.
translation: If I live, I can exist on Balusukura.Balurukura is the Canthium Parviflorum.Preferring hardship to oppression. “Submission to any necessity however grievous." (Elliot. )What can't be cured must be endured.

proverb: బ్రతికి వుండగా పాలు లేదు గాని, చచ్చిన వెనక గంగి గోవును దానము చేస్తానన్నట్టు.
translation: While he lived no milk was given to him, but he was promised that a sacred cow would be presented [on his account] to a Brahman, after his death.

proverb: బ్రతికే బిడ్డ అయితే, పాశిన వాసన యెందుకు వస్తున్నది.
translation: If the child is to live, why should there be such an offensive smell ?Said of a sure sign.
Brahma himself becomes enamoured.

proverb: బ్రహ్మ తలిస్తే ఆయుస్సుకు తక్కువా, మొగుడు తలిస్తే దెబ్బలకు తక్కువా.
translation: If Brahma wishes, will there be any lack in the length of your days? If the husband wishes, will there be any lack of blows ? ​
Will the writing written by Brahma fail ?
There is no turning to a Brahmastra.Brahmâstra is the fabulous weapon of Brahma.Said of anything which is irresistible.
A Brahman’s hand and an elephant’s trunk are never quiet.

proverb: బ్రాహ్మణుని మీది సంధ్యా కోమటి మీది అప్పూ నిలవదు.
translation: The prayers due by a Brahman will not remain [unsaid ], and the debt due by a Komati will not remain [unpaid].
The youngest among Brahmans, the eldest among ﬁshermen.Are made drudges.
You should not trust a black man among Brahmans, or a fair man among Pariahs.
Worship without faith is a mere waste of ﬂowers.Flowers are used in offerings.
[The thief] going to rob in Dharmapuri went crouching from Dharwada ( Dharwar ).
Like throwing a child into a well to ﬁnd out the depth.

proverb: నిశ్చితార్థము నాడే నీలగ వలశినది, నాగవల్లి దాకా వుండడము నా అదృష్టమే గదా.
translation: He ought to have died on the day of betrothal, it is my good fortune that he has lived until the fourth day of the marriageSaid ironically by a girl who had been married to an old man who died before the completion of the ceremonies.

If he has a mouth he will live.The son of the minister of a king of Ayodhya (Oudh) was caught stealing the king's ﬂowers.—-As he was being taken to the palace, his father called out " It is of no consequence, if he has a mouth he will live" The son took the hint, ate up the ﬂowers. denied the offence, and was believed by the king, who punished his accusers. (See Telugu Selections, 36th story. )

proverb: జీవరత్నము యిత్తడిని పొదిగితే, రత్నానికి యేమి లోపము.
translation: What does a ﬁne ruby suffer by being set in brass ?The disgrace of putting a talented man in a mean situation attaches to the employer not to the employé.
What is pleasant to the eye is good for the stomach.
